speaker: (Dhritarashtra)
sanskrit_sloka:
धर्मक्षेत्रे कुरुक्षेत्रे समवेता युयुत्सवः ।
मामकाः पाण्डवाश्चैव किमकुर्वत सञ्जय ॥
translation_en: In the field of dharma, at Kurukshetra, gathered together and eager to fight, what did my men and the Pāṇḍavas do, O Sanjaya?

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
दृष्ट्वा तु पाण्डवानीकं व्यूढं दुर्योधनस्तदा ।
आचार्यमुपसङ्गम्य राजा वचनमब्रवीत् ॥
translation_en: Then, seeing the Pāṇḍava army drawn up in battle formation, King Duryodhana approached his teacher and spoke these words.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
पश्यैतां पाण्डुपुत्राणामाचार्य महतीं चमूम् ।
व्यूढां द्रुपदपुत्रेण तव शिष्येण धीमता ॥
translation_en: O teacher, behold this great army of the sons of Pāṇḍu, arrayed by your wise disciple, the son of Drupada.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
अत्र शूरा महेष्वासा भीमार्जुनसमा युधि ।
युयुधानो विराटश्च द्रुपदश्च महारथः ॥
translation_en: Here are heroes, mighty archers, equal in battle to Bhīma and Arjuna: Yuyudhāna, Virāṭa, and Drupada, the great chariot-warrior.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
धृष्टकेतुश्चेकितानः काशिराजश्च वीर्यवान् ।
पुरुजित्कुन्तिभोजश्च शैब्यश्च नरपुङ्गवः ॥
translation_en: And Dhrishtaketu, Chekitana, the valiant king of Kāśi, Purujit, Kuntibhoja, and Śaibya, the foremost among men.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
युधामन्युश्च विक्रान्त उत्तमौजाश्च वीर्यवान् ।
सौभद्रो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्व एव महारथाः ॥
translation_en:
The valiant Yudhamanyu and the courageous Uttamauja,  
Saubhadra and the sons of Draupadi—all were great warriors.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
अस्माकं तु विशिष्टा ये तान्निबोध द्विजोत्तम ।
नायका मम सैन्यस्य संज्ञार्थं तान्ब्रवीमि ते ॥
translation_en: O best of the twice-born, know now those who are the foremost among us. For your information, I shall name to you the leaders of my army.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
भवान्भीष्मश्च कर्णश्च कृपश्च समितिञ्जयः ।
अश्वत्थामा विकर्णश्च सौमदत्तिस्तथैव च ॥
translation_en: You yourself, Bhīṣma, Karṇa, Kṛpa, and Samitijaya, as well as Aśvatthāmā, Vikarṇa, and Saumadatti.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
अन्ये च बहवः शूरा मदर्थे त्यक्तजीविताः ।
नानाशस्त्रप्रहरणाः सर्वे युद्धविशारदाः ॥
translation_en: And many other heroes, who have given up their lives for my sake, are armed with various weapons; all are skilled in warfare.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
अपर्याप्तं तदस्माकं बलं भीष्माभिरक्षितम् ।
पर्याप्तं त्विदमेतेषां बलं भीमाभिरक्षितम् ॥
translation_en:
Our force, protected by Bhīṣma, is inadequate;  
but the force of these men, protected by Bhīma, is adequate.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
अयनेषु च सर्वेषु यथाभागमवस्थिताः ।
भीष्ममेवाभिरक्षन्तु भवन्तः सर्व एव हि ॥
translation_en: Station yourselves at your respective positions in all the divisions of the army, and protect Bhīṣma alone, all of you.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
तस्य सञ्जनयन्हर्षं कुरुवृद्धः पितामहः ।
सिंहनादं विनद्योच्चैः शङ्खं दध्मौ प्रतापवान् ॥
translation_en: Then the elder of the Kurus, the mighty grandfather, arousing his joy, blew his conch with a loud lion’s roar.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
ततः शङ्खाश्च भेर्यश्च पणवानकगोमुखाः ।
सहसैवाभ्यहन्यन्त स शब्दस्तुमुलोऽभवत् ॥
translation_en: Then the conchshells, drums, panavas, and gomukhas were suddenly sounded, and the din became tumultuous.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
ततः श्वेतैर्हयैर्युक्ते महति स्यन्दने स्थितौ ।
माधवः पाण्डवश्चैव दिव्यौ शङ्खौ प्रदध्मतुः ॥
translation_en: Then, stationed in the great chariot yoked with white horses, Mādhava and Pāṇḍava blew their divine conches.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
पाञ्चजन्यं हृषीकेशो देवदत्तं धनञ्जयः ।
पौण्ड्रं दध्मौ महाशङ्खं भीमकर्मा वृकोदरः ॥
translation_en: Hṛṣīkeśa blew Pāñcajanya, Dhanañjaya blew Devadatta, and Vṛkodara, whose deeds are mighty, blew the great conch Pauṇḍra.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनन्तविजयं राजा कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।
नकुलः सहदेवश्च सुघोषमणिपुष्पकौ ॥
translation_en: King Yudhishthira, son of Kunti, blew the Anantavijaya; Nakula and Sahadeva blew Sughosha and Manipushpaka.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
काश्यश्च परमेष्वासः शिखण्डी च महारथः ।
धृष्टद्युम्नो विराटश्च सात्यकिश्चापराजितः ॥
translation_en: Kāśya, the supreme archer, and Śikhaṇḍin, the great chariot-warrior; Dṛṣṭadyumna, Virāṭa, and the unconquerable Sātyaki.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
द्रुपदो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्वशः पृथिवीपते ।
सौभद्रश्च महाबाहुः शङ्खान्दध्मुः पृथक्पृथक् ॥
translation_en: Drupada, the sons of Draupadi, and Saubhadra, the mighty-armed, each separately blew their conches, O lord of the earth.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
स घोषो धार्तराष्ट्राणां हृदयानि व्यदारयत् ।
नभश्च पृथिवीं चैव तुमुलोऽभ्यनुनादयन् ॥
translation_en: That tumultuous cry pierced the hearts of the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, resounding through the sky and the earth.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
अथ व्यवस्थितान्दृष्ट्वा धार्तराष्ट्रान् कपिध्वजः ।
प्रवृत्ते शस्त्रसम्पाते धनुरुद्यम्य पाण्डवः ॥
translation_en: Then, seeing the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra arrayed in position, the monkey-bannered Pāṇḍava lifted his bow as the clash of weapons was about to begin.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
हृषीकेशं तदा वाक्यमिदमाह महीपते ।
सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये रथं स्थापय मेऽच्युत ॥
translation_en:
Then Arjuna spoke these words to Hṛṣīkeśa, O king:  
“O Acyuta, place my chariot between the two armies.”

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यावदेतान्निरीक्षेऽहं योद्धुकामानवस्थितान् ।
कैर्मया सह योद्धव्यमस्मिन् रणसमुद्यमे ॥
translation_en:
While I observe these men, standing ready to fight,  
with whom am I to fight in this undertaking of battle?

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
योत्स्यमानानवेक्षेऽहं य एतेऽत्र समागताः ।
धार्तराष्ट्रस्य दुर्बुद्धेर्युद्धे प्रियचिकीर्षवः ॥
translation_en:
I see those who have gathered here, ready to fight,
seeking to please the ill-intentioned son of Dhritarashtra in battle.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
एवमुक्तो हृषीकेशो गुडाकेशेन भारत ।
सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये स्थापयित्वा रथोत्तमम् ॥
translation_en: Thus addressed by Guḍākeśa, Hṛṣīkeśa, O Bharata, placed the finest of chariots between the two armies.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
भीष्मद्रोणप्रमुखतः सर्वेषां च महीक्षिताम् ।
उवाच पार्थ पश्यैतान्समवेतान्कुरूनिति ॥
translation_en: In the presence of Bhishma and Drona, and of all the rulers of the earth, he said: “O Partha, behold these assembled Kurus.”

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
तत्रापश्यत्स्थितान्पार्थः पितॄनथ पितामहान् ।
आचार्यान्मातुलान्भ्रातॄन्पुत्रान्पौत्रान्सखींस्तथा ॥
translation_en: There Partha saw fathers, grandfathers, teachers, maternal uncles, brothers, sons, grandsons, and also friends.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
श्वशुरान्सुहृदश्चैव सेनयोरुभयोरपि ।
तान्समीक्ष्य स कौन्तेयः सर्वान्बन्धूनवस्थितान् ॥
translation_en: Seeing there fathers-in-law and friends in both armies, the son of Kunti saw all those relatives standing arrayed.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कृपया परयाविष्टो विषीदन्निदमब्रवीत् ।
दृष्ट्वेमं स्वजनं कृष्ण युयुत्सुं समुपस्थितम् ॥
translation_en:
Overwhelmed with compassion and distressed, he said this:

“O Kṛṣṇa, seeing my own kinsmen assembled here, eager to fight,”

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सीदन्ति मम गात्राणि मुखं च परिशुष्यति ।
वेपथुश्च शरीरे मे रोमहर्षश्च जायते ॥
translation_en:
My limbs are sinking, and my mouth is dry;  
my body trembles, and my hair stands on end.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
गाण्डीवं स्रंसते हस्तात्त्वक्चैव परिदह्यते ।
न च शक्नोम्यवस्थातुं भ्रमतीव च मे मनः ॥
translation_en:
Gandiva slips from my hand, and my skin is burning too.  
I cannot stand steady, and my mind seems to reel.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
निमित्तानि च पश्यामि विपरीतानि केशव ।
न च श्रेयोऽनुपश्यामि हत्वा स्वजनमाहवे ॥
translation_en: And I see contrary omens, O Keshava. Nor do I see any good in killing my own people in battle.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न काङ्क्षे विजयं कृष्ण न च राज्यं सुखानि च ।
किं नो राज्येन गोविन्द किं भोगैर्जीवितेन वा ॥
translation_en:
I do not desire victory, Krishna, nor kingdom, nor pleasures.  
What is a kingdom to us, Govinda, or pleasures, or even life?

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
येषामर्थे काङ्क्षितं नो राज्यं भोगाः सुखानि च ।
त इमेऽवस्थिता युद्धे प्राणांस्त्यक्त्वा धनानि च ॥
translation_en: For whose sake we desire kingdom, pleasures, and comforts—these stand here in battle, having given up their lives and wealth.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आचार्याः पितरः पुत्रास्तथैव च पितामहाः ।
मातुलाः श्वशुराः पौत्राः श्यालाः सम्बन्धिनस्तथा ॥
translation_en:
Teachers, fathers, sons, and also grandfathers,  
maternal uncles, fathers-in-law, grandsons, brothers-in-law, and likewise other relatives.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एतान्न हन्तुमिच्छामि घ्नतोऽपि मधुसूदन ।
अपि त्रैलोक्यराज्यस्य हेतोः किं नु महीकृते ॥
translation_en: O Madhusudana, I do not wish to kill these, even if they would kill me, even for the sake of dominion over the three worlds; what then of the earth?

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
निहत्य धार्तराष्ट्रान्नः का प्रीतिः स्याज्जनार्दन ।
पापमेवाश्रयेदस्मान्हत्वैतानाततायिनः ॥
translation_en: O Janardana, what joy would we have in killing the sons of Dhritarashtra? By killing these aggressors, only sin would come upon us.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तस्मान्नार्हा वयं हन्तुं धार्तराष्ट्रान्स्वबान्धवान् ।
स्वजनं हि कथं हत्वा सुखिनः स्याम माधव ॥
translation_en:
Therefore we are not right to kill the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, our own kinsmen.  
For how could we be happy after killing our own people, O Mādhava?

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यद्यप्येते न पश्यन्ति लोभोपहतचेतसः ।
कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं मित्रद्रोहे च पातकम् ॥
translation_en: Though these men, with minds overcome by greed, do not see the fault in destroying a family and the sin in betraying friends,

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कथं न ज्ञेयमस्माभिः पापादस्मान्निवर्तितुम् ।
कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं प्रपश्यद्भिर्जनार्दन ॥
translation_en: How should we not know to turn away from this sin, O Janardana, when we clearly see the fault caused by the destruction of the family?

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कुलक्षये प्रणश्यन्ति कुलधर्माः सनातनाः ।
धर्मे नष्टे कुलं कृत्स्नमधर्मोऽभिभवत्युत ॥
translation_en:
When the family is ruined, the eternal family dharmas perish;  
and when dharma is destroyed, adharma overwhelms the whole family.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अधर्माभिभवात्कृष्ण प्रदुष्यन्ति कुलस्त्रियः ।
स्त्रीषु दुष्टासु वार्ष्णेय जायते वर्णसङ्करः ॥
translation_en: O Kṛṣṇa, when irreligion prevails, the women of the family become corrupted; O Vārṣṇeya, when women are corrupted, there arises social confusion.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सङ्करो नरकायैव कुलघ्नानां कुलस्य च ।
पतन्ति पितरो ह्येषां लुप्तपिण्डोदकक्रियाः ॥
translation_en: Confusion of caste leads indeed to hell for the destroyers of the family and for the family itself. Their ancestors fall, deprived of the rites of offering of rice-balls and water.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
दोषैरेतैः कुलघ्नानां वर्णसङ्करकारकैः ।
उत्साद्यन्ते जातिधर्माः कुलधर्माश्च शाश्वताः ॥
translation_en: By these faults of the family-destroyers, which bring about the mixing of the varnas, the eternal dharmas of the caste and the family are destroyed.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
उत्सन्नकुलधर्माणां मनुष्याणां जनार्दन ।
नरके नियतं वासो भवतीत्यनुशुश्रुम ॥
translation_en: O Janardana, we have heard that for men whose family dharma has been destroyed, dwelling in hell is निश्चित.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अहो बत महत्पापं कर्तुं व्यवसिता वयम् ।
यद्राज्यसुखलोभेन हन्तुं स्वजनमुद्यताः ॥
translation_en: Alas, we are resolved to commit a great sin, namely to slay our own kinsmen out of greed for the pleasures of a kingdom.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदि मामप्रतीकारमशस्त्रं शस्त्रपाणयः ।
धार्तराष्ट्रा रणे हन्युस्तन्मे क्षेमतरं भवेत् ॥
translation_en: If the sons of Dhritarashtra, weapons in hand, should kill me in battle while I remain unresisting and unarmed, that would be better for me.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
एवमुक्त्वार्जुनः सङ्ख्ये रथोपस्थ उपाविशत् ।
विसृज्य सशरं चापं शोकसंविग्नमानसः ॥
translation_en:
Having said this in battle, Arjuna sat down on the chariot seat,  
casting aside his bow and arrows, his mind overwhelmed with grief.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
तं तथा कृपयाविष्टमश्रुपूर्णाकुलेक्षणम् ।
विषीदन्तमिदं वाक्यमुवाच मधुसूदनः ॥
translation_en: Seeing him thus overwhelmed by compassion, with his eyes full of tears and troubled, Madhusūdana spoke these words to the despondent one.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कुतस्त्वा कश्मलमिदं विषमे समुपस्थितम् ।
अनार्यजुष्टमस्वर्ग्यमकीर्तिकरमर्जुन ॥
translation_en: From where has this delusion come upon you in this critical moment, Arjuna? It is not fit for noble men, nor does it lead to heaven or fame.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
क्लैब्यं मा स्म गमः पार्थ नैतत्त्वय्युपपद्यते ।
क्षुद्रं हृदयदौर्बल्यं त्यक्त्वोत्तिष्ठ परन्तप ॥
translation_en:
Do not yield to unmanliness, Pārtha; it does not befit you.  
Casting off this petty weakness of heart, rise up, Parantapa.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कथं भीष्ममहं सङ्ख्ये द्रोणं च मधुसूदन ।
इषुभिः प्रतियोत्स्यामि पूजार्हावरिसूदन ॥
translation_en: How can I fight in battle against Bhishma and Drona with arrows, O Madhusudana, since they are worthy of worship, O destroyer of enemies?

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
गुरूनहत्वा हि महानुभावान्
श्रेयो भोक्तुं भैक्ष्यमपीह लोके ।
हत्वार्थकामांस्तु गुरूनिहैव
भुञ्जीय भोगान् रुधिरप्रदिग्धान् ॥
translation_en: For it is better in this world to live on alms than to kill these great-souled teachers. If I were to kill these teachers who seek wealth and pleasure here, I would enjoy pleasures stained with blood.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न चैतद्विद्मः कतरन्नो गरीयो
यद्वा जयेम यदि वा नो जयेयुः ।
यानेव हत्वा न जिजीविषाम-
स्तेऽवस्थिताः प्रमुखे धार्तराष्ट्राः ॥
translation_en:
We do not know which is better for us:
whether we should conquer them, or they should conquer us.
Those sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, standing before us,
would be the ones we would not wish to live after killing.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कार्पण्यदोषोपहतस्वभावः
पृच्छामि त्वां धर्मसम्मूढचेताः ।
यच्छ्रेयः स्यान्निश्चितं ब्रूहि तन्मे
शिष्यस्तेऽहं शाधि मां त्वां प्रपन्नम् ॥
translation_en: My nature is overcome by weakness, my mind confused about dharma. I ask You: tell me decisively what is best for me. I am Your disciple; I have taken refuge in You. Instruct me.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न हि प्रपश्यामि ममापनुद्याद्
यच्छोकमुच्छोषणमिन्द्रियाणाम् ।
अवाप्य भूमावसपत्नमृद्धं
राज्यं सुराणामपि चाधिपत्यम् ॥
translation_en: For I do not see what could dispel my grief, which is drying up my senses, even if I were to obtain a prosperous, unrivaled kingdom on earth and lordship over the gods.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
एवमुक्त्वा हृषीकेशं गुडाकेशः परन्तप ।
न योत्स्य इति गोविन्दमुक्त्वा तूष्णीं बभूव ह ॥
translation_en: Having thus spoken to Hṛṣīkeśa, Guḍākeśa, scorcher of foes, saying to Govinda, “I will not fight,” fell silent.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
तमुवाच हृषीकेशः प्रहसन्निव भारत ।
सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये विषीदन्तमिदं वचः ॥
translation_en:
Sanjaya said:

Hṛṣīkeśa spoke to him, as if smiling, in the midst of the two armies, while he was thus despondent: “O Bharata, these words.”

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अशोच्यानन्वशोचस्त्वं प्रज्ञावादांश्च भाषसे ।
गतासूनगतासूंश्च नानुशोचन्ति पण्डिताः ॥
translation_en: You grieve for those who should not be grieved for, and yet you speak words of wisdom. The wise do not grieve for the departed or for the living.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न त्वेवाहं जातु नासं न त्वं नेमे जनाधिपाः ।
न चैव न भविष्यामः सर्वे वयमतः परम् ॥
translation_en: Never indeed was I not, nor were you, nor were these lords of men; and never shall we all cease to be hereafter.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
देहिनोऽस्मिन्यथा देहे कौमारं यौवनं जरा ।
तथा देहान्तरप्राप्तिर्धीरस्तत्र न मुह्यति ॥
translation_en: Just as in this body the embodied one passes through childhood, youth, and old age, so too does he acquire another body; the steady person is not deluded by that.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मात्रास्पर्शास्तु कौन्तेय शीतोष्णसुखदुःखदाः ।
आगमापायिनोऽनित्यास्तांस्तितिक्षस्व भारत ॥
translation_en: O son of Kunti, the contacts of the senses with their objects bring cold and heat, pleasure and pain. These come and go; they are impermanent. Endure them, O Bharata.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यं हि न व्यथयन्त्येते पुरुषं पुरुषर्षभ ।
समदुःखसुखं धीरं सोऽमृतत्वाय कल्पते ॥
translation_en: For whom these do not distress, O best of men—who is even-minded in pain and pleasure, steadfast—he is fit for immortality.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नासतो विद्यते भावो नाभावो विद्यते सतः ।
उभयोरपि दृष्टोऽन्तस्त्वनयोस्तत्त्वदर्शिभिः ॥
translation_en:
The unreal has no being; the real has no nonbeing.  
The seers of truth have seen the end of both.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अविनाशि तु तद्विद्धि येन सर्वमिदं ततम् ।
विनाशमव्ययस्यास्य न कश्चित्कर्तुमर्हति ॥
translation_en: Know that to be indestructible by which all this is pervaded; no one is able to destroy this imperishable being.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अन्तवन्त इमे देहा नित्यस्योक्ताः शरीरिणः ।
अनाशिनोऽप्रमेयस्य तस्माद्युध्यस्व भारत ॥
translation_en: These embodied selves’ bodies are said to be finite, but the embodied self is eternal, indestructible, and immeasurable; therefore, fight, O Bharata.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
य एनं वेत्ति हन्तारं यश्चैनं मन्यते हतम् ।
उभौ तौ न विजानीतो नायं हन्ति न हन्यते ॥
translation_en: He who thinks of this one as a killer, and he who thinks of this one as killed—both of them do not understand; this one neither kills nor is killed.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न जायते म्रियते वा कदाचिन्
नायं भूत्वा भविता वा न भूयः ।
अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो
न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे ॥

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
वेदाविनाशिनं नित्यं य एनमजमव्ययम् ।
कथं स पुरुषः पार्थ कं घातयति हन्ति कम् ॥
translation_en: He who knows this self as indestructible, eternal, unborn, and imperishable—how can that person, O Pārtha, kill anyone, or cause anyone to be killed?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
वासांसि जीर्णानि यथा विहाय
नवानि गृह्णाति नरोऽपराणि ।
तथा शरीराणि विहाय जीर्णा-
न्यन्यानि संयाति नवानि देही ॥
translation_en: As a person casts off worn-out garments and takes up others that are new, so the embodied one casts off worn-out bodies and enters others that are new.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नैनं छिन्दन्ति शस्त्राणि नैनं दहति पावकः ।
न चैनं क्लेदयन्त्यापो न शोषयति मारुतः ॥
translation_en: Weapons do not cut It, fire does not burn It; water does not wet It, nor does wind dry It.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अच्छेद्योऽयमदाह्योऽयमक्लेद्योऽशोष्य एव च ।
नित्यः सर्वगतः स्थाणुरचलोऽयं सनातनः ॥
translation_en:
It cannot be cut, burned, wetted, or dried;  
it is eternal, all-pervading, स्थाणु, immovable, and ancient.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अव्यक्तोऽयमचिन्त्योऽयमविकार्योऽयमुच्यते ।
तस्मादेवं विदित्वैनं नानुशोचितुमर्हसि ॥
translation_en: This is said to be unmanifest, unthinkable, and unchanging. Therefore, knowing him as such, you should not grieve.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अथ चैनं नित्यजातं नित्यं वा मन्यसे मृतम् ।
तथापि त्वं महाबाहो नैवं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥
translation_en: And even if you think of him as constantly born, or constantly dead, still, O mighty-armed one, you should not grieve in this way.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
जातस्य हि ध्रुवो मृत्युर्ध्रुवं जन्म मृतस्य च ।
तस्मादपरिहार्येऽर्थे न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥
translation_en: For one who is born, death is certain; for one who has died, birth is certain. Therefore, over what is unavoidable, you should not grieve.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अव्यक्तादीनि भूतानि व्यक्तमध्यानि भारत ।
अव्यक्तनिधनान्येव तत्र का परिदेवना ॥
translation_en: O Bharata, beings are unmanifest in their beginning, manifest in their middle, and again unmanifest in their end. What cause is there for lamentation in this?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आश्चर्यवत्पश्यति कश्चिदेन-
माश्चर्यवद्वदति तथैव चान्यः ।
आश्चर्यवच्चैनमन्यः श‍ृणोति
श्रुत्वाप्येनं वेद न चैव कश्चित् ॥
translation_en: One sees this as a wonder; another speaks of it as a wonder; another hears of it as a wonder; and yet, even after hearing, no one truly knows it.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
देही नित्यमवध्योऽयं देहे सर्वस्य भारत ।
तस्मात्सर्वाणि भूतानि न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥
translation_en: O Bharata, the embodied one in the body of all is eternally indestructible. Therefore you should not grieve for any being.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
स्वधर्ममपि चावेक्ष्य न विकम्पितुमर्हसि ।
धर्म्याद्धि युद्धाच्छ्रेयोऽन्यत्क्षत्रियस्य न विद्यते ॥
translation_en:
Considering also your own dharma, you ought not to tremble.  
For a kshatriya, there is nothing better than a battle in accord with dharma.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदृच्छया चोपपन्नं स्वर्गद्वारमपावृतम् ।
सुखिनः क्षत्रियाः पार्थ लभन्ते युद्धमीदृशम् ॥
translation_en: By chance, they have come upon an open gate to heaven. Happy are the kshatriyas, O Partha, who obtain a battle like this.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अथ चेत्त्वमिमं धर्म्यं सङ्ग्रामं न करिष्यसि ।
ततः स्वधर्मं कीर्तिं च हित्वा पापमवाप्स्यसि ॥
translation_en: But if you will not fight this righteous battle, then, abandoning your own dharma and honor, you will incur sin.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अकीर्तिं चापि भूतानि कथयिष्यन्ति तेऽव्ययाम् ।
सम्भावितस्य चाकीर्तिर्मरणादतिरिच्यते ॥
translation_en: And people will speak of your imperishable disgrace. For one who has been honored, disgrace is worse than death.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
भयाद्रणादुपरतं मंस्यन्ते त्वां महारथाः ।
येषां च त्वं बहुमतो भूत्वा यास्यसि लाघवम् ॥
translation_en: The great warriors will think that you have withdrawn from battle out of fear, and, having been held in high esteem by them, you will come to be regarded lightly.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अवाच्यवादांश्च बहून्वदिष्यन्ति तवाहिताः ।
निन्दन्तस्तव सामर्थ्यं ततो दुःखतरं नु किम् ॥
translation_en: Your enemies will speak many unseemly words, disparaging your ability. What could be more painful than that?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
हतो वा प्राप्स्यसि स्वर्गं जित्वा वा भोक्ष्यसे महीम् ।
तस्मादुत्तिष्ठ कौन्तेय युद्धाय कृतनिश्चयः ॥
translation_en: If slain, you will attain heaven; if victorious, you will enjoy the earth. Therefore, O Kaunteya, rise up, resolved for battle.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सुखदुःखे समे कृत्वा लाभालाभौ जयाजयौ ।
ततो युद्धाय युज्यस्व नैवं पापमवाप्स्यसि ॥
translation_en: Treat pleasure and pain alike, gain and loss, victory and defeat. Then prepare for battle; thus you will not incur sin.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एषा तेऽभिहिता साङ्ख्ये बुद्धिर्योगे त्विमां श‍ृणु ।
बुद्ध्या युक्तो यया पार्थ कर्मबन्धं प्रहास्यसि ॥
translation_en: I have now declared to you the understanding of Sāṅkhya; now hear this understanding in yoga. Endowed with this understanding, O Pārtha, you will cast off the bondage of karma.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नेहाभिक्रमनाशोऽस्ति प्रत्यवायो न विद्यते ।
स्वल्पमप्यस्य धर्मस्य त्रायते महतो भयात् ॥
translation_en: In this endeavor, there is no loss, and there is no harm. Even a little of this dharma protects one from great fear.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिरेकेह कुरुनन्दन ।
बहुशाखा ह्यनन्ताश्च बुद्धयोऽव्यवसायिनाम् ॥
translation_en: O son of the Kurus, here in this matter there is only one resolute understanding; but the understandings of the indecisive are many-branched and endless.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यामिमां पुष्पितां वाचं प्रवदन्त्यविपश्चितः ।
वेदवादरताः पार्थ नान्यदस्तीति वादिनः ॥
translation_en: The unwise, delighting in the words of the Vedas, speak this flowery speech, O Partha, saying that there is nothing else.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कामात्मानः स्वर्गपरा जन्मकर्मफलप्रदाम् ।
क्रियाविशेषबहुलां भोगैश्वर्यगतिं प्रति ॥
translation_en: Those whose minds are full of desire, devoted to heaven, speak of the flowery words of ritual actions that grant birth and the fruits of action, with many special ceremonies aimed at pleasure and lordly power.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
भोगैश्वर्यप्रसक्तानां तयापहृतचेतसाम् ।
व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिः समाधौ न विधीयते ॥
translation_en: For those attached to enjoyment and lordship, whose minds are carried away by that, the resolute intellect is not established in samadhi.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
त्रैगुण्यविषया वेदा निस्त्रैगुण्यो भवार्जुन ।
निर्द्वन्द्वो नित्यसत्त्वस्थो निर्योगक्षेम आत्मवान् ॥
translation_en: The Vedas deal with the three gunas, O Arjuna. Be free from the three gunas, free from duality, ever established in sattva, and आत्मवान.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यावानर्थ उदपाने सर्वतः सम्प्लुतोदके ।
तावान्सर्वेषु वेदेषु ब्राह्मणस्य विजानतः ॥
translation_en: As much use is there in a well when water is flooded on all sides, so much use is there in all the Vedas for a brahmin who knows.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन ।
मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि ॥
translation_en:
Your right is to action alone, never to its fruits.  
Do not let the fruits of action be your motive, and do not be attached to inaction.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
योगस्थः कुरु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा धनञ्जय ।
सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योः समो भूत्वा समत्वं योग उच्यते ॥
translation_en:
Established in yoga, perform actions, abandoning attachment, O Dhananjaya.  
Being even-minded in success and failure is called yoga.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
दूरेण ह्यवरं कर्म बुद्धियोगाद्धनञ्जय ।
बुद्धौ शरणमन्विच्छ कृपणाः फलहेतवः ॥
translation_en: Action is far inferior, O Dhananjaya, to the yoga of buddhi. Seek refuge in buddhi; those who desire the fruits are miserly.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
बुद्धियुक्तो जहातीह उभे सुकृतदुष्कृते ।
तस्माद्योगाय युज्यस्व योगः कर्मसु कौशलम् ॥
translation_en: One who is endowed with intelligence abandons both good and evil deeds in this world. Therefore, devote yourself to yoga; yoga is skill in action.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कर्मजं बुद्धियुक्ता हि फलं त्यक्त्वा मनीषिणः ।
जन्मबन्धविनिर्मुक्ताः पदं गच्छन्त्यनामयम् ॥
translation_en: The wise, united with buddhi, renounce the fruit born of action; freed from the bondage of birth, they go to the flawless state.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदा ते मोहकलिलं बुद्धिर्व्यतितरिष्यति ।
तदा गन्तासि निर्वेदं श्रोतव्यस्य श्रुतस्य च ॥
translation_en: When your बुद्धि has crossed beyond the thicket of delusion, then you will become dispassionate toward what is to be heard and what has been heard.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
श्रुतिविप्रतिपन्ना ते यदा स्थास्यति निश्चला ।
समाधावचला बुद्धिस्तदा योगमवाप्स्यसि ॥
translation_en: When your understanding, confused by the Vedic teachings, stands firm and unshaken in samadhi, then you will attain yoga.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
स्थितप्रज्ञस्य का भाषा समाधिस्थस्य केशव ।
स्थितधीः किं प्रभाषेत किमासीत व्रजेत किम् ॥
translation_en: What is the mark of one whose wisdom is steady, O Keśava, who is in samadhi? How does one of steady mind speak, sit, or walk?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रजहाति यदा कामान्सर्वान्पार्थ मनोगतान् ।
आत्मन्येवात्मना तुष्टः स्थितप्रज्ञस्तदोच्यते ॥
translation_en: When a person gives up all desires that arise in the mind, O Pārtha, and is content in the Self alone through the Self, that person is called one स्थिितप्रज्ञ.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
दुःखेष्वनुद्विग्नमनाः सुखेषु विगतस्पृहः ।
वीतरागभयक्रोधः स्थितधीर्मुनिरुच्यते ॥
translation_en: One whose mind is not disturbed amid sorrows, who is free from longing amid pleasures, and who is free from attachment, fear, and anger is called a sage of steady wisdom.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यः सर्वत्रानभिस्नेहस्तत्तत्प्राप्य शुभाशुभम् ।
नाभिनन्दति न द्वेष्टि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
translation_en: One who is unattached everywhere, and who, on attaining this or that—whether good or evil—does not rejoice or hate, his understanding is steady.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदा संहरते चायं कूर्मोऽङ्गानीव सर्वशः ।
इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
translation_en: When, like a tortoise withdrawing its limbs on every side, one completely draws in the senses from their objects, that person’s wisdom is firmly established.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
विषया विनिवर्तन्ते निराहारस्य देहिनः ।
रसवर्जं रसोऽप्यस्य परं दृष्ट्वा निवर्तते ॥
translation_en: The sense objects turn away from the embodied one who abstains; the taste for them remains, but even that taste turns away upon seeing the Supreme.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यततो ह्यपि कौन्तेय पुरुषस्य विपश्चितः ।
इन्द्रियाणि प्रमाथीनि हरन्ति प्रसभं मनः ॥
translation_en: For even as he strives, O Kaunteya, the turbulent senses forcibly carry away the mind of a discerning person.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तानि सर्वाणि संयम्य युक्त आसीत मत्परः ।
वशे हि यस्येन्द्रियाणि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
translation_en: Controlling all of them, let him sit steadfast, intent on Me. For one whose senses are under control, his wisdom is firmly established.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ध्यायतो विषयान्पुंसः सङ्गस्तेषूपजायते ।
सङ्गात्सञ्जायते कामः कामात्क्रोधोऽभिजायते ॥
translation_en:
When a person dwells on sense objects, attachment to them arises.  
From attachment arises desire; from desire, anger is born.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
क्रोधाद्भवति सम्मोहः सम्मोहात्स्मृतिविभ्रमः ।
स्मृतिभ्रंशाद् बुद्धिनाशो बुद्धिनाशात्प्रणश्यति ॥
translation_en:
From anger arises delusion; from delusion, confusion of memory.  
From loss of memory comes loss of discernment; from loss of discernment, one is destroyed.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
रागद्वेषविमुक्तैस्तु विषयानिन्द्रियैश्चरन् ।
आत्मवश्यैर्विधेयात्मा प्रसादमधिगच्छति ॥
translation_en: But one who moves among sense-objects with the senses under control, free from attachment and aversion, and with the self disciplined, attains serenity.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रसादे सर्वदुःखानां हानिरस्योपजायते ।
प्रसन्नचेतसो ह्याशु बुद्धिः पर्यवतिष्ठते ॥
translation_en: With the arising of serenity, all sufferings are destroyed; for one whose mind is serene, the intellect quickly becomes steadfast.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नास्ति बुद्धिरयुक्तस्य न चायुक्तस्य भावना ।
न चाभावयतः शान्तिरशान्तस्य कुतः सुखम् ॥
translation_en:
For one who is not disciplined, there is no wisdom, and for one who is not disciplined, there is no steady contemplation.  
And for one who does not contemplate, there is no peace; how, then, can there be happiness for one without peace?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इन्द्रियाणां हि चरतां यन्मनोऽनुविधीयते ।
तदस्य हरति प्रज्ञां वायुर्नावमिवाम्भसि ॥
translation_en: For the mind, when it follows after the wandering senses, carries away his understanding, as the wind carries away a boat on the water.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तस्माद्यस्य महाबाहो निगृहीतानि सर्वशः ।
इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
translation_en: Therefore, O mighty-armed one, the senses of one whose senses are restrained from all sides by the sense-objects—his wisdom is established.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
या निशा सर्वभूतानां तस्यां जागर्ति संयमी ।
यस्यां जाग्रति भूतानि सा निशा पश्यतो मुनेः ॥
translation_en:
What is night for all beings is the waking time of the self-controlled one;  
what is waking time for beings is night for the sage who sees.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आपूर्यमाणमचलप्रतिष्ठं
समुद्रमापः प्रविशन्ति यद्वत् ।
तद्वत्कामा यं प्रविशन्ति सर्वे
स शान्तिमाप्नोति न कामकामी ॥
translation_en:
As waters enter the ocean, which remains unmoved and ever full,  
so all desires enter him.  
He attains peace, not the one who longs for desires.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
विहाय कामान्यः सर्वान्पुमांश्चरति निःस्पृहः ।
निर्ममो निरहङ्कारः स शान्तिमधिगच्छति ॥
translation_en: A person who has given up all desires moves about free from longing; free from possessiveness and free from egoism, that person attains peace.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एषा ब्राह्मी स्थितिः पार्थ नैनां प्राप्य विमुह्यति ।
स्थित्वास्यामन्तकालेऽपि ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृच्छति ॥
translation_en:
This is the Brahmic state, O Partha; having attained it, one is not deluded.  
Established in it even at the end of life, one attains Brahma-nirvana.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ज्यायसी चेत्कर्मणस्ते मता बुद्धिर्जनार्दन ।
तत्किं कर्मणि घोरे मां नियोजयसि केशव ॥
translation_en: Janardana, if you think that understanding is superior to action, then why, Keśava, do you urge me to this dreadful action?

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
व्यामिश्रेणेव वाक्येन बुद्धिं मोहयसीव मे ।
तदेकं वद निश्चित्य येन श्रेयोऽहमाप्नुयाम् ॥
translation_en: With your seemingly confused words, you appear to bewilder my understanding. Tell me that one thing, definitely, by which I may attain the highest good.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
लोकेऽस्मिन् द्विविधा निष्ठा पुरा प्रोक्ता मयानघ ।
ज्ञानयोगेन साङ्ख्यानां कर्मयोगेन योगिनाम् ॥
translation_en: In this world, O sinless one, I previously declared a twofold path: the yoga of knowledge for the Sāṅkhyas, and the yoga of action for the yogins.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न कर्मणामनारम्भान्नैष्कर्म्यं पुरुषोऽश्नुते ।
न च संन्यसनादेव सिद्धिं समधिगच्छति ॥
translation_en: A person does not attain freedom from action by abstaining from action, nor does one reach perfection by mere renunciation.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न हि कश्चित्क्षणमपि जातु तिष्ठत्यकर्मकृत् ।
कार्यते ह्यवशः कर्म सर्वः प्रकृतिजैर्गुणैः ॥
translation_en: For no one ever remains even for a moment without doing action; indeed, everyone is made to act helplessly by the gunas born of prakriti.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कर्मेन्द्रियाणि संयम्य य आस्ते मनसा स्मरन् ।
इन्द्रियार्थान्विमूढात्मा मिथ्याचारः स उच्यते ॥
translation_en: One who restrains the organs of action yet sits dwelling in the mind on sense objects, with a deluded self, is called a hypocrite.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यस्त्विन्द्रियाणि मनसा नियम्यारभतेऽर्जुन ।
कर्मेन्द्रियैः कर्मयोगमसक्तः स विशिष्यते ॥
translation_en: But one who, controlling the senses with the mind, begins, O Arjuna, the yoga of action with the organs of action, unattached, excels.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नियतं कुरु कर्म त्वं कर्म ज्यायो ह्यकर्मणः ।
शरीरयात्रापि च ते न प्रसिद्ध्येदकर्मणः ॥
translation_en: Perform the prescribed action; action is indeed better than inaction. Even the maintenance of the body would not be accomplished by inaction.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यज्ञार्थात्कर्मणोऽन्यत्र लोकोऽयं कर्मबन्धनः ।
तदर्थं कर्म कौन्तेय मुक्तसङ्गः समाचर ॥
translation_en: This world is bound by action done for any purpose other than yajna. Therefore, O Kaunteya, perform action for that purpose, free from attachment.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सहयज्ञाः प्रजाः सृष्ट्वा पुरोवाच प्रजापतिः ।
अनेन प्रसविष्यध्वमेष वोऽस्त्विष्टकामधुक् ॥
translation_en:
Having created beings together with yajna, Prajapati said of old:  
“By this you shall multiply; let this be the cow that yields your desired objects.”

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
देवान्भावयतानेन ते देवा भावयन्तु वः ।
परस्परं भावयन्तः श्रेयः परमवाप्स्यथ ॥
translation_en: By this, nourish the devas, and let those devas nourish you; nourishing one another, you will attain the highest good.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इष्टान्भोगान्हि वो देवा दास्यन्ते यज्ञभाविताः ।
तैर्दत्तानप्रदायैभ्यो यो भुङ्क्ते स्तेन एव सः ॥
translation_en:
The devas, nourished by yajna, will indeed grant you the enjoyed pleasures you desire.  
But one who enjoys what has been given by them, without offering back to them, is surely a thief.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यज्ञशिष्टाशिनः सन्तो मुच्यन्ते सर्वकिल्बिषैः ।
भुञ्जते ते त्वघं पापा ये पचन्त्यात्मकारणात् ॥
translation_en: Those who eat the remnants of yajna are freed from all sins; but those sinful men who cook for their own sake eat only sin.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अन्नाद्भवन्ति भूतानि पर्जन्यादन्नसम्भवः ।
यज्ञाद्भवति पर्जन्यो यज्ञः कर्मसमुद्भवः ॥
translation_en:
From food come beings; from rain comes food.  
From sacrifice comes rain; sacrifice is born of action.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कर्म ब्रह्मोद्भवं विद्धि ब्रह्माक्षरसमुद्भवम् ।
तस्मात्सर्वगतं ब्रह्म नित्यं यज्ञे प्रतिष्ठितम् ॥
translation_en: Know that karma arises from Brahman, and Brahman arises from the imperishable. Therefore the all-pervading Brahman is eternally established in yajna.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एवं प्रवर्तितं चक्रं नानुवर्तयतीह यः ।
अघायुरिन्द्रियारामो मोघं पार्थ स जीवति ॥
translation_en: Thus, O Pārtha, one who does not follow this wheel set in motion in this world, delighting only in the senses, lives in vain.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यस्त्वात्मरतिरेव स्यादात्मतृप्तश्च मानवः ।
आत्मन्येव च सन्तुष्टस्तस्य कार्यं न विद्यते ॥
translation_en: But for the person who rejoices in the Self alone, and is content with the Self, and is satisfied in the Self alone, there is nothing to be done.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नैव तस्य कृतेनार्थो नाकृतेनेह कश्चन ।
न चास्य सर्वभूतेषु कश्चिदर्थव्यपाश्रयः ॥
translation_en: For him, there is no purpose in what is done or not done here; nor does he depend on anyone or anything among all beings.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तस्मादसक्तः सततं कार्यं कर्म समाचर ।
असक्तो ह्याचरन्कर्म परमाप्नोति पूरुषः ॥
translation_en: Therefore, remain unattached and always perform the action that must be done; for by acting without attachment, a person attains the highest.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कर्मणैव हि संसिद्धिमास्थिता जनकादयः ।
लोकसङ्ग्रहमेवापि सम्पश्यन्कर्तुमर्हसि ॥
translation_en: For only by action did Janaka and others attain perfection; and considering the holding-together of the world, you too should act.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यद्यदाचरति श्रेष्ठस्तत्तदेवेतरो जनः ।
स यत्प्रमाणं कुरुते लोकस्तदनुवर्तते ॥
translation_en:
Whatever a श्रेष्ठ person does, that is what other people do.  
Whatever standard he sets, the world follows that.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न मे पार्थास्ति कर्तव्यं त्रिषु लोकेषु किञ्चन ।
नानवाप्तमवाप्तव्यं वर्त एव च कर्मणि ॥
translation_en: O Pārtha, there is nothing in the three worlds that I must do, nor anything unattained that I must attain; yet I remain engaged in action.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदि ह्यहं न वर्तेयं जातु कर्मण्यतन्द्रितः ।
मम वर्त्मानुवर्तन्ते मनुष्याः पार्थ सर्वशः ॥
translation_en: For if I were ever to cease acting, O Partha, remaining unwearied in action, people would in every way follow my path.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
उत्सीदेयुरिमे लोका न कुर्यां कर्म चेदहम् ।
सङ्करस्य च कर्ता स्यामुपहन्यामिमाः प्रजाः ॥
translation_en: If I did not perform action, these worlds would perish; and I would be the cause of confusion and the destruction of these beings.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सक्ताः कर्मण्यविद्वांसो यथा कुर्वन्ति भारत ।
कुर्याद्विद्वांस्तथासक्तश्चिकीर्षुर्लोकसङ्ग्रहम् ॥
translation_en: As the ignorant act with attachment to action, O Bharata, so should the wise act, unattached, desiring to maintain the welfare of the world.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न बुद्धिभेदं जनयेदज्ञानां कर्मसङ्गिनाम् ।
जोषयेत्सर्वकर्माणि विद्वान्युक्तः समाचरन् ॥
translation_en: A wise person, acting with discipline, should not create confusion in the understanding of those who are ignorant and attached to action, but should encourage them to perform all actions properly.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रकृतेः क्रियमाणानि गुणैः कर्माणि सर्वशः ।
अहङ्कारविमूढात्मा कर्ताहमिति मन्यते ॥
translation_en:
All actions are carried out in every way by the gunas of prakriti.
One whose self is deluded by ego thinks, “I am the doer.”

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तत्त्ववित्तु महाबाहो गुणकर्मविभागयोः ।
गुणा गुणेषु वर्तन्त इति मत्वा न सज्जते ॥
translation_en: But one who knows the truth, O mighty-armed one, about the division of the gunas and actions does not become attached, thinking, “The gunas act among the gunas.”

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रकृतेर्गुणसम्मूढाः सज्जन्ते गुणकर्मसु ।
तानकृत्स्नविदो मन्दान्कृत्स्नविन्न विचालयेत् ॥
translation_en: Deluded by the gunas of prakriti, they become attached to actions born of the gunas. One who knows the whole should not unsettle those who do not know the whole, the dull-minded.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मयि सर्वाणि कर्माणि संन्यस्याध्यात्मचेतसा ।
निराशीर्निर्ममो भूत्वा युध्यस्व विगतज्वरः ॥
translation_en: With the mind fixed on the Self, renouncing all actions in Me, free from desire and free from possessiveness, fight, without fever.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ये मे मतमिदं नित्यमनुतिष्ठन्ति मानवाः ।
श्रद्धावन्तोऽनसूयन्तो मुच्यन्ते तेऽपि कर्मभिः ॥
translation_en: Those human beings who consistently follow this teaching of Mine, full of faith and free from censure, are also released from their karma.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ये त्वेतदभ्यसूयन्तो नानुतिष्ठन्ति मे मतम् ।
सर्वज्ञानविमूढांस्तान्विद्धि नष्टानचेतसः ॥
translation_en: But those who, finding fault with this, do not follow my teaching—know them to be deluded in all knowledge and of ruined understanding.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सदृशं चेष्टते स्वस्याः प्रकृतेर्ज्ञानवानपि ।
प्रकृतिं यान्ति भूतानि निग्रहः किं करिष्यति ॥
translation_en:
Even a man of knowledge acts according to his own nature;  
beings follow their nature—what will restraint accomplish?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इन्द्रियस्येन्द्रियस्यार्थे रागद्वेषौ व्यवस्थितौ ।
तयोर्न वशमागच्छेत्तौ ह्यस्य परिपन्थिनौ ॥
translation_en: For each sense, attraction and aversion are fixed upon its objects. One should not come under their sway, for they are one’s enemies.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
श्रेयान्स्वधर्मो विगुणः परधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात् ।
स्वधर्मे निधनं श्रेयः परधर्मो भयावहः ॥
translation_en:
Better is one’s own dharma, though imperfectly performed, than another’s dharma well performed.  
Better death in one’s own dharma; another’s dharma is fraught with fear.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अथ केन प्रयुक्तोऽयं पापं चरति पूरुषः ।
अनिच्छन्नपि वार्ष्णेय बलादिव नियोजितः ॥
translation_en: Then by what is this person impelled to commit sin, O Vārṣṇeya, as if driven by force, even though unwilling?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
काम एष क्रोध एष रजोगुणसमुद्भवः ।
महाशनो महापाप्मा विद्ध्येनमिह वैरिणम् ॥

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
धूमेनाव्रियते वह्निर्यथादर्शो मलेन च ।
यथोल्बेनावृतो गर्भस्तथा तेनेदमावृतम् ॥
translation_en: As fire is covered by smoke, and a mirror by dust, so is this enveloped by that.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आवृतं ज्ञानमेतेन ज्ञानिनो नित्यवैरिणा ।
कामरूपेण कौन्तेय दुष्पूरेणानलेन च ॥
translation_en: Knowledge is veiled by this eternal enemy of the wise, O Kaunteya, in the form of desire, which is insatiable like fire.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इन्द्रियाणि मनो बुद्धिरस्याधिष्ठानमुच्यते ।
एतैर्विमोहयत्येष ज्ञानमावृत्य देहिनम् ॥
translation_en:
The senses, the mind, and the intellect are said to be its seat.  
By these it deludes the embodied one, obscuring his knowledge.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तस्मात्त्वमिन्द्रियाण्यादौ नियम्य भरतर्षभ ।
पाप्मानं प्रजहि ह्येनं ज्ञानविज्ञाननाशनम् ॥
translation_en: Therefore, O best of the Bhāratas, first restrain your senses, and then cast away this sinful foe, the destroyer of knowledge and wisdom.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इन्द्रियाणि पराण्याहुरिन्द्रियेभ्यः परं मनः ।
मनसस्तु परा बुद्धिर्यो बुद्धेः परतस्तु सः ॥
translation_en:
The senses, they say, are superior; superior to the senses is the mind.  
Superior to the mind is the intellect; but superior to the intellect is That.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एवं बुद्धेः परं बुद्ध्वा संस्तभ्यात्मानमात्मना ।
जहि शत्रुं महाबाहो कामरूपं दुरासदम् ॥
translation_en: Thus, understanding the Self to be beyond the intellect, steady the self by the self. O mighty-armed one, slay this enemy, hard to overcome, who takes the form of desire.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इमं विवस्वते योगं प्रोक्तवानहमव्ययम् ।
विवस्वान्मनवे प्राह मनुरिक्ष्वाकवेऽब्रवीत् ॥
translation_en: I taught this imperishable yoga to Vivasvān; Vivasvān taught it to Manu, and Manu spoke it to Ikṣvāku.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एवं परम्पराप्राप्तमिमं राजर्षयो विदुः ।
स कालेनेह महता योगो नष्टः परन्तप ॥
translation_en: Thus, this yoga was received through the succession of royal sages; the rajarsis knew it in this way. But, O scorcher of foes, in the course of a great time, this yoga has been lost here.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
स एवायं मया तेऽद्य योगः प्रोक्तः पुरातनः ।
भक्तोऽसि मे सखा चेति रहस्यं ह्येतदुत्तमम् ॥
translation_en: This very same ancient yoga has today been declared by me to you, for you are my devotee and my friend; indeed, this is the supreme secret.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अपरं भवतो जन्म परं जन्म विवस्वतः ।
कथमेतद्विजानीयां त्वमादौ प्रोक्तवानिति ॥
translation_en: Your birth is later, and the birth of Vivasvān is earlier. How am I to understand this—that You spoke it in the beginning?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
बहूनि मे व्यतीतानि जन्मानि तव चार्जुन ।
तान्यहं वेद सर्वाणि न त्वं वेत्थ परन्तप ॥
translation_en:
Many births of mine and yours have passed, O Arjuna;  
I know them all, but you do not, O scorcher of enemies.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अजोऽपि सन्नव्ययात्मा भूतानामीश्वरोऽपि सन् ।
प्रकृतिं स्वामधिष्ठाय सम्भवाम्यात्ममायया ॥
translation_en: Though I am unborn and imperishable, and though I am the Lord of beings, yet, relying on My own prakriti, I manifest Myself by My own maya.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत ।
अभ्युत्थानमधर्मस्य तदात्मानं सृजाम्यहम् ॥
translation_en: Whenever there is a decline of dharma, O Bhārata, and a rise of adharma, then I manifest Myself.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
परित्राणाय साधूनां विनाशाय च दुष्कृताम् ।
धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय सम्भवामि युगे युगे ॥
translation_en: For the protection of the good and for the destruction of the wicked, and for the establishment of dharma, I manifest myself age after age.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
जन्म कर्म च मे दिव्यमेवं यो वेत्ति तत्त्वतः ।
त्यक्त्वा देहं पुनर्जन्म नैति मामेति सोऽर्जुन ॥
translation_en: He who truly knows My divine birth and action thus does not, upon leaving the body, take birth again; he comes to Me, Arjuna.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
वीतरागभयक्रोधा मन्मया मामुपाश्रिताः ।
बहवो ज्ञानतपसा पूता मद्भावमागताः ॥
translation_en: Freed from attachment, fear, and anger, absorbed in Me and taking refuge in Me, many have been purified by the austerity of knowledge and have attained My state.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ये यथा मां प्रपद्यन्ते तांस्तथैव भजाम्यहम् ।
मम वर्त्मानुवर्तन्ते मनुष्याः पार्थ सर्वशः ॥
translation_en: As people approach Me, so do I respond to them. Men, O Partha, follow My path in every way.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
काङ्क्षन्तः कर्मणां सिद्धिं यजन्त इह देवताः ।
क्षिप्रं हि मानुषे लोके सिद्धिर्भवति कर्मजा ॥
translation_en:
Desiring success in their actions, men here worship the devas.  
For in the human world, success born of action comes quickly.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
चातुर्वर्ण्यं मया सृष्टं गुणकर्मविभागशः ।
तस्य कर्तारमपि मां विद्ध्यकर्तारमव्ययम् ॥
translation_en: The four varnas were created by Me according to the distinctions of guna and action. Know Me to be the non-doer, though imperishable and the doer thereof.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न मां कर्माणि लिम्पन्ति न मे कर्मफले स्पृहा ।
इति मां योऽभिजानाति कर्मभिर्न स बध्यते ॥
translation_en: Actions do not taint Me, nor is there in Me any desire for the fruits of action. One who thus knows Me is not bound by actions.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एवं ज्ञात्वा कृतं कर्म पूर्वैरपि मुमुक्षुभिः ।
कुरु कर्मैव तस्मात्त्वं पूर्वैः पूर्वतरं कृतम् ॥
translation_en: Having known this, even the ancient seekers of liberation performed action. Therefore do action, as the ancients did of old.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
किं कर्म किमकर्मेति कवयोऽप्यत्र मोहिताः ।
तत्ते कर्म प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वा मोक्ष्यसेऽशुभात् ॥
translation_en: What is action? What is inaction? Even the wise are perplexed about this. I shall explain to you that action, knowing which you will be freed from misfortune.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कर्मणो ह्यपि बोद्धव्यं बोद्धव्यं च विकर्मणः ।
अकर्मणश्च बोद्धव्यं गहना कर्मणो गतिः ॥
translation_en: One should understand action, and one should also understand forbidden action; and one should understand inaction as well, for the course of karma is profound.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कर्मण्यकर्म यः पश्येदकर्मणि च कर्म यः ।
स बुद्धिमान्मनुष्येषु स युक्तः कृत्स्नकर्मकृत् ॥
translation_en: He who sees action in inaction, and inaction in action, is wise among men; he is disciplined and a doer of all action.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यस्य सर्वे समारम्भाः कामसङ्कल्पवर्जिताः ।
ज्ञानाग्निदग्धकर्माणं तमाहुः पण्डितं बुधाः ॥
translation_en: Those whose every undertaking is free from desire and intention, whose actions have been burnt by the fire of knowledge, the wise call them learned.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
त्यक्त्वा कर्मफलासङ्गं नित्यतृप्तो निराश्रयः ।
कर्मण्यभिप्रवृत्तोऽपि नैव किञ्चित्करोति सः ॥
translation_en: Having renounced attachment to the fruits of action, ever content and निर्निराश्रय, he, though fully engaged in action, does nothing at all.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
निराशीर्यतचित्तात्मा त्यक्तसर्वपरिग्रहः ।
शारीरं केवलं कर्म कुर्वन्नाप्नोति किल्बिषम् ॥
translation_en: Free from hope, with mind and self controlled, and having relinquished all possessions, he performs only bodily action and does not incur sin.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदृच्छालाभसन्तुष्टो द्वन्द्वातीतो विमत्सरः ।
समः सिद्धावसिद्धौ च कृत्वापि न निबध्यते ॥
translation_en: Content with gain that comes by chance, beyond the pairs of opposites, free from envy, equal in success and failure, he is not bound even though he acts.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
गतसङ्गस्य मुक्तस्य ज्ञानावस्थितचेतसः ।
यज्ञायाचरतः कर्म समग्रं प्रविलीयते ॥
translation_en: For one who is free from attachment, liberated, and whose mind is established in knowledge, all action undertaken as yajna dissolves completely.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ब्रह्मार्पणं ब्रह्म हविर्ब्रह्माग्नौ ब्रह्मणा हुतम् ।
ब्रह्मैव तेन गन्तव्यं ब्रह्मकर्मसमाधिना ॥
translation_en: The offering is Brahman, the oblation is Brahman, offered into the fire of Brahman by Brahman; he by whom Brahman-action is attained is to be reached only through Brahman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
दैवमेवापरे यज्ञं योगिनः पर्युपासते ।
ब्रह्माग्नावपरे यज्ञं यज्ञेनैवोपजुह्वति ॥
translation_en: Some yogis perform sacrifice to the devas; others offer sacrifice by means of sacrifice itself in the fire of Brahman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
श्रोत्रादीनीन्द्रियाण्यन्ये संयमाग्निषु जुह्वति ।
शब्दादीन्विषयानन्य इन्द्रियाग्निषु जुह्वति ॥
translation_en: Others offer the senses, beginning with hearing, into the fires of restraint; others offer sound and other sense-objects into the fires of the senses.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वाणीन्द्रियकर्माणि प्राणकर्माणि चापरे ।
आत्मसंयमयोगाग्नौ जुह्वति ज्ञानदीपिते ॥
translation_en: Others offer all the functions of the senses and the functions of the life-force into the fire of self-control and yoga, kindled by knowledge.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
द्रव्ययज्ञास्तपोयज्ञा योगयज्ञास्तथापरे ।
स्वाध्यायज्ञानयज्ञाश्च यतयः संशितव्रताः ॥
translation_en: There are others who perform sacrifices of wealth, sacrifices of austerity, sacrifices of yoga, and, likewise, sacrifices of self-study and knowledge; these are disciplined ascetics, committed to strict vows.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अपाने जुह्वति प्राणं प्राणेऽपानं तथापरे ।
प्राणापानगती रुद्ध्वा प्राणायामपरायणाः ॥
translation_en:
Others offer the prana into the apana, and others the apana into the prana;  
still others, having restrained the movements of prana and apana, are devoted to pranayama.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अपरे नियताहाराः प्राणान्प्राणेषु जुह्वति ।
सर्वेऽप्येते यज्ञविदो यज्ञक्षपितकल्मषाः ॥
translation_en: Others, with regulated diet, offer the vital airs into the vital airs. All these, indeed, know sacrifice and are cleansed of sin through sacrifice.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यज्ञशिष्टामृतभुजो यान्ति ब्रह्म सनातनम् ।
नायं लोकोऽस्त्ययज्ञस्य कुतोऽन्यः कुरुसत्तम ॥
translation_en: Those who partake of the nectar remaining from yajna go to the eternal Brahman. O best of the Kurus, for one who does not perform yajna, there is no world here, let alone another.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एवं बहुविधा यज्ञा वितता ब्रह्मणो मुखे ।
कर्मजान्विद्धि तान्सर्वानेवं ज्ञात्वा विमोक्ष्यसे ॥
translation_en: Thus many kinds of yajnas are spread out in the mouth of Brahman. Know them all to be born of action; knowing this, you will be liberated.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
श्रेयान्द्रव्यमयाद्यज्ञाज्ज्ञानयज्ञः परन्तप ।
सर्वं कर्माखिलं पार्थ ज्ञाने परिसमाप्यते ॥
translation_en: The sacrifice of knowledge is better than a sacrifice of material things, O scorcher of foes. All action in its entirety, O Pārtha, culminates in knowledge.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तद्विद्धि प्रणिपातेन परिप्रश्नेन सेवया ।
उपदेक्ष्यन्ति ते ज्ञानं ज्ञानिनस्तत्त्वदर्शिनः ॥
translation_en: Know that by prostration, by inquiry, and by service; the knowers of truth, who see reality, will instruct you in knowledge.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यज्ज्ञात्वा न पुनर्मोहमेवं यास्यसि पाण्डव ।
येन भूतान्यशेषेण द्रक्ष्यस्यात्मन्यथो मयि ॥
translation_en:
Having known this, O Pāṇḍava, you will not again fall into such delusion.  
By this, you will behold all beings, without exception, in the Self, and then in Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अपि चेदसि पापेभ्यः सर्वेभ्यः पापकृत्तमः ।
सर्वं ज्ञानप्लवेनैव वृजिनं सन्तरिष्यसि ॥
translation_en: Even if you are the greatest sinner among all sinners, you will cross over all wrongdoing by the boat of knowledge alone.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यथैधांसि समिद्धोऽग्निर्भस्मसात्कुरुतेऽर्जुन ।
ज्ञानाग्निः सर्वकर्माणि भस्मसात्कुरुते तथा ॥
translation_en: As a blazing fire reduces fuel to ashes, O Arjuna, so the fire of knowledge reduces all karma to ashes.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न हि ज्ञानेन सदृशं पवित्रमिह विद्यते ।
तत्स्वयं योगसंसिद्धः कालेनात्मनि विन्दति ॥
translation_en: Certainly no purifier here exists equal to knowledge; one who has attained perfection in yoga finds that within the self in time.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
श्रद्धावाँल्लभते ज्ञानं तत्परः संयतेन्द्रियः ।
ज्ञानं लब्ध्वा परां शान्तिमचिरेणाधिगच्छति ॥
translation_en: One who is full of faith, devoted to it, and has controlled the senses attains knowledge. Having attained knowledge, he quickly attains supreme peace.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अज्ञश्चाश्रद्दधानश्च संशयात्मा विनश्यति ।
नायं लोकोऽस्ति न परो न सुखं संशयात्मनः ॥
translation_en: The ignorant and the faithless, and the one whose self is full of doubt, perish. For the doubting self, there is neither this world nor the next, nor happiness.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
योगसंन्यस्तकर्माणं ज्ञानसञ्छिन्नसंशयम् ।
आत्मवन्तं न कर्माणि निबध्नन्ति धनञ्जय ॥
translation_en: Actions do not bind one who has renounced action through yoga, whose doubts have been cut away by knowledge, and who is self-possessed, O Dhananjaya.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तस्मादज्ञानसम्भूतं हृत्स्थं ज्ञानासिनात्मनः ।
छित्त्वैनं संशयं योगमातिष्ठोत्तिष्ठ भारत ॥
translation_en: Therefore, with the sword of knowledge, cut away this doubt in your own heart, born of ignorance. Arise, O Bharata, and take up yoga.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
संन्यासं कर्मणां कृष्ण पुनर्योगं च शंससि ।
यच्छ्रेय एतयोरेकं तन्मे ब्रूहि सुनिश्चितम् ॥
translation_en: O Kṛṣṇa, you praise renunciation of action and also yoga. Tell me with certainty which of the two is better.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
संन्यासः कर्मयोगश्च निःश्रेयसकरावुभौ ।
तयोस्तु कर्मसंन्यासात्कर्मयोगो विशिष्यते ॥
translation_en: Renunciation and karma-yoga both lead to the highest good, but of the two, karma-yoga is superior to renunciation of action.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ज्ञेयः स नित्यसंन्यासी यो न द्वेष्टि न काङ्क्षति ।
निर्द्वन्द्वो हि महाबाहो सुखं बन्धात्प्रमुच्यते ॥
translation_en: He should be known as a perpetual renunciant who neither hates nor desires; free from duality, O mighty-armed one, he is easily freed from bondage.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
साङ्ख्ययोगौ पृथग्बालाः प्रवदन्ति न पण्डिताः ।
एकमप्यास्थितः सम्यगुभयोर्विन्दते फलम् ॥
translation_en: Children speak of Sāṅkhya and Yoga as separate; the wise do not. One who is rightly established in either attains the फल of both.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यत्साङ्ख्यैः प्राप्यते स्थानं तद्योगैरपि गम्यते ।
एकं साङ्ख्यं च योगं च यः पश्यति स पश्यति ॥
translation_en:
What is attained by Sāṅkhya is also attained by Yoga.  
He who sees Sāṅkhya and Yoga as one, he sees.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
संन्यासस्तु महाबाहो दुःखमाप्तुमयोगतः ।
योगयुक्तो मुनिर्ब्रह्म नचिरेणाधिगच्छति ॥
translation_en: But renunciation, O mighty-armed one, is hard to attain without yoga; the sage who is devoted to yoga soon attains Brahman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
योगयुक्तो विशुद्धात्मा विजितात्मा जितेन्द्रियः ।
सर्वभूतात्मभूतात्मा कुर्वन्नपि न लिप्यते ॥
translation_en: United in yoga, with a purified self, self-controlled, and with the senses conquered, he, whose self has become the self of all beings, is not tainted even while acting.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नैव किञ्चित्करोमीति युक्तो मन्येत तत्त्ववित् ।
पश्यञ्श‍ृण्वन्स्पृशञ्जिघ्रन्नश्नन्गच्छन्स्वपञ्श्वसन् ॥
translation_en: The one who is disciplined and knows the truth should think, “I do nothing at all,” while seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating, walking, sleeping, and breathing.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रलपन्विसृजन्गृह्णन्नुन्मिषन्निमिषन्नपि ।
इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेषु वर्तन्त इति धारयन् ॥
translation_en: Speaking, releasing, grasping, opening and closing the eyes, he should hold that the senses are moving among the objects of the senses.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ब्रह्मण्याधाय कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा करोति यः ।
लिप्यते न स पापेन पद्मपत्रमिवाम्भसा ॥
translation_en: One who performs actions, having renounced attachment, and placing them in Brahman, is not tainted by sin, just as a lotus leaf is not tainted by water.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कायेन मनसा बुद्ध्या केवलैरिन्द्रियैरपि ।
योगिनः कर्म कुर्वन्ति सङ्गं त्यक्त्वात्मशुद्धये ॥
translation_en: With the body, mind, intellect, and even the senses alone, yogis perform action, having renounced attachment, for the purification of the self.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
युक्तः कर्मफलं त्यक्त्वा शान्तिमाप्नोति नैष्ठिकीम् ।
अयुक्तः कामकारेण फले सक्तो निबध्यते ॥
translation_en: One who is disciplined, having renounced the fruit of action, attains steady peace; one who is undisciplined, attached to the fruit through desire, is bound.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वकर्माणि मनसा संन्यस्यास्ते सुखं वशी ।
नवद्वारे पुरे देही नैव कुर्वन्न कारयन् ॥
translation_en: Having renounced all actions in the mind, the embodied one dwells happily, self-controlled, in the city of nine gates, neither acting nor causing action.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न कर्तृत्वं न कर्माणि लोकस्य सृजति प्रभुः ।
न कर्मफलसंयोगं स्वभावस्तु प्रवर्तते ॥
translation_en: The Lord does not create agency, nor actions, nor the connection of action with its fruits for the world; but nature itself proceeds.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नादत्ते कस्यचित्पापं न चैव सुकृतं विभुः ।
अज्ञानेनावृतं ज्ञानं तेन मुह्यन्ति जन्तवः ॥
translation_en: The all-pervading Lord does not take anyone’s sin or merit. Knowledge is veiled by ignorance; therefore beings are deluded.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ज्ञानेन तु तदज्ञानं येषां नाशितमात्मनः ।
तेषामादित्यवज्ज्ञानं प्रकाशयति तत्परम् ॥
translation_en: But in those whose ignorance has been destroyed by knowledge of the Self, that knowledge reveals the Supreme like the sun.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तद्बुद्धयस्तदात्मानस्तन्निष्ठास्तत्परायणाः ।
गच्छन्त्यपुनरावृत्तिं ज्ञाननिर्धूतकल्मषाः ॥
translation_en: Those whose understanding is directed toward That, whose self is grounded in That, whose steadiness is in That, and whose final aim is That, go to the state from which there is no return, their impurities having been washed away by knowledge

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
विद्याविनयसम्पन्ने ब्राह्मणे गवि हस्तिनि ।
शुनि चैव श्वपाके च पण्डिताः समदर्शिनः ॥
translation_en: The wise see with equal vision a learned and humble Brahmin, a cow, an elephant, a dog, and one who eats dogs

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इहैव तैर्जितः सर्गो येषां साम्ये स्थितं मनः ।
निर्दोषं हि समं ब्रह्म तस्माद् ब्रह्मणि ते स्थिताः ॥
translation_en: Here in this world itself, creation is conquered by those whose mind rests in equanimity. For Brahman is indeed flawless and even; therefore they are established in Brahman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न प्रहृष्येत्प्रियं प्राप्य नोद्विजेत्प्राप्य चाप्रियम् ।
स्थिरबुद्धिरसम्मूढो ब्रह्मविद् ब्रह्मणि स्थितः ॥
translation_en: One should not rejoice on obtaining what is pleasant, nor become agitated on obtaining what is unpleasant; with steady understanding, undeluded, a knower of Brahman, established in Brahman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
बाह्यस्पर्शेष्वसक्तात्मा विन्दत्यात्मनि यत्सुखम् ।
स ब्रह्मयोगयुक्तात्मा सुखमक्षयमश्नुते ॥
translation_en:
With the self unattached to external contacts, one finds the happiness that is in the self.  
That one, with the self engaged in Brahma-yoga, attains inexhaustible happiness.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ये हि संस्पर्शजा भोगा दुःखयोनय एव ते ।
आद्यन्तवन्तः कौन्तेय न तेषु रमते बुधः ॥
translation_en: For the pleasures born of contact are indeed sources of suffering; they have a beginning and an end, O Kaunteya, and the wise do not delight in them.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
शक्नोतीहैव यः सोढुं प्राक्शरीरविमोक्षणात् ।
कामक्रोधोद्भवं वेगं स युक्तः स सुखी नरः ॥
translation_en: He who is able, while still in this body, to endure the surge born of desire and anger, is a disciplined man; he is a happy man.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
योऽन्तःसुखोऽन्तरारामस्तथान्तर्ज्योतिरेव यः ।
स योगी ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं ब्रह्मभूतोऽधिगच्छति ॥
translation_en: One who is सुख within, रमण within, and whose light is within—having become Brahman, he attains Brahman-nirvana.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
लभन्ते ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृषयः क्षीणकल्मषाः ।
छिन्नद्वैधा यतात्मानः सर्वभूतहिते रताः ॥
translation_en: The sages, whose sins have been destroyed, whose doubts are cut away, who are self-controlled, and who delight in the welfare of all beings, attain Brahma-nirvana.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कामक्रोधवियुक्तानां यतीनां यतचेतसाम् ।
अभितो ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं वर्तते विदितात्मनाम् ॥
translation_en: For those ascetics who are free from lust and anger, who have controlled their minds and know the Self, Brahman-nirvana lies all around.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
स्पर्शान्कृत्वा बहिर्बाह्यांश्चक्षुश्चैवान्तरे भ्रुवोः ।
प्राणापानौ समौ कृत्वा नासाभ्यन्तरचारिणौ ॥
translation_en: Having placed external contacts outside, and the eyes between the eyebrows, and keeping the inhalation and exhalation balanced, moving within the nostrils,

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यतेन्द्रियमनोबुद्धिर्मुनिर्मोक्षपरायणः ।
विगतेच्छाभयक्रोधो यः सदा मुक्त एव सः ॥
translation_en: A sage who has controlled the senses, mind, and intellect, and is devoted to liberation, whose desire, fear, and anger have departed—he is ever मुक्त, indeed.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
भोक्तारं यज्ञतपसां सर्वलोकमहेश्वरम् ।
सुहृदं सर्वभूतानां ज्ञात्वा मां शान्तिमृच्छति ॥
translation_en: Knowing Me as the enjoyer of sacrifices and austerities, the great Lord of all the worlds, and the friend of all beings, one attains peace.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनाश्रितः कर्मफलं कार्यं कर्म करोति यः ।
स संन्यासी च योगी च न निरग्निर्न चाक्रियः ॥
translation_en: He who performs the action that should be done, without relying on the fruit of action, is both a renunciant and a yogi, not one without fire and not one without action.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यं संन्यासमिति प्राहुर्योगं तं विद्धि पाण्डव ।
न ह्यसंन्यस्तसङ्कल्पो योगी भवति कश्चन ॥
translation_en: Understand that what they call renunciation is yoga, O Pāṇḍava; for no one becomes a yogī without renouncing intention.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आरुरुक्षोर्मुनेर्योगं कर्म कारणमुच्यते ।
योगारूढस्य तस्यैव शमः कारणमुच्यते ॥
translation_en:
For a sage who seeks to ascend to yoga, action is said to be the means;
for the same one, when established in yoga, stillness is said to be the means.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदा हि नेन्द्रियार्थेषु न कर्मस्वनुषज्जते ।
सर्वसङ्कल्पसंन्यासी योगारूढस्तदोच्यते ॥
translation_en: For when one is not attached to sense-objects or to actions, and has renounced all intentions, then one is said to have ascended to yoga.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
उद्धरेदात्मनात्मानं नात्मानमवसादयेत् ।
आत्मैव ह्यात्मनो बन्धुरात्मैव रिपुरात्मनः ॥
translation_en: One should lift the self by the self; one should not bring the self down. For the self is indeed the self’s friend, and the self is also the self’s enemy.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
बन्धुरात्मात्मनस्तस्य येनात्मैवात्मना जितः ।
अनात्मनस्तु शत्रुत्वे वर्तेतात्मैव शत्रुवत् ॥
translation_en: For that person, the self is the friend of the self by whom the self has been conquered by the self; but for one whose self is not conquered, the self alone acts as an enemy, like an enemy.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
जितात्मनः प्रशान्तस्य परमात्मा समाहितः ।
शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु तथा मानापमानयोः ॥
translation_en: For one who has conquered the self and is tranquil, the Supreme Self is well settled, in cold and heat, and in pleasure and pain, as well as in honor and dishonor.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ज्ञानविज्ञानतृप्तात्मा कूटस्थो विजितेन्द्रियः ।
युक्त इत्युच्यते योगी समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चनः ॥
translation_en: The yogi whose self is satisfied with knowledge and realization, who is steadfast and has conquered the senses, and to whom a lump of earth, a stone, and gold are the same, is called integrated.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सुहृन्मित्रार्युदासीनमध्यस्थद्वेष्यबन्धुषु ।
साधुष्वपि च पापेषु समबुद्धिर्विशिष्यते ॥
translation_en: He is distinguished by equal-mindedness toward benefactors, friends, enemies, the indifferent, mediators, the hateful, relatives, the righteous, and the sinful.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
योगी युञ्जीत सततमात्मानं रहसि स्थितः ।
एकाकी यतचित्तात्मा निराशीरपरिग्रहः ॥
translation_en: The yogin should constantly discipline himself, dwelling in solitude, alone, with mind and self controlled, free from desire and free from possessions.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
शुचौ देशे प्रतिष्ठाप्य स्थिरमासनमात्मनः ।
नात्युच्छ्रितं नातिनीचं चैलाजिनकुशोत्तरम् ॥
translation_en: Having set up for oneself a firm seat in a clean place, neither too high nor too low, made of cloth, deerskin, and kusa grass.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तत्रैकाग्रं मनः कृत्वा यतचित्तेन्द्रियक्रियः ।
उपविश्यासने युञ्ज्याद्योगमात्मविशुद्धये ॥
translation_en: There, having made the mind one-pointed and having restrained the activities of mind and senses, one should sit on the seat and practice yoga for the purification of the self.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
समं कायशिरोग्रीवं धारयन्नचलं स्थिरः ।
सम्प्रेक्ष्य नासिकाग्रं स्वं दिशश्चानवलोकयन् ॥
translation_en:
Holding the body, head, and neck straight and still,  
fixed, steady, gazing at the tip of his own nose, not looking around.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रशान्तात्मा विगतभीर्ब्रह्मचारिव्रते स्थितः ।
मनः संयम्य मच्चित्तो युक्त आसीत मत्परः ॥
translation_en: With mind शांत and free from fear, established in the vow of celibacy, controlling the mind, with consciousness fixed on Me, let him sit, devoted to Me as the supreme.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
युञ्जन्नेवं सदात्मानं योगी नियतमानसः ।
शान्तिं निर्वाणपरमां मत्संस्थामधिगच्छति ॥
translation_en: Thus, constantly disciplining the self in this way, the yogī with a controlled mind attains peace, the supreme nirvāṇa, abiding in Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नात्यश्नतस्तु योगोऽस्ति न चैकान्तमनश्नतः ।
न चातिस्वप्नशीलस्य जाग्रतो नैव चार्जुन ॥
translation_en: Yoga is not for one who eats too much, nor for one who does not eat at all; nor for one who sleeps too much, nor for one who is always awake, Arjuna.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
युक्ताहारविहारस्य युक्तचेष्टस्य कर्मसु ।
युक्तस्वप्नावबोधस्य योगो भवति दुःखहा ॥
translation_en: For one whose eating and recreation are regulated, whose actions are regulated, and whose sleep and waking are regulated, yoga becomes the destroyer of sorrow.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदा विनियतं चित्तमात्मन्येवावतिष्ठते ।
निःस्पृहः सर्वकामेभ्यो युक्त इत्युच्यते तदा ॥
translation_en: When the disciplined mind remains fixed solely in the Self, then he is said to be free from craving and established in yoga.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यथा दीपो निवातस्थो नेङ्गते सोपमा स्मृता ।
योगिनो यतचित्तस्य युञ्जतो योगमात्मनः ॥
translation_en: As a lamp in a windless place does not flicker, so is the yogin of controlled mind, practicing yoga of the Self.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यत्रोपरमते चित्तं निरुद्धं योगसेवया ।
यत्र चैवात्मनात्मानं पश्यन्नात्मनि तुष्यति ॥
translation_en: When the mind, restrained by the practice of yoga, becomes still, and when, seeing the self by the self, one is content in the self.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सुखमात्यन्तिकं यत्तद् बुद्धिग्राह्यमतीन्द्रियम् ।
वेत्ति यत्र न चैवायं स्थितश्चलति तत्त्वतः ॥
translation_en: That supreme happiness, which is grasped by the intellect and beyond the senses, which one knows there, and from which one does not depart in truth.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यं लब्ध्वा चापरं लाभं मन्यते नाधिकं ततः ।
यस्मिन्स्थितो न दुःखेन गुरुणापि विचाल्यते ॥
translation_en: Having obtained that, he considers no other gain greater than it; established in which, he is not shaken even by the heaviest sorrow.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तं विद्याद् दुःखसंयोगवियोगं योगसंज्ञितम् ।
स निश्चयेन योक्तव्यो योगोऽनिर्विण्णचेतसा ॥
translation_en: Know that yoga to be the severance of union with suffering; that yoga should be practiced with resolute determination and a mind that does not lose heart.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सङ्कल्पप्रभवान्कामांस्त्यक्त्वा सर्वानशेषतः ।
मनसैवेन्द्रियग्रामं विनियम्य समन्ततः ॥
translation_en: Abandoning completely all desires born of resolve, and restraining the whole group of senses on every side by the mind alone.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
शनैः शनैरुपरमेद् बुद्ध्या धृतिगृहीतया ।
आत्मसंस्थं मनः कृत्वा न किञ्चिदपि चिन्तयेत् ॥
translation_en: Little by little, with the intellect held firm by steadiness, one should bring the mind to rest in the Self and think of nothing at all.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यतो यतो निश्चरति मनश्चञ्चलमस्थिरम् ।
ततस्ततो नियम्यैतदात्मन्येव वशं नयेत् ॥
translation_en: Whenever the restless, unsteady mind wanders away, one should restrain it from that and bring it back under the control of the Atman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रशान्तमनसं ह्येनं योगिनं सुखमुत्तमम् ।
उपैति शान्तरजसं ब्रह्मभूतमकल्मषम् ॥
translation_en: For the yogin whose mind is peaceful, whose rajas has been quieted, who is free from sin and become Brahman, supreme happiness comes to him.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
युञ्जन्नेवं सदात्मानं योगी विगतकल्मषः ।
सुखेन ब्रह्मसंस्पर्शमत्यन्तं सुखमश्नुते ॥
translation_en: Thus, constantly disciplining the self, the yogi, freed from impurity, easily attains supreme bliss through contact with Brahman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वभूतस्थमात्मानं सर्वभूतानि चात्मनि ।
ईक्षते योगयुक्तात्मा सर्वत्र समदर्शनः ॥
translation_en: The one whose self is disciplined by yoga sees the Self abiding in all beings, and all beings in the Self, and sees equally everywhere.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यो मां पश्यति सर्वत्र सर्वं च मयि पश्यति ।
तस्याहं न प्रणश्यामि स च मे न प्रणश्यति ॥
translation_en: One who sees Me everywhere and sees everything in Me, for him I am not lost, and he is not lost to Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वभूतस्थितं यो मां भजत्येकत्वमास्थितः ।
सर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि स योगी मयि वर्तते ॥
translation_en: The yogī who is established in unity worships Me, dwelling in all beings. In all conditions, he abides in Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आत्मौपम्येन सर्वत्र समं पश्यति योऽर्जुन ।
सुखं वा यदि वा दुःखं स योगी परमो मतः ॥
translation_en: Arjuna, the yogi who sees everything as equal everywhere, by comparing it with his own self—whether happiness or sorrow—is regarded as the highest.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
योऽयं योगस्त्वया प्रोक्तः साम्येन मधुसूदन ।
एतस्याहं न पश्यामि चञ्चलत्वात्स्थितिं स्थिराम् ॥
translation_en: O Madhusūdana, this yoga that you have spoken of as evenness, I do not see its enduring steadiness, because of the restlessness of the mind.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
चञ्चलं हि मनः कृष्ण प्रमाथि बलवद् दृढम् ।
तस्याहं निग्रहं मन्ये वायोरिव सुदुष्करम् ॥
translation_en: For the mind is restless, Kṛṣṇa, turbulent, powerful, and stubborn; I regard its restraint as difficult as restraining the wind.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
असंशयं महाबाहो मनो दुर्निग्रहं चलम् ।
अभ्यासेन तु कौन्तेय वैराग्येण च गृह्यते ॥
translation_en: Undoubtedly, O mighty-armed one, the mind is difficult to restrain and restless. But, O Kaunteya, it is brought under control by practice and by detachment.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
असंयतात्मना योगो दुष्प्राप इति मे मतिः ।
वश्यात्मना तु यतता शक्योऽवाप्तुमुपायतः ॥
translation_en: Yoga is difficult to attain for one whose self is not controlled; but for one who has self-control and strives, it can be attained by proper means, in my view.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अयतिः श्रद्धयोपेतो योगाच्चलितमानसः ।
अप्राप्य योगसंसिद्धिं कां गतिं कृष्ण गच्छति ॥
translation_en: Arjuna said: If one is not self-controlled but is endowed with faith, and whose mind has strayed from yoga, O Krishna, what destination does he attain, having failed to reach perfection in yoga?

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कच्चिन्नोभयविभ्रष्टश्छिन्नाभ्रमिव नश्यति ।
अप्रतिष्ठो महाबाहो विमूढो ब्रह्मणः पथि ॥
translation_en: Does such a one, fallen from both, not perish like a shattered cloud, deprived of support, O mighty-armed one, deluded on the path of Brahman?

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एतन्मे संशयं कृष्ण छेत्तुमर्हस्यशेषतः ।
त्वदन्यः संशयस्यास्य छेत्ता न ह्युपपद्यते ॥
translation_en: O Kṛṣṇa, you are indeed fit to dispel this doubt of mine completely. No one else besides you is capable of removing this doubt.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पार्थ नैवेह नामुत्र विनाशस्तस्य विद्यते ।
न हि कल्याणकृत्कश्चिद् दुर्गतिं तात गच्छति ॥
translation_en: Partha, there is no destruction for him, neither here nor hereafter. For no one who does good, my dear, ever comes to a bad end.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्राप्य पुण्यकृतां लोकानुषित्वा शाश्वतीः समाः ।
शुचीनां श्रीमतां गेहे योगभ्रष्टोऽभिजायते ॥
translation_en: Having attained the worlds of the righteous and dwelling there for many long years, the one who has fallen from yoga is born in the house of the pure and the prosperous.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अथवा योगिनामेव कुले भवति धीमताम् ।
एतद्धि दुर्लभतरं लोके जन्म यदीदृशम् ॥
translation_en: Or else he is born in the family of wise yogis; for such a birth is very difficult to obtain in this world.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तत्र तं बुद्धिसंयोगं लभते पौर्वदेहिकम् ।
यतते च ततो भूयः संसिद्धौ कुरुनन्दन ॥
translation_en: There he regains that union of understanding from a former body, and then, O Kurunandana, he strives onward again toward perfection.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पूर्वाभ्यासेन तेनैव ह्रियते ह्यवशोऽपि सः ।
जिज्ञासुरपि योगस्य शब्दब्रह्मातिवर्तते ॥
translation_en: By that very prior practice, he is drawn on, even against his will; even the seeker of yoga goes beyond the Brahman of sound.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रयत्नाद्यतमानस्तु योगी संशुद्धकिल्बिषः ।
अनेकजन्मसंसिद्धस्ततो याति परां गतिम् ॥
translation_en: But the yogi, striving diligently and purified of all blemishes, perfected through many births, then attains the supreme goal.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तपस्विभ्योऽधिको योगी ज्ञानिभ्योऽपि मतोऽधिकः ।
कर्मिभ्यश्चाधिको योगी तस्माद्योगी भवार्जुन ॥
translation_en: The yogi is greater than ascetics, and is thought to be greater than the jnanis as well. The yogi is greater than those devoted to action; therefore, Arjuna, become a yogi.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
