sanskrit_sloka:
योगिनामपि सर्वेषां मद्गतेनान्तरात्मना ।
श्रद्धावान्भजते यो मां स मे युक्ततमो मतः ॥
translation_en: Among all yogis too, the one who worships Me with faith, with his inner self devoted to Me, is regarded by Me as the most closely united.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मय्यासक्तमनाः पार्थ योगं युञ्जन्मदाश्रयः ।
असंशयं समग्रं मां यथा ज्ञास्यसि तच्छृणु ॥
translation_en: O Partha, with your mind attached to me, taking refuge in me, practice yoga; listen as I tell you how you will know me completely and without doubt.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ज्ञानं तेऽहं सविज्ञानमिदं वक्ष्याम्यशेषतः ।
यज्ज्ञात्वा नेह भूयोऽन्यज्ज्ञातव्यमवशिष्यते ॥
translation_en: I shall now declare to you this knowledge together with realization, in full; knowing it, nothing further remains here to be known.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मनुष्याणां सहस्रेषु कश्चिद्यतति सिद्धये ।
यततामपि सिद्धानां कश्चिन्मां वेत्ति तत्त्वतः ॥
translation_en: Among thousands of human beings, perhaps one strives for perfection; and even among those who have attained perfection and strive, perhaps one knows Me in truth.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
भूमिरापोऽनलो वायुः खं मनो बुद्धिरेव च ।
अहङ्कार इतीयं मे भिन्ना प्रकृतिरष्टधा ॥
translation_en: Earth, water, fire, air, ether, mind, intellect, and ego—thus is My prakriti divided eightfold.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अपरेयमितस्त्वन्यां प्रकृतिं विद्धि मे पराम् ।
जीवभूतां महाबाहो ययेदं धार्यते जगत् ॥
translation_en: This is my lower nature; know, O mighty-armed one, that there is another nature of mine, the higher one, the living principle, by which this whole world is upheld.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एतद्योनीनि भूतानि सर्वाणीत्युपधारय ।
अहं कृत्स्नस्य जगतः प्रभवः प्रलयस्तथा ॥
translation_en: Understand that all beings have these as their source. I am the origin and also the dissolution of the entire universe.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मत्तः परतरं नान्यत्किञ्चिदस्ति धनञ्जय ।
मयि सर्वमिदं प्रोतं सूत्रे मणिगणा इव ॥
translation_en: There is nothing else higher than Me, O Dhananjaya. All this is strung on Me, like rows of gems on a thread.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
रसोऽहमप्सु कौन्तेय प्रभास्मि शशिसूर्ययोः ।
प्रणवः सर्ववेदेषु शब्दः खे पौरुषं नृषु ॥
translation_en: I am the taste in water, O Kaunteya, the radiance in the moon and the sun; I am the om in all the Vedas, the sound in space, and the manliness in men.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पुण्यो गन्धः पृथिव्यां च तेजश्चास्मि विभावसौ ।
जीवनं सर्वभूतेषु तपश्चास्मि तपस्विषु ॥
translation_en:
I am the pure fragrance in the earth, and the radiance in fire.  
I am the life in all beings, and the austerity in ascetics.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
बीजं मां सर्वभूतानां विद्धि पार्थ सनातनम् ।
बुद्धिर्बुद्धिमतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम् ॥
translation_en: O Pārtha, know Me as the eternal seed of all beings. Among the intelligent, I am intelligence; among the radiant, I am radiance.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
बलं बलवतां चाहं कामरागविवर्जितम् ।
धर्माविरुद्धो भूतेषु कामोऽस्मि भरतर्षभ ॥
translation_en: I am the strength of the strong, free from desire and passion. O chief of the Bharatas, in beings I am desire that is not opposed to dharma.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ये चैव सात्त्विका भावा राजसास्तामसाश्च ये ।
मत्त एवेति तान्विद्धि न त्वहं तेषु ते मयि ॥
translation_en: Whatever states are sāttvika, rājasika, or tāmasika—know them all as arising from Me; but I am not in them, while they are in Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
त्रिभिर्गुणमयैर्भावैरेभिः सर्वमिदं जगत् ।
मोहितं नाभिजानाति मामेभ्यः परमव्ययम् ॥
translation_en: This whole world, deluded by these three states made of the gunas, does not know Me, who am beyond them and imperishable.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
दैवी ह्येषा गुणमयी मम माया दुरत्यया ।
मामेव ये प्रपद्यन्ते मायामेतां तरन्ति ते ॥
translation_en: This divine Maya of Mine, made of the gunas, is hard to overcome. But those who take refuge in Me alone cross beyond this Maya.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न मां दुष्कृतिनो मूढाः प्रपद्यन्ते नराधमाः ।
माययापहृतज्ञाना आसुरं भावमाश्रिताः ॥
translation_en: The wicked and deluded, the lowest among men, whose knowledge has been carried away by maya and who have taken refuge in demoniac nature, do not surrender to Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
चतुर्विधा भजन्ते मां जनाः सुकृतिनोऽर्जुन ।
आर्तो जिज्ञासुरर्थार्थी ज्ञानी च भरतर्षभ ॥
translation_en: Four kinds of people of good deeds worship Me, Arjuna: the distressed, the seeker of knowledge, the seeker of gain, and the wise, O best of the Bharatas.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तेषां ज्ञानी नित्ययुक्त एकभक्तिर्विशिष्यते ।
प्रियो हि ज्ञानिनोऽत्यर्थमहं स च मम प्रियः ॥
translation_en: Among them, the man of wisdom, ever steadfast and devoted to one alone, is श्रेष्ठ. For I am exceedingly dear to the wise one, and he is dear to Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
उदाराः सर्व एवैते ज्ञानी त्वात्मैव मे मतम् ।
आस्थितः स हि युक्तात्मा मामेवानुत्तमां गतिम् ॥
translation_en:
All of these are indeed noble; but I regard the wise one as My very self.  
For, steadfast in self, he is established in Me alone as the supreme goal.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
बहूनां जन्मनामन्ते ज्ञानवान्मां प्रपद्यते ।
वासुदेवः सर्वमिति स महात्मा सुदुर्लभः ॥
translation_en: At the end of many births, the wise one comes to Me. That great soul who knows, “Vāsudeva is all,” is very rare.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कामैस्तैस्तैर्हृतज्ञानाः प्रपद्यन्तेऽन्यदेवताः ।
तं तं नियममास्थाय प्रकृत्या नियताः स्वया ॥
translation_en: Those whose knowledge has been carried away by those various desires take refuge in other deities; following this and that rule, they are constrained by their own nature.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यो यो यां यां तनुं भक्तः श्रद्धयार्चितुमिच्छति ।
तस्य तस्याचलां श्रद्धां तामेव विदधाम्यहम् ॥
translation_en: Whatever form, whatever body, a devotee wishes to worship with faith, I make that very faith firm in him.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
स तया श्रद्धया युक्तस्तस्याराधनमीहते ।
लभते च ततः कामान्मयैव विहितान्हि तान् ॥
translation_en: Endowed with that faith, he seeks to worship that one; then he obtains those desires, as indeed they are ordained by Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अन्तवत्तु फलं तेषां तद्भवत्यल्पमेधसाम् ।
देवान्देवयजो यान्ति मद्भक्ता यान्ति मामपि ॥
translation_en: But the fruit obtained by those of limited understanding is perishable. Those who worship the devas go to the devas, while My devotees come to Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अव्यक्तं व्यक्तिमापन्नं मन्यन्ते मामबुद्धयः ।
परं भावमजानन्तो ममाव्ययमनुत्तमम् ॥
translation_en: The unwise think of Me, the unmanifest, as having assumed manifest form, not knowing My imperishable, supreme nature.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नाहं प्रकाशः सर्वस्य योगमायासमावृतः ।
मूढोऽयं नाभिजानाति लोको मामजमव्ययम् ॥
translation_en: I am not manifest to all, being veiled by my yoga-maya. This deluded world does not know me as unborn and imperishable.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
वेदाहं समतीतानि वर्तमानानि चार्जुन ।
भविष्याणि च भूतानि मां तु वेद न कश्चन ॥
translation_en: I know the beings of the past, the present, and the future, O Arjuna; but no one knows Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इच्छाद्वेषसमुत्थेन द्वन्द्वमोहेन भारत ।
सर्वभूतानि सम्मोहं सर्गे यान्ति परन्तप ॥
translation_en: O Bharata, all beings are deluded at birth by the delusion of duality, arising from desire and aversion, O scorcher of foes.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
येषां त्वन्तगतं पापं जनानां पुण्यकर्मणाम् ।
ते द्वन्द्वमोहनिर्मुक्ता भजन्ते मां दृढव्रताः ॥
translation_en: But those whose sins have come to an end, who have acted righteously, are freed from the delusion of duality; with firm resolve, they worship Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
जरामरणमोक्षाय मामाश्रित्य यतन्ति ये ।
ते ब्रह्म तद्विदुः कृत्स्नमध्यात्मं कर्म चाखिलम् ॥
translation_en: Those who take refuge in Me and strive for release from old age and death know Brahman, the whole of adhyatma, and all karma.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
साधिभूताधिदैवं मां साधियज्ञं च ये विदुः ।
प्रयाणकालेऽपि च मां ते विदुर्युक्तचेतसः ॥
translation_en: Those who know Me together with the adhibhūta, the adhidaiva, and the adhiyajña—those steadfast in mind know Me even at the time of death.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
किं तद् ब्रह्म किमध्यात्मं किं कर्म पुरुषोत्तम ।
अधिभूतं च किं प्रोक्तमधिदैवं किमुच्यते ॥
translation_en: Arjuna said: What is that Brahman? What is adhyatma? What is karma, O Purushottama? What is called adhibhuta, and what is meant by adhidaiva?

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अधियज्ञः कथं कोऽत्र देहेऽस्मिन्मधुसूदन ।
प्रयाणकाले च कथं ज्ञेयोऽसि नियतात्मभिः ॥
translation_en: How, and who, is the adhiyajna in this body, O Madhusūdana? And how are You to be known at the time of death by the self-controlled?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अक्षरं ब्रह्म परमं स्वभावोऽध्यात्ममुच्यते ।
भूतभावोद्भवकरो विसर्गः कर्मसंज्ञितः ॥
translation_en: The imperishable is Brahman, the supreme; svabhava is called adhyatma. The offering that brings about the arising of beings is called karma.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अधिभूतं क्षरो भावः पुरुषश्चाधिदैवतम् ।
अधियज्ञोऽहमेवात्र देहे देहभृतां वर ॥
translation_en: Adhibhuta is the perishable nature, and the Purusha is Adhidaivata; and in this body, I alone am the Adhiyajna, O best of embodied beings.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अन्तकाले च मामेव स्मरन्मुक्त्वा कलेवरम् ।
यः प्रयाति स मद्भावं याति नास्त्यत्र संशयः ॥
translation_en: And at the time of death, whoever, remembering Me alone, leaves the body and departs, attains My state; there is no doubt about it.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यं यं वापि स्मरन्भावं त्यजत्यन्ते कलेवरम् ।
तं तमेवैति कौन्तेय सदा तद्भावभावितः ॥
translation_en: Whatever state one remembers when leaving the body at the end, O Kaunteya, one attains that very state, being always absorbed in that state.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तस्मात्सर्वेषु कालेषु मामनुस्मर युध्य च ।
मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्मामेवैष्यस्यसंशयः ॥
translation_en: Therefore, remember Me at all times and fight. With mind and intellect offered to Me, you will surely come to Me, without doubt.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अभ्यासयोगयुक्तेन चेतसा नान्यगामिना ।
परमं पुरुषं दिव्यं याति पार्थानुचिन्तयन् ॥
translation_en: With the mind fixed in practice and yoga, not wandering elsewhere, one attains the supreme divine Purusha by constant contemplation, O Pārtha.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कविं पुराणमनुशासितार-
मणोरणीयंसमनुस्मरेद्यः ।
सर्वस्य धातारमचिन्त्यरूप-
मादित्यवर्णं तमसः परस्तात् ॥
translation_en: Whoever remembers the omniscient, ancient, ruler of all, subtler than the subtle, the supporter of all, of inconceivable form, radiant like the sun, beyond darkness,

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रयाणकाले मनसाऽचलेन
भक्त्या युक्तो योगबलेन चैव ।
भ्रुवोर्मध्ये प्राणमावेश्य सम्यक्
स तं परं पुरुषमुपैति दिव्यम् ॥
translation_en: At the time of departure, with the mind unmoving, and endowed with devotion and indeed with the power of yoga, properly fixing the life-breath between the eyebrows, one attains that divine Supreme Person.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदक्षरं वेदविदो वदन्ति
विशन्ति यद्यतयो वीतरागाः ।
यदिच्छन्तो ब्रह्मचर्यं चरन्ति
तत्ते पदं सङ्ग्रहेण प्रवक्ष्ये ॥
translation_en: I shall now briefly explain to you that imperishable state which the knowers of the Veda call the imperishable, into which the ascetics enter free from attachment, and which those who seek it practice brahmacharya to attain.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वद्वाराणि संयम्य मनो हृदि निरुध्य च ।
मूर्ध्न्याधायात्मनः प्राणमास्थितो योगधारणाम् ॥
translation_en: Restraining all the gates, and confining the mind in the heart, placing the life-breath in the head, he remains steadfast in yoga concentration.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ओमित्येकाक्षरं ब्रह्म व्याहरन्मामनुस्मरन् ।
यः प्रयाति त्यजन्देहं स याति परमां गतिम् ॥
translation_en: Uttering the one-syllable Brahman, Om, and remembering Me, he who departs, leaving the body, attains the supreme goal.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनन्यचेताः सततं यो मां स्मरति नित्यशः ।
तस्याहं सुलभः पार्थ नित्ययुक्तस्य योगिनः ॥
translation_en: For the yogi who, with an undivided mind, constantly and always remembers Me, I am easy to attain, O Partha, for one who is ever devoted.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मामुपेत्य पुनर्जन्म दुःखालयमशाश्वतम् ।
नाप्नुवन्ति महात्मानः संसिद्धिं परमां गताः ॥
translation_en: Having attained Me, the great-souled ones no longer come to rebirth, which is a home of sorrow and impermanence; they have attained the supreme perfection.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आब्रह्मभुवनाल्लोकाः पुनरावर्तिनोऽर्जुन ।
मामुपेत्य तु कौन्तेय पुनर्जन्म न विद्यते ॥
translation_en: Arjuna, all worlds up to the realm of Brahma are subject to return; but having come to Me, O son of Kunti, there is no rebirth.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सहस्रयुगपर्यन्तमहर्यद् ब्रह्मणो विदुः ।
रात्रिं युगसहस्रान्तां तेऽहोरात्रविदो जनाः ॥
translation_en: Those who know the day of Brahman declare it to last for a thousand yugas, and the night, for the end of a thousand yugas; those are the people who know day and night.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अव्यक्ताद् व्यक्तयः सर्वाः प्रभवन्त्यहरागमे ।
रात्र्यागमे प्रलीयन्ते तत्रैवाव्यक्तसंज्ञके ॥
translation_en: At the coming of day, all manifestations arise from the unmanifest; and at the coming of night, they dissolve back into that same unmanifest.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
भूतग्रामः स एवायं भूत्वा भूत्वा प्रलीयते ।
रात्र्यागमेऽवशः पार्थ प्रभवत्यहरागमे ॥
translation_en: This same multitude of beings, again and again coming into being, is dissolved; at the coming of night it is helpless, O Pārtha, and at the coming of day it comes forth.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
परस्तस्मात्तु भावोऽन्योऽव्यक्तोऽव्यक्तात्सनातनः ।
यः स सर्वेषु भूतेषु नश्यत्सु न विनश्यति ॥
translation_en:
But beyond that is another, unmanifest, eternal reality, which is higher than the unmanifest.  
It does not perish when all beings perish.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अव्यक्तोऽक्षर इत्युक्तस्तमाहुः परमां गतिम् ।
यं प्राप्य न निवर्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम ॥
translation_en: The unmanifest is called the imperishable; they speak of it as the supreme goal. Attaining it, they do not return—that is My supreme abode.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पुरुषः स परः पार्थ भक्त्या लभ्यस्त्वनन्यया ।
यस्यान्तःस्थानि भूतानि येन सर्वमिदं ततम् ॥
translation_en: That Supreme Person, O Partha, is attainable by single-minded devotion. All beings dwell within Him, and by Him all this is pervaded.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यत्र काले त्वनावृत्तिमावृत्तिं चैव योगिनः ।
प्रयाता यान्ति तं कालं वक्ष्यामि भरतर्षभ ॥
translation_en: I shall tell you, O best of the Bhāratas, of the time when the yogins who depart attain non-return, and also the time when they return.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अग्निर्ज्योतिरहः शुक्लः षण्मासा उत्तरायणम् ।
तत्र प्रयाता गच्छन्ति ब्रह्म ब्रह्मविदो जनाः ॥
translation_en: Fire, light, day, the bright fortnight, the six months of the northward course—by these, those who depart and know Brahman go to Brahman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
धूमो रात्रिस्तथा कृष्णः षण्मासा दक्षिणायनम् ।
तत्र चान्द्रमसं ज्योतिर्योगी प्राप्य निवर्तते ॥
translation_en: Smoke, night, and likewise the dark fortnight, and the six months of the southern course—there the yogī, attaining the lunar light, returns.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
शुक्लकृष्णे गती ह्येते जगतः शाश्वते मते ।
एकया यात्यनावृत्तिमन्ययावर्तते पुनः ॥
translation_en: These two, the bright and the dark, are indeed held to be the world’s eternal paths; by one one goes on without returning, by the other one returns again.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नैते सृती पार्थ जानन्योगी मुह्यति कश्चन ।
तस्मात्सर्वेषु कालेषु योगयुक्तो भवार्जुन ॥
translation_en: O Pārtha, no yogin who knows these two paths is deluded. Therefore, be united with yoga at all times, O Arjuna.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
वेदेषु यज्ञेषु तपःसु चैव
दानेषु यत्पुण्यफलं प्रदिष्टम् ।
अत्येति तत्सर्वमिदं विदित्वा
योगी परं स्थानमुपैति चाद्यम् ॥
translation_en: Whatever meritorious fruit is declared in the Vedas, in sacrifices, in austerities, and in gifts—having known all this, the yogi surpasses it all and attains the supreme, primordial abode.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इदं तु ते गुह्यतमं प्रवक्ष्याम्यनसूयवे ।
ज्ञानं विज्ञानसहितं यज्ज्ञात्वा मोक्ष्यसेऽशुभात् ॥
translation_en: But to you, who are free from envy, I shall declare this most secret knowledge, together with realization, knowing which you will be freed from inauspiciousness.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
राजविद्या राजगुह्यं पवित्रमिदमुत्तमम् ।
प्रत्यक्षावगमं धर्म्यं सुसुखं कर्तुमव्ययम् ॥
translation_en: This is the royal knowledge, the royal secret, the supreme purifier. It is directly realized, in accord with dharma, very easy to practice, and imperishable.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अश्रद्दधानाः पुरुषा धर्मस्यास्य परन्तप ।
अप्राप्य मां निवर्तन्ते मृत्युसंसारवर्त्मनि ॥
translation_en: Men who have no faith in this dharma, O Parantapa, return to the path of death and samsara without attaining Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मया ततमिदं सर्वं जगदव्यक्तमूर्तिना ।
मत्स्थानि सर्वभूतानि न चाहं तेष्ववस्थितः ॥
translation_en: By Me, in My unmanifest form, this entire universe is pervaded. All beings abide in Me, but I do not abide in them.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न च मत्स्थानि भूतानि पश्य मे योगमैश्वरम् ।
भूतभृन्न च भूतस्थो ममात्मा भूतभावनः ॥
translation_en: And yet beings do not abide in Me. Behold My divine yoga: My Self, the sustainer of beings, is not established in beings, though it supports all beings.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यथाकाशस्थितो नित्यं वायुः सर्वत्रगो महान् ।
तथा सर्वाणि भूतानि मत्स्थानीत्युपधारय ॥
translation_en: As the mighty wind, abiding everywhere in the sky, ever moves through all space, so understand that all beings abide in Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वभूतानि कौन्तेय प्रकृतिं यान्ति मामिकाम् ।
कल्पक्षये पुनस्तानि कल्पादौ विसृजाम्यहम् ॥
translation_en: O son of Kunti, all beings enter into My prakriti at the end of a kalpa; and at the beginning of a kalpa, I send them forth again.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रकृतिं स्वामवष्टभ्य विसृजामि पुनः पुनः ।
भूतग्राममिमं कृत्स्नमवशं प्रकृतेर्वशात् ॥
translation_en: Holding fast to My own prakriti, I again and again send forth this entire multitude of beings, helpless under the sway of prakriti.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न च मां तानि कर्माणि निबध्नन्ति धनञ्जय ।
उदासीनवदासीनमसक्तं तेषु कर्मसु ॥
translation_en: Nor do those actions bind Me, O Dhananjaya, as I sit detached, like one unconcerned, with regard to those actions.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मयाध्यक्षेण प्रकृतिः सूयते सचराचरम् ।
हेतुनानेन कौन्तेय जगद्विपरिवर्तते ॥
translation_en:
Under My supervision, prakriti brings forth the moving and the unmoving.
O Kaunteya, by this cause the world revolves.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अवजानन्ति मां मूढा मानुषीं तनुमाश्रितम् ।
परं भावमजानन्तो मम भूतमहेश्वरम् ॥
translation_en: The foolish despise Me when I have taken on a human body, not knowing My higher nature as the great Lord of beings.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मोघाशा मोघकर्माणो मोघज्ञाना विचेतसः ।
राक्षसीमासुरीं चैव प्रकृतिं मोहिनीं श्रिताः ॥
translation_en: Their hopes are futile, their actions futile, their knowledge futile, and they are deluded in mind. Taking refuge in the deluding, demonic nature, they too follow it.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
महात्मानस्तु मां पार्थ दैवीं प्रकृतिमाश्रिताः ।
भजन्त्यनन्यमनसो ज्ञात्वा भूतादिमव्ययम् ॥
translation_en: But the great souls, O Pārtha, taking refuge in My divine prakriti, worship Me with undivided minds, knowing Me as the imperishable source of beings.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सततं कीर्तयन्तो मां यतन्तश्च दृढव्रताः ।
नमस्यन्तश्च मां भक्त्या नित्ययुक्ता उपासते ॥
translation_en: Always glorifying Me and striving with firm vows, bowing to Me with devotion, ever steadfast, they worship Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ज्ञानयज्ञेन चाप्यन्ये यजन्तो मामुपासते ।
एकत्वेन पृथक्त्वेन बहुधा विश्वतोमुखम् ॥
translation_en: Others, worshiping Me by the sacrifice of knowledge, also devote themselves to Me, perceiving Me as one, as separate, and as having many faces in all directions.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अहं क्रतुरहं यज्ञः स्वधाहमहमौषधम् ।
मन्त्रोऽहमहमेवाज्यमहमग्निरहं हुतम् ॥
translation_en:
I am the ritual; I am the sacrifice; I am the offering to the ancestors; I am the herb.  
I am the mantra; I am the clarified butter; I am the fire; I am the oblation.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पिताहमस्य जगतो माता धाता पितामहः ।
वेद्यं पवित्रमोङ्कार ऋक्साम यजुरेव च ॥
translation_en: I am the father of this universe, the mother, the upholder, and the grandfather; the knowable, the purifier, the syllable Om, and also the Ṛg, Sāma, and Yajur Vedas.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
गतिर्भर्ता प्रभुः साक्षी निवासः शरणं सुहृत् ।
प्रभवः प्रलयः स्थानं निधानं बीजमव्ययम् ॥
translation_en: The goal, the sustainer, the Lord, the witness, the abode, the refuge, and the well-wisher; the source, the dissolution, the resting place, the treasury, and the imperishable seed.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तपाम्यहमहं वर्षं निगृह्णाम्युत्सृजामि च ।
अमृतं चैव मृत्युश्च सदसच्चाहमर्जुन ॥
translation_en:
I send forth heat, I withhold the rain and release it.
I am immortality and death, being and nonbeing, Arjuna.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
त्रैविद्या मां सोमपाः पूतपापा
यज्ञैरिष्ट्वा स्वर्गतिं प्रार्थयन्ते ।
ते पुण्यमासाद्य सुरेन्द्रलोक-
मश्नन्ति दिव्यान्दिवि देवभोगान् ॥
translation_en: The knowers of the three Vedas, drinkers of soma, purified of sin, worship Me with sacrifices and pray for access to heaven. Having attained merit, they reach the world of Indra and enjoy the divine pleasures of the gods in heaven.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ते तं भुक्त्वा स्वर्गलोकं विशालं
क्षीणे पुण्ये मर्त्यलोकं विशन्ति ।
एवं त्रयीधर्ममनुप्रपन्ना
गतागतं कामकामा लभन्ते ॥
translation_en: Having enjoyed that vast heavenly world, when their merit is exhausted, they enter the world of mortals. Thus, adhering to the dharma of the three Vedas, those desirous of pleasures attain the state of coming and going.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनन्याश्चिन्तयन्तो मां ये जनाः पर्युपासते ।
तेषां नित्याभियुक्तानां योगक्षेमं वहाम्यहम् ॥
translation_en: Those who, thinking of Me as the sole object, worship Me with exclusive devotion—I carry what they lack and preserve what they have.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
येऽप्यन्यदेवता भक्ता यजन्ते श्रद्धयान्विताः ।
तेऽपि मामेव कौन्तेय यजन्त्यविधिपूर्वकम् ॥
translation_en: Those devotees of other deities who worship with faith, O Kaunteya, also worship Me alone, though not according to the proper method.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अहं हि सर्वयज्ञानां भोक्ता च प्रभुरेव च ।
न तु मामभिजानन्ति तत्त्वेनातश्च्यवन्ति ते ॥
translation_en: For I am indeed the enjoyer and also the lord of all yajnas; but they do not know Me in truth, and therefore they fall away.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यान्ति देवव्रता देवान्पितॄन्यान्ति पितृव्रताः ।
भूतानि यान्ति भूतेज्या यान्ति मद्याजिनोऽपि माम् ॥
translation_en: Those devoted to the devas go to the devas; those devoted to the pitṛs go to the pitṛs. Those who worship the beings go to the beings; those who worship Me come to Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पत्रं पुष्पं फलं तोयं यो मे भक्त्या प्रयच्छति ।
तदहं भक्त्युपहृतमश्नामि प्रयतात्मनः ॥
translation_en:
Whoever offers Me, with devotion, a leaf, a flower, a fruit, or water,  
I accept that devoted offering from the self-disciplined one.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यत्करोषि यदश्नासि यज्जुहोषि ददासि यत् ।
यत्तपस्यसि कौन्तेय तत्कुरुष्व मदर्पणम् ॥
translation_en: Whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer, whatever you give, whatever austerities you perform, O Kaunteya, do that as an offering to Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
शुभाशुभफलैरेवं मोक्ष्यसे कर्मबन्धनैः ।
संन्यासयोगयुक्तात्मा विमुक्तो मामुपैष्यसि ॥
translation_en:
Thus you will be freed from the bondage of action, with its good and evil results.  
With the self disciplined by the yoga of renunciation, you will come to Me, मुक्त from bondage.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
समोऽहं सर्वभूतेषु न मे द्वेष्योऽस्ति न प्रियः ।
ये भजन्ति तु मां भक्त्या मयि ते तेषु चाप्यहम् ॥
translation_en:
I am equal toward all beings; there is none hateful to Me and none dear.  
But those who worship Me with devotion are in Me, and I too am in them.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अपि चेत्सुदुराचारो भजते मामनन्यभाक् ।
साधुरेव स मन्तव्यः सम्यग्व्यवसितो हि सः ॥
translation_en: Even if one of very wicked conduct worships Me with undivided devotion, he should indeed be regarded as righteous, for he has rightly resolved.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
क्षिप्रं भवति धर्मात्मा शश्वच्छान्तिं निगच्छति ।
कौन्तेय प्रतिजानीहि न मे भक्तः प्रणश्यति ॥
translation_en:
Soon he becomes righteous in soul and attains lasting peace.  
O Kaunteya, proclaim it: My devotee never perishes.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मां हि पार्थ व्यपाश्रित्य येऽपि स्युः पापयोनयः ।
स्त्रियो वैश्यास्तथा शूद्रास्तेऽपि यान्ति परां गतिम् ॥
translation_en: For, O Pārtha, those who take refuge in Me, even if they are born from a sinful womb—women, vaiśyas, and śūdras—even they attain the supreme goal.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
किं पुनर्ब्राह्मणाः पुण्या भक्ता राजर्षयस्तथा ।
अनित्यमसुखं लोकमिमं प्राप्य भजस्व माम् ॥
translation_en: How much more, then, pious brāhmaṇas and devoted royal sages! Having attained this impermanent, joyless world, worship Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु ।
मामेवैष्यसि युक्त्वैवमात्मानं मत्परायणः ॥
translation_en:
Fix your mind on Me, be devoted to Me, sacrifice to Me, bow to Me.  
Thus, with your self intent on Me as your supreme goal, you will come to Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
भूय एव महाबाहो श‍ृणु मे परमं वचः ।
यत्तेऽहं प्रीयमाणाय वक्ष्यामि हितकाम्यया ॥
translation_en: Once again, O mighty-armed one, hear my supreme word. Out of desire for your welfare, I shall speak to you, who are dear to me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न मे विदुः सुरगणाः प्रभवं न महर्षयः ।
अहमादिर्हि देवानां महर्षीणां च सर्वशः ॥
translation_en: Neither the hosts of the devas nor the great sages know My source; for I am indeed the origin of the devas and of the great sages in every respect.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यो मामजमनादिं च वेत्ति लोकमहेश्वरम् ।
असम्मूढः स मर्त्येषु सर्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते ॥
translation_en: He who knows Me as unborn and without beginning, and as the great Lord of the worlds, undeluded among mortals, is released from all sins.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
बुद्धिर्ज्ञानमसम्मोहः क्षमा सत्यं दमः शमः ।
सुखं दुःखं भवोऽभावो भयं चाभयमेव च ॥
translation_en:
Intelligence, knowledge, non-delusion, forgiveness, truth, self-restraint, and calmness;
pleasure and pain, being and non-being, fear and fearlessness.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अहिंसा समता तुष्टिस्तपो दानं यशोऽयशः ।
भवन्ति भावा भूतानां मत्त एव पृथग्विधाः ॥
translation_en: Nonviolence, equanimity, contentment, austerity, charity, and fame and infamy—these diverse dispositions of beings arise from Me alone.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
महर्षयः सप्त पूर्वे चत्वारो मनवस्तथा ।
मद्भावा मानसा जाता येषां लोक इमाः प्रजाः ॥
translation_en: The seven great sages of old, and the four Manus as well, were born of My mind; from them these creatures of the world have come forth.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एतां विभूतिं योगं च मम यो वेत्ति तत्त्वतः ।
सोऽविकम्पेन योगेन युज्यते नात्र संशयः ॥
translation_en:
He who truly knows this glory of Mine and this yoga
is united with unwavering yoga; there is no doubt about it.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अहं सर्वस्य प्रभवो मत्तः सर्वं प्रवर्तते ।
इति मत्वा भजन्ते मां बुधा भावसमन्विताः ॥
translation_en: I am the source of all; from Me everything proceeds. Knowing this, the wise, endowed with true feeling, worship Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मच्चित्ता मद्गतप्राणा बोधयन्तः परस्परम् ।
कथयन्तश्च मां नित्यं तुष्यन्ति च रमन्ति च ॥
translation_en: With their minds fixed on Me, their lives devoted to Me, enlightening one another and ever speaking of Me, they are content and rejoicing.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तेषां सततयुक्तानां भजतां प्रीतिपूर्वकम् ।
ददामि बुद्धियोगं तं येन मामुपयान्ति ते ॥
translation_en: To those who are ever devoted and worship Me with love, I give the yoga of understanding by which they come to Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तेषामेवानुकम्पार्थमहमज्ञानजं तमः ।
नाशयाम्यात्मभावस्थो ज्ञानदीपेन भास्वता ॥
translation_en: Out of compassion for them, I, abiding in their inner being, destroy the darkness born of ignorance with the shining lamp of knowledge.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
परं ब्रह्म परं धाम पवित्रं परमं भवान् ।
पुरुषं शाश्वतं दिव्यमादिदेवमजं विभुम् ॥
translation_en: You are the Supreme Brahman, the supreme abode, the supremely pure one; the eternal, divine Purusha, the primal God, unborn and all-pervading.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आहुस्त्वामृषयः सर्वे देवर्षिर्नारदस्तथा ।
असितो देवलो व्यासः स्वयं चैव ब्रवीषि मे ॥
translation_en: All the sages, as well as the devarṣi Nārada, Asita, Devala, and Vyāsa, have said this of You, and You yourself are saying it to me.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वमेतदृतं मन्ये यन्मां वदसि केशव ।
न हि ते भगवन्व्यक्तिं विदुर्देवा न दानवाः ॥
translation_en: I consider all this that you speak to me as true, O Keśava; for neither the devas nor the dānavas know Your manifestation, O Bhagavan.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
स्वयमेवात्मनात्मानं वेत्थ त्वं पुरुषोत्तम ।
भूतभावन भूतेश देवदेव जगत्पते ॥
translation_en:
You yourself know yourself by yourself, O Purushottama.  
O source of all beings, lord of beings, god of gods, lord of the world.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
वक्तुमर्हस्यशेषेण दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतयः ।
याभिर्विभूतिभिर्लोकानिमांस्त्वं व्याप्य तिष्ठसि ॥
translation_en: You should speak in full of your divine manifestations, by which you pervade these worlds and abide in them.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कथं विद्यामहं योगिंस्त्वां सदा परिचिन्तयन् ।
केषु केषु च भावेषु चिन्त्योऽसि भगवन्मया ॥
translation_en: How am I to know You, O yogin, by constantly contemplating You? And in what, and in what states, should You be contemplated by me, O Bhagavan?

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
विस्तरेणात्मनो योगं विभूतिं च जनार्दन ।
भूयः कथय तृप्तिर्हि श‍ृण्वतो नास्ति मेऽमृतम् ॥
translation_en: O Janārdana, tell me once more in detail of your yoga and your manifestations, for I am not yet satisfied with hearing this nectar.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
हन्त ते कथयिष्यामि दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतयः ।
प्राधान्यतः कुरुश्रेष्ठ नास्त्यन्तो विस्तरस्य मे ॥
translation_en: Well, I will tell you of my divine manifestations, chief among them, O best of the Kurus; there is no end to the विस्तार of mine.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अहमात्मा गुडाकेश सर्वभूताशयस्थितः ।
अहमादिश्च मध्यं च भूतानामन्त एव च ॥
translation_en: O Gudakesha, I am the Atman dwelling in the heart of all beings. I am the beginning, the middle, and also the end of beings.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आदित्यानामहं विष्णुर्ज्योतिषां रविरंशुमान् ।
मरीचिर्मरुतामस्मि नक्षत्राणामहं शशी ॥
translation_en:
Among the Ādityas I am Viṣṇu; among lights, the radiant sun.  
Among the Maruts I am Marīci; among the stars I am the moon.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
वेदानां सामवेदोऽस्मि देवानामस्मि वासवः ।
इन्द्रियाणां मनश्चास्मि भूतानामस्मि चेतना ॥
translation_en:
Among the Vedas I am the Sāma Veda; among the gods I am Vāsava.  
Among the senses I am the mind, and among beings I am consciousness.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
रुद्राणां शङ्करश्चास्मि वित्तेशो यक्षरक्षसाम् ।
वसूनां पावकश्चास्मि मेरुः शिखरिणामहम् ॥
translation_en: Among the Rudras I am Shankara, and among the Yakshas and Rakshasas I am the lord of wealth; among the Vasus I am fire, and among mountains I am Meru.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पुरोधसां च मुख्यं मां विद्धि पार्थ बृहस्पतिम् ।
सेनानीनामहं स्कन्दः सरसामस्मि सागरः ॥
translation_en: Among priests, O Pārtha, know Me to be Bṛhaspati, the chief; among army commanders, I am Skanda; among lakes, I am the ocean.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
महर्षीणां भृगुरहं गिरामस्म्येकमक्षरम् ।
यज्ञानां जपयज्ञोऽस्मि स्थावराणां हिमालयः ॥
translation_en: Among the great sages, I am Bhṛgu; among utterances, the single syllable; among sacrifices, I am the sacrifice of japa; among the immovable, I am the Himalayas.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अश्वत्थः सर्ववृक्षाणां देवर्षीणां च नारदः ।
गन्धर्वाणां चित्ररथः सिद्धानां कपिलो मुनिः ॥
translation_en:
Among all trees, I am the aśvattha; among the divine sages, I am Nārada.  
Among the Gandharvas, I am Citraratha; among the Siddhas, I am the sage Kapila.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
उच्चैःश्रवसमश्वानां विद्धि माममृतोद्भवम् ।
ऐरावतं गजेन्द्राणां नराणां च नराधिपम् ॥
translation_en: Know Me as Ucchaihshravas among horses, born of nectar; among lordly elephants, Airavata; and among men, the king.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आयुधानामहं वज्रं धेनूनामस्मि कामधुक् ।
प्रजनश्चास्मि कन्दर्पः सर्पाणामस्मि वासुकिः ॥
translation_en:
Among weapons, I am the thunderbolt; among cows, I am Kamadhuk.  
Among those who produce offspring, I am Kandarpa; among serpents, I am Vāsuki.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनन्तश्चास्मि नागानां वरुणो यादसामहम् ।
पितॄणामर्यमा चास्मि यमः संयमतामहम् ॥
translation_en:
Among the nāgas I am Ananta, and among the aquatic beings I am Varuṇa.  
Among the ancestors I am Aryaman, and among those who restrain I am Yama.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रह्लादश्चास्मि दैत्यानां कालः कलयतामहम् ।
मृगाणां च मृगेन्द्रोऽहं वैनतेयश्च पक्षिणाम् ॥
translation_en: Among the daityas I am Prahlada, and among those who measure, I am Time. Among beasts I am the lord of beasts, and among birds I am Vainateya.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पवनः पवतामस्मि रामः शस्त्रभृतामहम् ।
झषाणां मकरश्चास्मि स्रोतसामस्मि जाह्नवी ॥
translation_en: Among the winds I am Vayu, among the wielders of weapons I am Rama; among fishes I am the makara, and among rivers I am the Jahnavi.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्गाणामादिरन्तश्च मध्यं चैवाहमर्जुन ।
अध्यात्मविद्या विद्यानां वादः प्रवदतामहम् ॥
translation_en: O Arjuna, among creations I am the beginning, the end, and the middle. Among knowledges, I am the knowledge of the self; among disputants, I am the debate.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अक्षराणामकारोऽस्मि द्वन्द्वः सामासिकस्य च ।
अहमेवाक्षयः कालो धाताहं विश्वतोमुखः ॥
translation_en:
Among letters I am A, and of compounds I am the dual;  
I am also inexhaustible Time, the sustainer, and the one with faces everywhere.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मृत्युः सर्वहरश्चाहमुद्भवश्च भविष्यताम् ।
कीर्तिः श्रीर्वाक्च नारीणां स्मृतिर्मेधा धृतिः क्षमा ॥
translation_en:
I am Death, the all-seizer, and the source of what is yet to come.  
Among women, I am fame, splendor, speech, memory, intelligence, steadfastness, and forgiveness.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
बृहत्साम तथा साम्नां गायत्री छन्दसामहम् ।
मासानां मार्गशीर्षोऽहमृतूनां कुसुमाकरः ॥
translation_en: Among the Sāma hymns I am the Bṛhat-sāma; among meters, I am Gāyatrī. Among months I am Mārgaśīrṣa, and among the seasons I am spring, the season of flowers.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
द्यूतं छलयतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम् ।
जयोऽस्मि व्यवसायोऽस्मि सत्त्वं सत्त्ववतामहम् ॥
translation_en: I am the gambling of cheaters, and I am the splendor of the splendid. I am victory, I am resolve, and I am the sattva of those possessed of sattva.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
वृष्णीनां वासुदेवोऽस्मि पाण्डवानां धनञ्जयः ।
मुनीनामप्यहं व्यासः कवीनामुशना कविः ॥
translation_en:
Among the Vṛṣṇis, I am Vāsudeva; among the Pāṇḍavas, I am Dhananjaya.  
Among the sages, I am Vyāsa; among the poets, I am Uśanas, the poet.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
दण्डो दमयतामस्मि नीतिरस्मि जिगीषताम् ।
मौनं चैवास्मि गुह्यानां ज्ञानं ज्ञानवतामहम् ॥
translation_en:
Among those who punish, I am punishment; among those who seek victory, I am policy.  
Among secrets, I am silence; among the wise, I am knowledge.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यच्चापि सर्वभूतानां बीजं तदहमर्जुन ।
न तदस्ति विना यत्स्यान्मया भूतं चराचरम् ॥
translation_en:
And I am also the seed of all beings, Arjuna;  
there is nothing moving or unmoving that could exist without Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नान्तोऽस्ति मम दिव्यानां विभूतीनां परन्तप ।
एष तूद्देशतः प्रोक्तो विभूतेर्विस्तरो मया ॥
translation_en: O scorcher of foes, there is no end to My divine manifestations. This has been spoken by Me only in summary of the विस्तार of My opulences.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यद्यद्विभूतिमत्सत्त्वं श्रीमदूर्जितमेव वा ।
तत्तदेवावगच्छ त्वं मम तेजोंऽशसम्भवम् ॥
translation_en: Whatever being is glorious, prosperous, or powerful, know that it is born from a part of My splendor.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अथवा बहुनैतेन किं ज्ञातेन तवार्जुन ।
विष्टभ्याहमिदं कृत्स्नमेकांशेन स्थितो जगत् ॥
translation_en: Or what is the use of knowing all this, Arjuna? I stand pervading this entire world with a single fraction of myself.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मदनुग्रहाय परमं गुह्यमध्यात्मसंज्ञितम् ।
यत्त्वयोक्तं वचस्तेन मोहोऽयं विगतो मम ॥
translation_en: Out of grace toward me, you have spoken this supreme secret, known as the teaching of the Self; by those words, this delusion of mine has now been dispelled.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
भवाप्ययौ हि भूतानां श्रुतौ विस्तरशो मया ।
त्वत्तः कमलपत्राक्ष माहात्म्यमपि चाव्ययम् ॥
translation_en: I have heard in detail from you of the arising and dissolution of beings, and also of your unfailing majesty, O lotus-eyed one.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एवमेतद्यथात्थ त्वमात्मानं परमेश्वर ।
द्रष्टुमिच्छामि ते रूपमैश्वरं पुरुषोत्तम ॥
translation_en: Thus, as You describe Yourself, O supreme Lord, I wish to see Your divine form, O Purushottama.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मन्यसे यदि तच्छक्यं मया द्रष्टुमिति प्रभो ।
योगेश्वर ततो मे त्वं दर्शयात्मानमव्ययम् ॥
translation_en: If you think that I can see it, O Lord, then show me your imperishable Self, O Yogeshvara.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पश्य मे पार्थ रूपाणि शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ।
नानाविधानि दिव्यानि नानावर्णाकृतीनि च ॥
translation_en: Behold, O Partha, my forms in hundreds and thousands, of many kinds, divine, and of many colors and shapes.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पश्यादित्यान्वसून्रुद्रानश्विनौ मरुतस्तथा ।
बहून्यदृष्टपूर्वाणि पश्याश्चर्याणि भारत ॥
translation_en:
See the Adityas, the Vasus, the Rudras, the Aśvins, and the Maruts as well.  
O Bharata, see many wonders never seen before.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इहैकस्थं जगत्कृत्स्नं पश्याद्य सचराचरम् ।
मम देहे गुडाकेश यच्चान्यद् द्रष्टुमिच्छसि ॥
translation_en: Here, in one place, see today the whole universe, with all that moves and does not move, O Guḍākeśa; and whatever else you wish to see in My body.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न तु मां शक्यसे द्रष्टुमनेनैव स्वचक्षुषा ।
दिव्यं ददामि ते चक्षुः पश्य मे योगमैश्वरम् ॥
translation_en: But you cannot see Me with these, your own eyes. I give you divine vision; behold My divine yoga.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
एवमुक्त्वा ततो राजन्महायोगेश्वरो हरिः ।
दर्शयामास पार्थाय परमं रूपमैश्वरम् ॥
translation_en: Having spoken thus, O king, Hari, the great Lord of yoga, then showed to Partha His supreme divine form.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनेकवक्त्रनयनमनेकाद्भुतदर्शनम् ।
अनेकदिव्याभरणं दिव्यानेकोद्यतायुधम् ॥
translation_en: with many faces and eyes, and many wondrous forms; adorned with many divine ornaments, and bearing many uplifted divine weapons.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
दिव्यमाल्याम्बरधरं दिव्यगन्धानुलेपनम् ।
सर्वाश्चर्यमयं देवमनन्तं विश्वतोमुखम् ॥
translation_en:
Wearing divine garlands and garments, anointed with divine fragrances,  
the wondrous god, infinite, with faces on every side.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
दिवि सूर्यसहस्रस्य भवेद्युगपदुत्थिता ।
यदि भाः सदृशी सा स्याद्भासस्तस्य महात्मनः ॥
translation_en: If a thousand suns were to rise together in the sky, that might be like the splendor of that great soul.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
तत्रैकस्थं जगत्कृत्स्नं प्रविभक्तमनेकधा ।
अपश्यद्देवदेवस्य शरीरे पाण्डवस्तदा ॥
translation_en: There, the Pāṇḍava then saw the entire universe, all in one place, yet divided in many ways, in the body of the god of gods.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
ततः स विस्मयाविष्टो हृष्टरोमा धनञ्जयः ।
प्रणम्य शिरसा देवं कृताञ्जलिरभाषत ॥
translation_en: Then Dhananjaya, filled with wonder, his hair standing on end, bowed his head to the Deva with joined palms and spoke.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पश्यामि देवांस्तव देव देहे
सर्वांस्तथा भूतविशेषसङ्घान् ।
ब्रह्माणमीशं कमलासनस्थ-
मृषींश्च सर्वानुरगांश्च दिव्यान् ॥
translation_en: I see all the gods in your body, O God, and all the diverse host of beings; Brahma seated on the lotus, the sages, and all the divine serpents.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनेकबाहूदरवक्त्रनेत्रं
पश्यामि त्वां सर्वतोऽनन्तरूपम् ।
नान्तं न मध्यं न पुनस्तवादिं
पश्यामि विश्वेश्वर विश्वरूप ॥
translation_en:
I see You with many arms, bellies, mouths, and eyes,  
with forms unending on every side.  
I see no end, no middle, and no beginning of You,  
O Lord of the universe, O universal form.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
किरीटिनं गदिनं चक्रिणं च
तेजोराशिं सर्वतो दीप्तिमन्तम् ।
पश्यामि त्वां दुर्निरीक्ष्यं समन्ताद्
दीप्तानलार्कद्युतिमप्रमेयम् ॥
translation_en:
I see you with a crown, a mace, and a discus,
a mass of radiance, shining on all sides,
hard to gaze upon from every direction,
immeasurable in splendor, like blazing fire and the sun.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
त्वमक्षरं परमं वेदितव्यं
त्वमस्य विश्वस्य परं निधानम् ।
त्वमव्ययः शाश्वतधर्मगोप्ता
सनातनस्त्वं पुरुषो मतो मे ॥

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनादिमध्यान्तमनन्तवीर्य-
मनन्तबाहुं शशिसूर्यनेत्रम् ।
पश्यामि त्वां दीप्तहुताशवक्त्रं
स्वतेजसा विश्वमिदं तपन्तम् ॥
translation_en: I see you as without beginning, middle, or end, of infinite power, with endless arms and with eyes like the moon and the sun, with a face of blazing fire, scorching this entire universe with your own radiance.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
द्यावापृथिव्योरिदमन्तरं हि
व्याप्तं त्वयैकेन दिशश्च सर्वाः ।
दृष्ट्वाद्भुतं रूपमुग्रं तवेदं
लोकत्रयं प्रव्यथितं महात्मन् ॥
translation_en: Indeed, this whole space between heaven and earth and all the directions is pervaded by you alone. Seeing this wondrous, terrible form of yours, the three worlds are shaken, O Mahātman.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अमी हि त्वां सुरसङ्घा विशन्ति
केचिद्भीताः प्राञ्जलयो गृणन्ति ।
स्वस्तीत्युक्त्वा महर्षिसिद्धसङ्घाः
स्तुवन्ति त्वां स्तुतिभिः पुष्कलाभिः ॥
translation_en:
Indeed, these hosts of the gods enter you;
some, भयभीत, with folded hands praise you.
The hosts of great sages and Siddhas, saying “svasti,”
extol you with abundant hymns.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
रुद्रादित्या वसवो ये च साध्या
विश्वेऽश्विनौ मरुतश्चोष्मपाश्च ।
गन्धर्वयक्षासुरसिद्धसङ्घा
वीक्षन्ते त्वां विस्मिताश्चैव सर्वे ॥
translation_en:
The Rudras, the Adityas, the Vasus, and the Sadhyas,  
the Viśve, the Aśvins, the Maruts, and the Uṣmapās;  
the Gandharvas, Yakshas, Asuras, and Siddhas—  
all of them gaze at you in wonder.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
रूपं महत्ते बहुवक्त्रनेत्रं
महाबाहो बहुबाहूरुपादम् ।
बहूदरं बहुदंष्ट्राकरालं
दृष्ट्वा लोकाः प्रव्यथितास्तथाहम् ॥
translation_en:
Your great form, O mighty-armed one, has many faces and eyes,  
many arms, thighs, and feet;  
many bellies, and terrible with many teeth.  
Seeing this, the worlds are shaken, and so am I.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नभःस्पृशं दीप्तमनेकवर्णं
व्यात्ताननं दीप्तविशालनेत्रम् ।
दृष्ट्वा हि त्वां प्रव्यथितान्तरात्मा
धृतिं न विन्दामि शमं च विष्णो ॥
translation_en:
Touching the sky, blazing, of many colors, with open mouths and blazing, vast eyes,  
seeing you, my inner self is shaken; I find neither steadiness nor peace, O Vishnu.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
दंष्ट्राकरालानि च ते मुखानि
दृष्ट्वैव कालानलसन्निभानि ।
दिशो न जाने न लभे च शर्म
प्रसीद देवेश जगन्निवास ॥
translation_en: Seeing your faces, terrible with fangs and resembling the fire of time, I know not the directions and find no peace. Be gracious, O Lord of the gods, abode of the universe.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अमी च त्वां धृतराष्ट्रस्य पुत्राः
सर्वे सहैवावनिपालसङ्घैः ।
भीष्मो द्रोणः सूतपुत्रस्तथासौ
सहास्मदीयैरपि योधमुख्यैः ॥
translation_en: And these, the sons of Dhritarashtra, all together with the hosts of kings of the earth: Bhishma, Drona, and also that son of Suta, together with our chief warriors.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
वक्त्राणि ते त्वरमाणा विशन्ति
दंष्ट्राकरालानि भयानकानि ।
केचिद्विलग्ना दशनान्तरेषु
सन्दृश्यन्ते चूर्णितैरुत्तमाङ्गैः ॥
translation_en:
I see Your mouths, terrible with fangs, swallowing them in haste;  
some are seen with their heads crushed, caught between the spaces of the teeth.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यथा नदीनां बहवोऽम्बुवेगाः
समुद्रमेवाभिमुखा द्रवन्ति ।
तथा तवामी नरलोकवीरा
विशन्ति वक्त्राण्यभिविज्वलन्ति ॥
translation_en:
As many currents of rivers rush toward the sea,  
so these heroes of the human world enter your blazing mouths.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यथा प्रदीप्तं ज्वलनं पतङ्गा
विशन्ति नाशाय समृद्धवेगाः ।
तथैव नाशाय विशन्ति लोका-
स्तवापि वक्त्राणि समृद्धवेगाः ॥
translation_en: As moths with great speed enter a blazing flame to their destruction, so too the worlds, with great speed, enter your mouths to destruction.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
लेलिह्यसे ग्रसमानः समन्ताल्-
लोकान्समग्रान्वदनैर्ज्वलद्भिः ।
तेजोभिरापूर्य जगत्समग्रं
भासस्तवोग्राः प्रतपन्ति विष्णो ॥
translation_en:
You are licking up all the worlds on every side with your flaming mouths,  
filling the entire universe with your radiance.  
Your fierce rays are scorching the whole world, O Vishnu.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आख्याहि मे को भवानुग्ररूपो
नमोऽस्तु ते देववर प्रसीद ।
विज्ञातुमिच्छामि भवन्तमाद्यं
न हि प्रजानामि तव प्रवृत्तिम् ॥
translation_en:
Tell me who you are, O fierce-formed one.  
Homage to you, best of the devas; be gracious.  
I wish to know you, the primal one;  
for I do not understand your activity.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कालोऽस्मि लोकक्षयकृत्प्रवृद्धो
लोकान्समाहर्तुमिह प्रवृत्तः ।
ऋतेऽपि त्वां न भविष्यन्ति सर्वे
येऽवस्थिताः प्रत्यनीकेषु योधाः ॥
translation_en: I am Time, grown to maturity, the destroyer of the worlds, engaged here in gathering the worlds together. Even without you, all the warriors stationed in the opposing armies will not survive.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तस्मात्त्वमुत्तिष्ठ यशो लभस्व
जित्वा शत्रून् भुङ्क्ष्व राज्यं समृद्धम् ।
मयैवैते निहताः पूर्वमेव
निमित्तमात्रं भव सव्यसाचिन् ॥

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
द्रोणं च भीष्मं च जयद्रथं च
कर्णं तथान्यानपि योधवीरान् ।
मया हतांस्त्वं जहि मा व्यथिष्ठा
युध्यस्व जेतासि रणे सपत्नान् ॥
translation_en:
Drona and Bhishma and Jayadratha,  
Karna, and other valiant warriors too—  
slay those who have already been slain by me;  
do not be troubled. Fight, and you will conquer your foes in battle.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
एतच्छ्रुत्वा वचनं केशवस्य
कृताञ्जलिर्वेपमानः किरीटी ।
नमस्कृत्वा भूय एवाह कृष्णं
सगद्गदं भीतभीतः प्रणम्य ॥
translation_en: Hearing these words of Keshava, Kiriti, with folded hands and trembling, bowed down to Krishna again and again. In fear and with a choked voice, he prostrated himself.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
स्थाने हृषीकेश तव प्रकीर्त्या
जगत्प्रहृष्यत्यनुरज्यते च ।
रक्षांसि भीतानि दिशो द्रवन्ति
सर्वे नमस्यन्ति च सिद्धसङ्घाः ॥
translation_en: Rightly, O Hrishikesha, the world rejoices and delights in your glory; the fearful rakshasas flee in all directions, and all the hosts of siddhas bow down.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कस्माच्च ते न नमेरन्महात्मन्
गरीयसे ब्रह्मणोऽप्यादिकर्त्रे ।
अनन्त देवेश जगन्निवास
त्वमक्षरं सदसत्तत्परं यत् ॥
translation_en:
And why should they not bow to you, O Mahatman, greater than even Brahman, the primal creator?  
O infinite one, Lord of the gods, abode of the world, you are the imperishable, the supreme reality beyond being and non-being.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
त्वमादिदेवः पुरुषः पुराण-
स्त्वमस्य विश्वस्य परं निधानम् ।
वेत्तासि वेद्यं च परं च धाम
त्वया ततं विश्वमनन्तरूप ॥
translation_en:
You are the original Deva, the ancient Purusha,
the supreme refuge of this universe.
You are the knower and the knowable, and the supreme abode;
the universe is pervaded by you, whose forms are endless.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
वायुर्यमोऽग्निर्वरुणः शशाङ्कः
प्रजापतिस्त्वं प्रपितामहश्च ।
नमो नमस्तेऽस्तु सहस्रकृत्वः
पुनश्च भूयोऽपि नमो नमस्ते ॥
translation_en:
You are Vayu, Yama, Agni, Varuna, the Moon, Prajapati, and the great-grandfather.  
Obeisance to you a thousand times; again and again, obeisance to you.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नमः पुरस्तादथ पृष्ठतस्ते
नमोऽस्तु ते सर्वत एव सर्व ।
अनन्तवीर्यामितविक्रमस्त्वं
सर्वं समाप्नोषि ततोऽसि सर्वः ॥

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सखेति मत्वा प्रसभं यदुक्तं
हे कृष्ण हे यादव हे सखेति ।
अजानता महिमानं तवेदं
मया प्रमादात्प्रणयेन वापि ॥
translation_en:
Thinking of You as a friend, I addressed You rashly, saying, “O Krishna, O Yadava, O friend,”  
not knowing this greatness of Yours,  
whether through carelessness or through affection.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यच्चावहासार्थमसत्कृतोऽसि
विहारशय्यासनभोजनेषु ।
एकोऽथवाप्यच्युत तत्समक्षं
तत्क्षामये त्वामहमप्रमेयम् ॥
translation_en: Whatever I may have treated you with disrespect, in jest or through familiarity, in recreation, in bed, at rest, or at meals, O Acyuta, whether alone or in the presence of others—I beg forgiveness from you, the immeasurable One.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पितासि लोकस्य चराचरस्य
त्वमस्य पूज्यश्च गुरुर्गरीयान् ।
न त्वत्समोऽस्त्यभ्यधिकः कुतोऽन्यो
लोकत्रयेऽप्यप्रतिमप्रभाव ॥
translation_en:
You are the father of the moving and unmoving world,
its object of reverence and the greatest guru.
There is none equal to you; how then could there be anyone greater than you,
unrivaled in power in the three worlds.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तस्मात्प्रणम्य प्रणिधाय कायं
प्रसादये त्वामहमीशमीड्यम् ।
पितेव पुत्रस्य सखेव सख्युः
प्रियः प्रियायार्हसि देव सोढुम् ॥
translation_en: Therefore, bowing down and laying my body low, I beg You, O Lord worthy of praise, to be gracious to me. Like a father to his son, like a friend to his friend, like one dear to one dear, O God, You should endure this.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अदृष्टपूर्वं हृषितोऽस्मि दृष्ट्वा
भयेन च प्रव्यथितं मनो मे ।
तदेव मे दर्शय देव रूपं
प्रसीद देवेश जगन्निवास ॥
translation_en:
Seeing what I had never seen before, I am filled with delight, yet my mind is also shaken with fear.  
Show me again that very form, O Deva; be gracious, O Lord of the gods, abode of the universe.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
किरीटिनं गदिनं चक्रहस्तं
इच्छामि त्वां द्रष्टुमहं तथैव ।
तेनैव रूपेण चतुर्भुजेन
सहस्रबाहो भव विश्वमूर्ते ॥
translation_en:
I wish to see you, crowned, holding the mace and discus,
just as before, in that same four-armed form.
O thousand-armed one, O universal form, appear thus.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मया प्रसन्नेन तवार्जुनेदं
रूपं परं दर्शितमात्मयोगात् ।
तेजोमयं विश्वमनन्तमाद्यं
यन्मे त्वदन्येन न दृष्टपूर्वम् ॥
translation_en: Out of favor toward you, Arjuna, I have shown this supreme form by my own yoga: the radiant, infinite, primal universal form, which none before you has ever seen.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न वेदयज्ञाध्ययनैर्न दानै-
र्न च क्रियाभिर्न तपोभिरुग्रैः ।
एवंरूपः शक्य अहं नृलोके
द्रष्टुं त्वदन्येन कुरुप्रवीर ॥
translation_en: Neither by study of the Vedas and sacrifices, nor by gifts, nor by rites, nor by severe austerities can I be seen in this human world in this form, O foremost of the Kurus, by anyone other than you.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मा ते व्यथा मा च विमूढभावो
दृष्ट्वा रूपं घोरमीदृङ्ममेदम् ।
व्यपेतभीः प्रीतमनाः पुनस्त्वं
तदेव मे रूपमिदं प्रपश्य ॥
translation_en:
Do not be troubled, and do not be bewildered at seeing this terrible form of Mine.  
With fear dispelled and your mind made glad, look again upon this very form of Mine.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
इत्यर्जुनं वासुदेवस्तथोक्त्वा
स्वकं रूपं दर्शयामास भूयः ।
आश्वासयामास च भीतमेनं
भूत्वा पुनः सौम्यवपुर्महात्मा ॥
translation_en: Thus, having spoken thus to Arjuna, Vasudeva once more revealed his own form. Then the great-souled one, taking on a gentle form again, reassured this terrified one.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
दृष्ट्वेदं मानुषं रूपं तव सौम्यं जनार्दन ।
इदानीमस्मि संवृत्तः सचेताः प्रकृतिं गतः ॥
translation_en:
Seeing this gentle human form of Yours, O Janārdana,  
I have now come back to my senses and returned to my nature.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सुदुर्दर्शमिदं रूपं दृष्टवानसि यन्मम ।
देवा अप्यस्य रूपस्य नित्यं दर्शनकाङ्क्षिणः ॥
translation_en: This form of Mine, which you have seen, is exceedingly difficult to behold; even the gods are always eager to see this form.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नाहं वेदैर्न तपसा न दानेन न चेज्यया ।
शक्य एवंविधो द्रष्टुं दृष्टवानसि मां यथा ॥
translation_en: Neither by the Vedas, nor by austerity, nor by charity, nor by sacrifice can I be seen in this form as you have seen Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
भक्त्या त्वनन्यया शक्य अहमेवंविधोऽर्जुन ।
ज्ञातुं द्रष्टुं च तत्त्वेन प्रवेष्टुं च परन्तप ॥
translation_en: But by exclusive devotion, O Arjuna, I can be known and seen in this form and entered into in truth, O scorcher of foes.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मत्कर्मकृन्मत्परमो मद्भक्तः सङ्गवर्जितः ।
निर्वैरः सर्वभूतेषु यः स मामेति पाण्डव ॥
translation_en: Whoever does work for Me, regards Me as the highest, is devoted to Me, and is free from attachment, free from enmity toward all beings, O Pāṇḍava, comes to Me.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एवं सततयुक्ता ये भक्तास्त्वां पर्युपासते ।
ये चाप्यक्षरमव्यक्तं तेषां के योगवित्तमाः ॥
translation_en: Arjuna said: Those devotees who, being ever steadfast, worship You, and those who also worship the imperishable, the unmanifest—among them, who are the more accomplished in yoga?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मय्यावेश्य मनो ये मां नित्ययुक्ता उपासते ।
श्रद्धया परयोपेताः ते मे युक्ततमा मताः ॥
translation_en: Those who, with minds fixed on Me, ever steadfast, worship Me with supreme faith—such are regarded by Me as the most steadfast in yoga.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ये त्वक्षरमनिर्देश्यमव्यक्तं पर्युपासते ।
सर्वत्रगमचिन्त्यञ्च कूटस्थमचलन्ध्रुवम् ॥
translation_en: But those who worship the imperishable, the indefinable, the unmanifest, all-pervading, unthinkable, unchanging, and immovable, the constant, the steadfast, are devoted to Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सन्नियम्येन्द्रियग्रामं सर्वत्र समबुद्धयः ।
ते प्राप्नुवन्ति मामेव सर्वभूतहिते रताः ॥
translation_en: Having restrained the whole group of senses, and being equal-minded everywhere, those devoted to the welfare of all beings attain Me alone.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
क्लेशोऽधिकतरस्तेषामव्यक्तासक्तचेतसाम् ।
अव्यक्ता हि गतिर्दुःखं देहवद्भिरवाप्यते ॥
translation_en: For those whose minds are attached to the unmanifest, the trouble is greater; for the path of the unmanifest is hard to attain by those who are embodied.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ये तु सर्वाणि कर्माणि मयि संन्यस्य मत्पराः ।
अनन्येनैव योगेन मां ध्यायन्त उपासते ॥
translation_en: But those who, renouncing all actions in Me, are devoted to Me alone, meditate upon Me and worship Me through undivided yoga.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तेषामहं समुद्धर्ता मृत्युसंसारसागरात् ।
भवामि नचिरात्पार्थ मय्यावेशितचेतसाम् ॥
translation_en: For those whose minds are fixed on Me, O Pārtha, I soon become their deliverer from the ocean of saṃsāra and death.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मय्येव मन आधत्स्व मयि बुद्धिं निवेशय ।
निवसिष्यसि मय्येव अत ऊर्ध्वं न संशयः ॥
translation_en:
Fix your mind on Me alone; place your understanding in Me.  
Thereafter you will dwell in Me alone; of this there is no doubt.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अथ चित्तं समाधातुं न शक्नोषि मयि स्थिरम् ।
अभ्यासयोगेन ततो मामिच्छाप्तुं धनञ्जय ॥
translation_en: If you are unable to fix your mind steadily on Me, then, O Dhananjaya, seek to attain Me by means of practice-yoga.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अभ्यासेऽप्यसमर्थोऽसि मत्कर्मपरमो भव ।
मदर्थमपि कर्माणि कुर्वन्सिद्धिमवाप्स्यसि ॥
translation_en: If you are unable even to practice this, then be devoted to working for Me. By performing actions for My sake, you will attain perfection.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अथैतदप्यशक्तोऽसि कर्तुं मद्योगमाश्रितः ।
सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं ततः कुरु यतात्मवान् ॥
translation_en: If you are unable even to do this, taking refuge in My yoga, then, self-controlled, renounce the fruits of all actions.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
श्रेयो हि ज्ञानमभ्यासाज्ज्ञानाद्ध्यानं विशिष्यते ।
ध्यानात्कर्मफलत्यागस्त्यागाच्छान्तिरनन्तरम् ॥
translation_en: For practice, knowledge is indeed better; than knowledge, meditation is superior. From meditation comes renunciation of the fruits of action, and from renunciation, peace follows at once.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अद्वेष्टा सर्वभूतानां मैत्रः करुण एव च ।
निर्ममो निरहङ्कारः समदुःखसुखः क्षमी ॥
translation_en: One who hates no being, who is friendly and compassionate, free from possessiveness and egoism, even-minded in sorrow and joy, and forgiving.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सन्तुष्टः सततं योगी यतात्मा दृढनिश्चयः ।
मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः ॥
translation_en: Content and self-controlled, the yogi, firm in resolve, whose mind and intellect are offered to Me—he who is My devotee is dear to Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यस्मान्नोद्विजते लोको लोकान्नोद्विजते च यः ।
हर्षामर्षभयोद्वेगैर्मुक्तो यः स च मे प्रियः ॥
translation_en: The one from whom the world does not shrink, and who does not shrink from the world, and who is free from joy, anger, fear, and agitation—he is dear to Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनपेक्षः शुचिर्दक्ष उदासीनो गतव्यथः ।
सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः ॥
translation_en: Free from attachment, pure, skillful, impartial, and untroubled, one who has renounced all undertakings and is devoted to Me is dear to Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यो न हृष्यति न द्वेष्टि न शोचति न काङ्क्षति ।
शुभाशुभपरित्यागी भक्तिमान्यः स मे प्रियः ॥
translation_en: One who does not rejoice, does not hate, does not grieve, and does not desire; who renounces both the auspicious and the inauspicious—such a devotee is dear to Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
समः शत्रौ च मित्रे च तथा मानापमानयोः ।
शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु समः सङ्गविवर्जितः ॥
translation_en: Equal toward foe and friend alike, and also in honor and dishonor; equal in cold and heat, pleasure and pain, and free from attachment.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तुल्यनिन्दास्तुतिर्मौनी सन्तुष्टो येन केनचित् ।
अनिकेतः स्थिरमतिर्भक्तिमान्मे प्रियो नरः ॥
translation_en: One who is equal to blame and praise, silent, content with whatever comes, homeless, steady-minded, and devoted to Me is dear to Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ये तु धर्म्यामृतमिदं यथोक्तं पर्युपासते ।
श्रद्दधाना मत्परमा भक्तास्तेऽतीव मे प्रियाः ॥
translation_en: But those devotees who, full of faith, worship this dharma, this nectar, as described above, with Me as their supreme goal—those are exceedingly dear to Me.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव क्षेत्रं क्षेत्रज्ञमेव च ।
एतद्वेदितुमिच्छामि ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं च केशव ॥
translation_en: I wish to know prakriti and purusha, as well as the field and the knower of the field, and also knowledge and what is to be known, O Keśava.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इदं शरीरं कौन्तेय क्षेत्रमित्यभिधीयते ।
एतद्यो वेत्ति तं प्राहुः क्षेत्रज्ञ इति तद्विदः ॥
translation_en: This body, O Kaunteya, is called the field; and one who knows it is called the knower of the field by those who know this.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
क्षेत्रज्ञं चापि मां विद्धि सर्वक्षेत्रेषु भारत ।
क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोर्ज्ञानं यत्तज्ज्ञानं मतं मम ॥
translation_en:
And know Me also, O Bharata, as the knower of the field in all fields.  
That knowledge of the field and of the knower of the field is what I regard as true knowledge.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तत्क्षेत्रं यच्च यादृक्च यद्विकारि यतश्च यत् ।
स च यो यत्प्रभावश्च तत्समासेन मे श‍ृणु ॥
translation_en: That field, and what it is like, and its changes, and from where, and also who he is and what his power is—hear this from me in brief.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ऋषिभिर्बहुधा गीतं छन्दोभिर्विविधैः पृथक् ।
ब्रह्मसूत्रपदैश्चैव हेतुमद्भिर्विनिश्चितैः ॥
translation_en: It has been sung in many ways by the sages, in various metrical forms, and also in the definitive aphorisms of the Brahma-sūtra.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
महाभूतान्यहङ्कारो बुद्धिरव्यक्तमेव च ।
इन्द्रियाणि दशैकं च पञ्च चेन्द्रियगोचराः ॥
translation_en: The great elements, ego, intellect, and also the unmanifest; the ten senses, the one, and the five sense-objects.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इच्छा द्वेषः सुखं दुःखं सङ्घातश्चेतना धृतिः ।
एतत्क्षेत्रं समासेन सविकारमुदाहृतम् ॥
translation_en:
Desire, aversion, pleasure, pain, the aggregate, consciousness, and resolve—  
this field, together with its modifications, has been briefly described.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अमानित्वमदम्भित्वमहिंसा क्षान्तिरार्जवम् ।
आचार्योपासनं शौचं स्थैर्यमात्मविनिग्रहः ॥
translation_en:
Humility, absence of ostentation, nonviolence, forbearance, straightforwardness,  
service to the ācārya, purity, steadfastness, and self-restraint.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इन्द्रियार्थेषु वैराग्यमनहङ्कार एव च ।
जन्ममृत्युजराव्याधिदुःखदोषानुदर्शनम् ॥
translation_en:
Detachment from sense objects, and also absence of egoism;
and perception of the faults of birth, death, old age, disease, and suffering.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
असक्तिरनभिष्वङ्गः पुत्रदारगृहादिषु ।
नित्यं च समचित्तत्वमिष्टानिष्टोपपत्तिषु ॥
translation_en: Non-attachment and absence of possessiveness toward sons, wives, homes, and the like; and constant evenness of mind in meeting the agreeable and the disagreeable.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मयि चानन्ययोगेन भक्तिरव्यभिचारिणी ।
विविक्तदेशसेवित्वमरतिर्जनसंसदि ॥
translation_en: Unwavering bhakti to Me by unswerving yoga, and a liking for solitude in secluded places, with no delight in the company of people.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अध्यात्मज्ञाननित्यत्वं तत्त्वज्ञानार्थदर्शनम् ।
एतज्ज्ञानमिति प्रोक्तमज्ञानं यदतोऽन्यथा ॥
translation_en: Constancy in knowledge of the Self, and insight into the meaning of true knowledge—this is declared to be knowledge; whatever is otherwise is ignorance.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ज्ञेयं यत्तत्प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वामृतमश्नुते ।
अनादिमत्परं ब्रह्म न सत्तन्नासदुच्यते ॥
translation_en: I will declare that which is to be known; knowing it, one attains immortality: the beginningless supreme Brahman, which is said to be neither being nor non-being.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वतः पाणिपादं तत्सर्वतोऽक्षिशिरोमुखम् ।
सर्वतः श्रुतिमल्लोके सर्वमावृत्य तिष्ठति ॥
translation_en: With hands and feet everywhere, with eyes, heads, and faces everywhere, with ears everywhere in the world, it remains enveloping all things.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वेन्द्रियगुणाभासं सर्वेन्द्रियविवर्जितम् ।
असक्तं सर्वभृच्चैव निर्गुणं गुणभोक्तृ च ॥
translation_en: Shining with the qualities of all the senses, yet devoid of all the senses; unattached, yet sustaining all; beyond the gunas, yet the enjoyer of the gunas.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
बहिरन्तश्च भूतानामचरं चरमेव च ।
सूक्ष्मत्वात्तदविज्ञेयं दूरस्थं चान्तिके च तत् ॥
translation_en: It is outside and inside all beings; it is unmoving and moving as well. Because of its subtlety, it is not knowable; it is far away and also near.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अविभक्तं च भूतेषु विभक्तमिव च स्थितम् ।
भूतभर्तृ च तज्ज्ञेयं ग्रसिष्णु प्रभविष्णु च ॥
translation_en: Undivided, yet appearing as though divided among beings, it should be understood as the sustainer of beings, the devourer, and the source of their arising.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ज्योतिषामपि तज्ज्योतिस्तमसः परमुच्यते ।
ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं ज्ञानगम्यं हृदि सर्वस्य विष्ठितम् ॥
translation_en: Among lights, that is the light said to be beyond darkness. It is knowledge, the object of knowledge, attainable through knowledge, and abiding in the heart of all.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इति क्षेत्रं तथा ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं चोक्तं समासतः ।
मद्भक्त एतद्विज्ञाय मद्भावायोपपद्यते ॥
translation_en:
Thus the field, knowledge, and the knowable have been briefly stated.  
My devotee, having understood this, becomes fit for My being.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव विद्ध्यनादी उभावपि ।
विकारांश्च गुणांश्चैव विद्धि प्रकृतिसम्भवान् ॥
translation_en: Know prakriti and purusha as both without beginning; and know also the modifications and the gunas as born of prakriti.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कार्यकारणकर्तृत्वे हेतुः प्रकृतिरुच्यते ।
पुरुषः सुखदुःखानां भोक्तृत्वे हेतुरुच्यते ॥
translation_en:
Prakriti is said to be the cause in the making of cause and effect;
purusha is said to be the cause of the experiencing of सुख and duhkha.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पुरुषः प्रकृतिस्थो हि भुङ्क्ते प्रकृतिजान्गुणान् ।
कारणं गुणसङ्गोऽस्य सदसद्योनिजन्मसु ॥
translation_en: The purusha, abiding in prakriti, indeed experiences the gunas born of prakriti. Attachment to the gunas is the cause of his birth in good and evil wombs.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
उपद्रष्टानुमन्ता च भर्ता भोक्ता महेश्वरः ।
परमात्मेति चाप्युक्तो देहेऽस्मिन्पुरुषः परः ॥
translation_en: The purusha in this body is also called the witness, the permitter, the supporter, the enjoyer, and the great Lord; he is likewise spoken of as the supreme Atman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
य एवं वेत्ति पुरुषं प्रकृतिं च गुणैः सह ।
सर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि न स भूयोऽभिजायते ॥
translation_en: He who thus knows purusha and prakriti together with the gunas, in whatever condition he may live, is not born again.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ध्यानेनात्मनि पश्यन्ति केचिदात्मानमात्मना ।
अन्ये साङ्ख्येन योगेन कर्मयोगेन चापरे ॥
translation_en: Some behold the Self in the Self by means of meditation; others by Sāṅkhya-yoga, and still others by karma-yoga.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अन्ये त्वेवमजानन्तः श्रुत्वान्येभ्य उपासते ।
तेऽपि चातितरन्त्येव मृत्युं श्रुतिपरायणाः ॥
translation_en: Others, not knowing this, worship after hearing of it from others. Even they, devoted to hearing, surely cross beyond death.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यावत्सञ्जायते किञ्चित्सत्त्वं स्थावरजङ्गमम् ।
क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञसंयोगात्तद्विद्धि भरतर्षभ ॥
translation_en: Whatever living entity is born, whether moving or unmoving, know that, O best of the Bharatas, as arising from the conjunction of kshetra and kshetrajna.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
समं सर्वेषु भूतेषु तिष्ठन्तं परमेश्वरम् ।
विनश्यत्स्वविनश्यन्तं यः पश्यति स पश्यति ॥
translation_en: He who sees the Supreme Lord as equally dwelling in all beings, the imperishable within the perishing, he truly sees.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
समं पश्यन्हि सर्वत्र समवस्थितमीश्वरम् ।
न हिनस्त्यात्मनात्मानं ततो याति परां गतिम् ॥
translation_en: Seeing the Lord equally present everywhere, one does not harm oneself by the self; thus one attains the supreme goal.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रकृत्यैव च कर्माणि क्रियमाणानि सर्वशः ।
यः पश्यति तथात्मानमकर्तारं स पश्यति ॥
translation_en: The one who sees that all actions are being performed in every way by prakriti alone, and likewise sees the Atman as not the doer, truly sees.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदा भूतपृथग्भावमेकस्थमनुपश्यति ।
तत एव च विस्तारं ब्रह्म सम्पद्यते तदा ॥
translation_en: When one sees the manifold distinctness of beings abiding in the One, and also their expansion from that very source, then one attains Brahman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनादित्वान्निर्गुणत्वात्परमात्मायमव्ययः ।
शरीरस्थोऽपि कौन्तेय न करोति न लिप्यते ॥
translation_en: Because it is without beginning and without gunas, this imperishable Supreme Self, though abiding in the body, O Kaunteya, neither acts nor is tainted.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यथा सर्वगतं सौक्ष्म्यादाकाशं नोपलिप्यते ।
सर्वत्रावस्थितो देहे तथात्मा नोपलिप्यते ॥
translation_en: Just as all-pervading space is not tainted by its subtlety, so the Atman, abiding everywhere in the body, is not tainted.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यथा प्रकाशयत्येकः कृत्स्नं लोकमिमं रविः ।
क्षेत्रं क्षेत्री तथा कृत्स्नं प्रकाशयति भारत ॥
translation_en: Just as the one sun illumines this entire world, so too, O Bharata, the Kshetri illumines the whole Kshetra.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोरेवमन्तरं ज्ञानचक्षुषा ।
भूतप्रकृतिमोक्षं च ये विदुर्यान्ति ते परम् ॥
translation_en: Those who thus discern, with the eye of knowledge, the distinction between kshetra and kshetrajna, and the release of beings from prakriti, attain the Supreme.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
परं भूयः प्रवक्ष्यामि ज्ञानानां ज्ञानमुत्तमम् ।
यज्ज्ञात्वा मुनयः सर्वे परां सिद्धिमितो गताः ॥
translation_en: I will speak again of the highest wisdom, the best among all knowledges; having known this, all the sages from here have attained supreme perfection.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इदं ज्ञानमुपाश्रित्य मम साधर्म्यमागताः ।
सर्गेऽपि नोपजायन्ते प्रलये न व्यथन्ति च ॥
translation_en: Taking refuge in this knowledge, they attain a nature like Mine; at creation they are not born, and at dissolution they are not troubled.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मम योनिर्महद् ब्रह्म तस्मिन्गर्भं दधाम्यहम् ।
सम्भवः सर्वभूतानां ततो भवति भारत ॥
translation_en:
My womb is the great Brahman; in that I place the seed.  
From that, O Bharata, comes the birth of all beings.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वयोनिषु कौन्तेय मूर्तयः सम्भवन्ति याः ।
तासां ब्रह्म महद्योनिरहं बीजप्रदः पिता ॥
translation_en: O son of Kunti, whatever forms arise in all wombs, the great Brahman is their womb, and I am the father who gives the seed.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सत्त्वं रजस्तम इति गुणाः प्रकृतिसम्भवाः ।
निबध्नन्ति महाबाहो देहे देहिनमव्ययम् ॥
translation_en: Sattva, rajas, and tamas—the gunas born of prakriti—bind the imperishable embodied being in the body, O mighty-armed one.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तत्र सत्त्वं निर्मलत्वात्प्रकाशकमनामयम् ।
सुखसङ्गेन बध्नाति ज्ञानसङ्गेन चानघ ॥
translation_en: There, sattva, being pure, is luminous and free from illness; O sinless one, it binds by attachment to happiness and by attachment to knowledge.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
रजो रागात्मकं विद्धि तृष्णासङ्गसमुद्भवम् ।
तन्निबध्नाति कौन्तेय कर्मसङ्गेन देहिनम् ॥
translation_en: Know rajas, O Kaunteya, as of the nature of passion, born of thirst and attachment; it binds the embodied being through attachment to action.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तमस्त्वज्ञानजं विद्धि मोहनं सर्वदेहिनाम् ।
प्रमादालस्यनिद्राभिस्तन्निबध्नाति भारत ॥
translation_en:
Know tamas to be born of ignorance, deluding all embodied beings.  
It binds them through heedlessness, laziness, and sleep, O Bharata.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सत्त्वं सुखे सञ्जयति रजः कर्मणि भारत ।
ज्ञानमावृत्य तु तमः प्रमादे सञ्जयत्युत ॥
translation_en: Sattva binds one to happiness, rajas to action, O Bharata; but tamas, covering knowledge, binds one to heedlessness.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
रजस्तमश्चाभिभूय सत्त्वं भवति भारत ।
रजः सत्त्वं तमश्चैव तमः सत्त्वं रजस्तथा ॥
translation_en: O Bharata, sattva prevails, overpowering rajas and tamas; rajas overpowers sattva and tamas, and likewise tamas overpowers sattva and rajas.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वद्वारेषु देहेऽस्मिन्प्रकाश उपजायते ।
ज्ञानं यदा तदा विद्याद्विवृद्धं सत्त्वमित्युत ॥
translation_en: When light arises through all the openings in this body, and knowledge grows, then one should know that sattva has become predominant.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
लोभः प्रवृत्तिरारम्भः कर्मणामशमः स्पृहा ।
रजस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे भरतर्षभ ॥
translation_en: Greed, activity, the undertaking of actions, unrest, and desire—these are born, O best of the Bhāratas, when rajas has increased.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अप्रकाशोऽप्रवृत्तिश्च प्रमादो मोह एव च ।
तमस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे कुरुनन्दन ॥
translation_en: Absence of light, inactivity, heedlessness, and delusion—these are born when tamas increases, O joy of the Kurus.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदा सत्त्वे प्रवृद्धे तु प्रलयं याति देहभृत् ।
तदोत्तमविदां लोकानमलान्प्रतिपद्यते ॥
translation_en: When, with sattva predominant, the embodied being passes away, then he attains the pure worlds of those who know the highest.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
रजसि प्रलयं गत्वा कर्मसङ्गिषु जायते ।
तथा प्रलीनस्तमसि मूढयोनिषु जायते ॥
translation_en:
Going to dissolution in rajas, he is born among those attached to action.  
Likewise, having dissolved in tamas, he is born in deluded wombs.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कर्मणः सुकृतस्याहुः सात्त्विकं निर्मलं फलम् ।
रजसस्तु फलं दुःखमज्ञानं तमसः फलम् ॥
translation_en: They say the stainless result of good action is sāttvika; but the fruit of rajas is suffering, and the fruit of tamas is ignorance.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सत्त्वात्सञ्जायते ज्ञानं रजसो लोभ एव च ।
प्रमादमोहौ तमसो भवतोऽज्ञानमेव च ॥
translation_en: From sattva arises knowledge; from rajas, greed. From tamas arise heedlessness, delusion, and ignorance.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ऊर्ध्वं गच्छन्ति सत्त्वस्था मध्ये तिष्ठन्ति राजसाः ।
जघन्यगुणवृत्तिस्था अधो गच्छन्ति तामसाः ॥
translation_en: Those established in sattva go upward; those in rajas remain in the middle; those fixed in the lower quality of tamas go downward.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नान्यं गुणेभ्यः कर्तारं यदा द्रष्टानुपश्यति ।
गुणेभ्यश्च परं वेत्ति मद्भावं सोऽधिगच्छति ॥
translation_en: When the seer perceives that there is no agent other than the gunas, and knows Me as beyond the gunas, he attains My state.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
गुणानेतानतीत्य त्रीन्देही देहसमुद्भवान् ।
जन्ममृत्युजरादुःखैर्विमुक्तोऽमृतमश्नुते ॥
translation_en: Having transcended these three gunas, the embodied one, arising from the body, is freed from birth, death, old age, and sorrow, and attains immortality.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कैर्लिङ्गैस्त्रीन्गुणानेतानतीतो भवति प्रभो ।
किमाचारः कथं चैतांस्त्रीन्गुणानतिवर्तते ॥
translation_en: By what signs does one who has gone beyond these three gunas become recognized, O Lord? What is his conduct, and how does he pass beyond these three gunas?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रकाशं च प्रवृत्तिं च मोहमेव च पाण्डव ।
न द्वेष्टि सम्प्रवृत्तानि न निवृत्तानि काङ्क्षति ॥
translation_en: O Pāṇḍava, he does not hate illumination, activity, or delusion when they arise, nor does he long for them when they cease.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
उदासीनवदासीनो गुणैर्यो न विचाल्यते ।
गुणा वर्तन्त इत्येवं योऽवतिष्ठति नेङ्गते ॥
translation_en: Remaining like one indifferent, who is not shaken by the gunas, and who stands firm, thinking, “It is only the gunas that are acting,” does not stir.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
समदुःखसुखः स्वस्थः समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चनः ।
तुल्यप्रियाप्रियो धीरस्तुल्यनिन्दात्मसंस्तुतिः ॥
translation_en: The steadfast one is alike in pain and pleasure, established in the self, and regards clod, stone, and gold alike; to him, the pleasant and the unpleasant are the same, and he is the same in censure and praise.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मानापमानयोस्तुल्यस्तुल्यो मित्रारिपक्षयोः ।
सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी गुणातीतः स उच्यते ॥
translation_en: He who is alike in honor and dishonor, alike toward friends and enemies, and who has abandoned all undertakings is said to be beyond the gunas.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मां च योऽव्यभिचारेण भक्तियोगेन सेवते ।
स गुणान्समतीत्यैतान्ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते ॥
translation_en: And he who serves Me with unswerving bhakti-yoga transcends these gunas and is fit for Brahman-realization.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ब्रह्मणो हि प्रतिष्ठाहममृतस्याव्ययस्य च ।
शाश्वतस्य च धर्मस्य सुखस्यैकान्तिकस्य च ॥
translation_en: For I am the foundation of Brahman, of immortality and of imperishable dharma, and of eternal, unending bliss.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ऊर्ध्वमूलमधःशाखमश्वत्थं प्राहुरव्ययम् ।
