छन्दांसि यस्य पर्णानि यस्तं वेद स वेदवित् ॥
translation_en: They speak of the imperishable ashwatha tree with roots above and branches below; its leaves are the hymns of the Vedas. One who knows it knows the Vedas.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अधश्चोर्ध्वं प्रसृतास्तस्य शाखा
गुणप्रवृद्धा विषयप्रवालाः ।
अधश्च मूलान्यनुसन्ततानि
कर्मानुबन्धीनि मनुष्यलोके ॥
translation_en:
Its branches extend downward and upward, nourished by the gunas; the buds are objects of the senses.  
And below, its roots continue spreading, binding people through action in the human world.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न रूपमस्येह तथोपलभ्यते
नान्तो न चादिर्न च सम्प्रतिष्ठा ।
अश्वत्थमेनं सुविरूढमूलं
असङ्गशस्त्रेण दृढेन छित्त्वा ॥

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ततः पदं तत्परिमार्गितव्यं
यस्मिन्गता न निवर्तन्ति भूयः ।
तमेव चाद्यं पुरुषं प्रपद्ये ।
यतः प्रवृत्तिः प्रसृता पुराणी ॥
translation_en:
Then the state must be sought,  
from which, once gone, they do not return again.  
I seek refuge in that same primal Purusha,  
from whom this ancient activity has spread forth.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
निर्मानमोहा जितसङ्गदोषा
अध्यात्मनित्या विनिवृत्तकामाः ।
द्वन्द्वैर्विमुक्ताः सुखदुःखसंज्ञै-
र्गच्छन्त्यमूढाः पदमव्ययं तत् ॥
translation_en: Free from delusion and pride, having conquered the fault of attachment, ever devoted to the Self, having renounced desire, and freed from the pairs of opposites called pleasure and pain, the undeluded attain that imperishable state.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न तद्भासयते सूर्यो न शशाङ्को न पावकः ।
यद्गत्वा न निवर्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम ॥
translation_en:
Neither the sun, nor the moon, nor fire illumines that;
having gone there, they do not return—that is My supreme abode.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ममैवांशो जीवलोके जीवभूतः सनातनः ।
मनःषष्ठानीन्द्रियाणि प्रकृतिस्थानि कर्षति ॥
translation_en: An eternal portion of Me in the world of living beings becomes the jiva and draws the mind as the sixth, together with the senses, which abide in prakriti.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
शरीरं यदवाप्नोति यच्चाप्युत्क्रामतीश्वरः ।
गृहीत्वैतानि संयाति वायुर्गन्धानिवाशयात् ॥
translation_en: As the wind takes fragrances from their source and carries them away, so the Lord, taking these from the body, departs with them when He acquires a body and when He leaves it.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
श्रोत्रं चक्षुः स्पर्शनं च रसनं घ्राणमेव च ।
अधिष्ठाय मनश्चायं विषयानुपसेवते ॥
translation_en: Taking possession of hearing, sight, touch, taste, and smell, and also the mind, he experiences the objects of the senses.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
उत्क्रामन्तं स्थितं वापि भुञ्जानं वा गुणान्वितम् ।
विमूढा नानुपश्यन्ति पश्यन्ति ज्ञानचक्षुषः ॥
translation_en: The deluded do not see Him departing, abiding, or enjoying, while those with the eye of knowledge do see.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यतन्तो योगिनश्चैनं पश्यन्त्यात्मन्यवस्थितम् ।
यतन्तोऽप्यकृतात्मानो नैनं पश्यन्त्यचेतसः ॥
translation_en: Striving yogis see Him abiding in the self; those whose selves are untrained and who lack discernment do not see Him, even though they strive.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदादित्यगतं तेजो जगद्भासयतेऽखिलम् ।
यच्चन्द्रमसि यच्चाग्नौ तत्तेजो विद्धि मामकम् ॥
translation_en: Know that the splendor which, dwelling in the sun, illumines the whole world, and that which is in the moon and in fire—that splendor is Mine.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
गामाविश्य च भूतानि धारयाम्यहमोजसा ।
पुष्णामि चौषधीः सर्वाः सोमो भूत्वा रसात्मकः ॥
translation_en: Entering the earth, I sustain all beings by My power; becoming Soma, of the nature of sap, I nourish all herbs.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अहं वैश्वानरो भूत्वा प्राणिनां देहमाश्रितः ।
प्राणापानसमायुक्तः पचाम्यन्नं चतुर्विधम् ॥
translation_en: I, becoming the fire of digestion and abiding in the bodies of living beings, unite with prana and apana and digest the four kinds of food.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वस्य चाहं हृदि सन्निविष्टो
मत्तः स्मृतिर्ज्ञानमपोहनञ्च ।
वेदैश्च सर्वैरहमेव वेद्यो
वेदान्तकृद्वेदविदेव चाहम् ॥

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
द्वाविमौ पुरुषौ लोके क्षरश्चाक्षर एव च ।
क्षरः सर्वाणि भूतानि कूटस्थोऽक्षर उच्यते ॥
translation_en: There are two purushas in this world: the perishable and the imperishable. The perishable is all beings; the unchanging is called the imperishable.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
उत्तमः पुरुषस्त्वन्यः परमात्मेत्युदाहृतः ।
यो लोकत्रयमाविश्य बिभर्त्यव्यय ईश्वरः ॥
translation_en: The supreme Purusha is another, spoken of as the Paramatman: the imperishable Lord who enters the three worlds and sustains them.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यस्मात्क्षरमतीतोऽहमक्षरादपि चोत्तमः ।
अतोऽस्मि लोके वेदे च प्रथितः पुरुषोत्तमः ॥
translation_en: Because I transcend the perishable, and am even superior to the imperishable, I am therefore renowned in the world and in the Vedas as the supreme Purusha.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यो मामेवमसम्मूढो जानाति पुरुषोत्तमम् ।
स सर्वविद्भजति मां सर्वभावेन भारत ॥
translation_en: He who, being undeluded, thus knows Me as the Supreme Purusha, O Bharata, worships Me with all his being; he is all-knowing.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इति गुह्यतमं शास्त्रमिदमुक्तं मयानघ ।
एतद्बुद्ध्वा बुद्धिमान्स्यात्कृतकृत्यश्च भारत ॥
translation_en:
Thus I have taught you this most secret teaching, O sinless one.
Knowing this, one becomes wise and has accomplished what is to be done, O Bharata.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अभयं सत्त्वसंशुद्धिर्ज्ञानयोगव्यवस्थितिः ।
दानं दमश्च यज्ञश्च स्वाध्यायस्तप आर्जवम् ॥
translation_en:
Fearlessness, purity of sattva, steadfastness in knowledge and yoga,  
charity, self-control, sacrifice, study of the self, austerity, and straightforwardness.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अहिंसा सत्यमक्रोधस्त्यागः शान्तिरपैशुनम् ।
दया भूतेष्वलोलुप्त्वं मार्दवं ह्रीरचापलम् ॥
translation_en: Nonviolence, truthfulness, absence of anger, renunciation, tranquility, freedom from slander, compassion toward all beings, lack of greed, gentleness, modesty, and steadiness.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तेजः क्षमा धृतिः शौचमद्रोहो नातिमानिता ।
भवन्ति सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातस्य भारत ॥
translation_en: Radiance, forgiveness, steadfastness, purity, absence of malice, and absence of pride—these belong to one born with divine qualities, O Bharata.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
दम्भो दर्पोऽभिमानश्च क्रोधः पारुष्यमेव च ।
अज्ञानं चाभिजातस्य पार्थ सम्पदमासुरीम् ॥
translation_en: Hypocrisy, arrogance, conceit, anger, and harshness, as well as ignorance—these, O Pārtha, are the demoniac qualities of one who is born to such a nature.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
दैवी सम्पद्विमोक्षाय निबन्धायासुरी मता ।
मा शुचः सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातोऽसि पाण्डव ॥
translation_en: Divine qualities are said to lead to liberation, while the demonic lead to bondage. Do not grieve, O Pāṇḍava; you have been born with divine qualities.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
द्वौ भूतसर्गौ लोकेऽस्मिन्दैव आसुर एव च ।
दैवो विस्तरशः प्रोक्त आसुरं पार्थ मे श‍ृणु ॥
translation_en: In this world there are two kinds of beings: the divine and the demoniac. The divine has been described at length; now hear from me, O Pārtha, about the demoniac.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रवृत्तिं च निवृत्तिं च जना न विदुरासुराः ।
न शौचं नापि चाचारो न सत्यं तेषु विद्यते ॥
translation_en: The asuric do not know what should be undertaken and what should be renounced. In them there is neither purity, nor proper conduct, nor truth.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
असत्यमप्रतिष्ठं ते जगदाहुरनीश्वरम् ।
अपरस्परसम्भूतं किमन्यत्कामहैतुकम् ॥
translation_en: They say that the world is unreal, without basis, without a Lord, born of mutual union, and produced by desire alone—what else?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एतां दृष्टिमवष्टभ्य नष्टात्मानोऽल्पबुद्धयः ।
प्रभवन्त्युग्रकर्माणः क्षयाय जगतोऽहिताः ॥
translation_en: Holding to this view, these ruined souls, of little बुद्धि and violent deeds, arise as enemies, working for the destruction of the world.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
काममाश्रित्य दुष्पूरं दम्भमानमदान्विताः ।
मोहाद्गृहीत्वासद्ग्राहान्प्रवर्तन्तेऽशुचिव्रताः ॥
translation_en:
Relying on insatiable desire, filled with hypocrisy, pride, and arrogance,  
deluded, they seize upon false views and undertake impure vows.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
चिन्तामपरिमेयां च प्रलयान्तामुपाश्रिताः ।
कामोपभोगपरमा एतावदिति निश्चिताः ॥
translation_en: They take refuge in endless anxiety until death, and are devoted to the supreme enjoyment of desire, convinced that this alone is all there is.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आशापाशशतैर्बद्धाः कामक्रोधपरायणाः ।
ईहन्ते कामभोगार्थमन्यायेनार्थसञ्चयान् ॥
translation_en: Bound by hundreds of bonds of hope, devoted to desire and anger, they strive by unjust means to accumulate wealth for the sake of pleasure.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इदमद्य मया लब्धमिमं प्राप्स्ये मनोरथम् ।
इदमस्तीदमपि मे भविष्यति पुनर्धनम् ॥
translation_en: Today I have gained this; I shall obtain this desire of mine. This is mine now, and this wealth will be mine again.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
असौ मया हतः शत्रुर्हनिष्ये चापरानपि ।
ईश्वरोऽहमहं भोगी सिद्धोऽहं बलवान्सुखी ॥
translation_en: “I have slain that enemy, and I shall slay the others as well. I am the lord, I am the enjoyer, I am accomplished, I am strong, I am happy.”

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आढ्योऽभिजनवानस्मि कोऽन्योऽस्ति सदृशो मया ।
यक्ष्ये दास्यामि मोदिष्य इत्यज्ञानविमोहिताः ॥
translation_en:
“I am wealthy and of noble birth; who else is equal to me?”  
Deluded by ignorance, they think, “I will sacrifice, I will give, I will rejoice.”

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनेकचित्तविभ्रान्ता मोहजालसमावृताः ।
प्रसक्ताः कामभोगेषु पतन्ति नरकेऽशुचौ ॥
translation_en: Bewildered by many thoughts and enveloped in the net of delusion, attached to the enjoyment of desires, they fall into a foul hell.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आत्मसम्भाविताः स्तब्धा धनमानमदान्विताः ।
यजन्ते नामयज्ञैस्ते दम्भेनाविधिपूर्वकम् ॥
translation_en: Self-conceited, stubborn, and filled with the intoxication of wealth and pride, they perform sacrifices in name only, with ostentation and without regard for proper rules.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अहङ्कारं बलं दर्पं कामं क्रोधं च संश्रिताः ।
मामात्मपरदेहेषु प्रद्विषन्तोऽभ्यसूयकाः ॥
translation_en: Relying on ego, strength, arrogance, desire, and anger, they envy Me and hate Me in their own bodies and in those of others.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तानहं द्विषतः क्रूरान्संसारेषु नराधमान् ।
क्षिपाम्यजस्रमशुभानासुरीष्वेव योनिषु ॥
translation_en: Those hateful, cruel, and vile among men—those inauspicious ones—I cast again and again into demonic wombs in samsara.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आसुरीं योनिमापन्ना मूढा जन्मनि जन्मनि ।
मामप्राप्यैव कौन्तेय ततो यान्त्यधमां गतिम् ॥
translation_en: Those deluded ones, having fallen into asuric wombs again and again in birth after birth, O Kaunteya, never attaining Me, thus go to the lowest state.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
त्रिविधं नरकस्येदं द्वारं नाशनमात्मनः ।
कामः क्रोधस्तथा लोभस्तस्मादेतत्त्रयं त्यजेत् ॥
translation_en: There are three gates to hell, destructive of the self: lust, anger, and greed. Therefore one should give up these three.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एतैर्विमुक्तः कौन्तेय तमोद्वारैस्त्रिभिर्नरः ।
आचरत्यात्मनः श्रेयस्ततो याति परां गतिम् ॥
translation_en: O son of Kunti, the man who is freed from these three gates of darkness practices what is best for himself, and then attains the supreme goal.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यः शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य वर्तते कामकारतः ।
न स सिद्धिमवाप्नोति न सुखं न परां गतिम् ॥
translation_en: He who, setting aside the injunctions of the scriptures, acts according to desire alone attains neither perfection, nor happiness, nor the highest goal.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तस्माच्छास्त्रं प्रमाणं ते कार्याकार्यव्यवस्थितौ ।
ज्ञात्वा शास्त्रविधानोक्तं कर्म कर्तुमिहार्हसि ॥
translation_en: Therefore, the scripture is your authority in determining what should be done and what should not be done. Knowing what is taught by the scriptural injunction, you should here perform action.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ये शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य यजन्ते श्रद्धयान्विताः ।
तेषां निष्ठा तु का कृष्ण सत्त्वमाहो रजस्तमः ॥
translation_en: Those who, abandoning the injunctions of the scriptures, perform worship with faith—what is their basis, O Krishna: sattva, rajas, or tamas?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
त्रिविधा भवति श्रद्धा देहिनां सा स्वभावजा ।
सात्त्विकी राजसी चैव तामसी चेति तां श‍ृणु ॥
translation_en: Faith embodied beings possess is of three kinds, born of their own nature: sāttvika, rājasika, and tāmasika. Hear of them from me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सत्त्वानुरूपा सर्वस्य श्रद्धा भवति भारत ।
श्रद्धामयोऽयं पुरुषो यो यच्छ्रद्धः स एव सः ॥
translation_en: O Bharata, everyone’s faith is in accordance with their nature. A person is made of faith; whatever his faith is, that is what he is.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यजन्ते सात्त्विका देवान्यक्षरक्षांसि राजसाः ।
प्रेतान्भूतगणांश्चान्ये यजन्ते तामसा जनाः ॥
translation_en: Those in sattva worship the devas; those in rajas worship the yakshas and rakshasas; and others, who are in tamas, worship the departed and the hosts of spirits.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अशास्त्रविहितं घोरं तप्यन्ते ये तपो जनाः ।
दम्भाहङ्कारसंयुक्ताः कामरागबलान्विताः ॥
translation_en: Those who perform severe austerities not enjoined by scripture, being joined with ostentation and egoism, and driven by desire, attachment, and force.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कर्षयन्तः शरीरस्थं भूतग्राममचेतसः ।
मां चैवान्तःशरीरस्थं तान्विद्ध्यासुरनिश्चयान् ॥
translation_en: Those who, being devoid of discernment, torment the body’s collection of beings and Me too, dwelling within the body—know them to be of demonic resolve.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आहारस्त्वपि सर्वस्य त्रिविधो भवति प्रियः ।
यज्ञस्तपस्तथा दानं तेषां भेदमिमं श‍ृणु ॥
translation_en: Food, too, dear to all, is of three kinds. Hear this distinction among yajna, austerity, and charity.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आयुःसत्त्वबलारोग्यसुखप्रीतिविवर्धनाः ।
रस्याः स्निग्धाः स्थिरा हृद्या आहाराः सात्त्विकप्रियाः ॥
translation_en: Foods that are juicy, nourishing, stable, and pleasing to the heart are dear to those in sattva; they increase life, purity, strength, health, happiness, and delight.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कट्वम्ललवणात्युष्णतीक्ष्णरूक्षविदाहिनः ।
आहारा राजसस्येष्टा दुःखशोकामयप्रदाः ॥
translation_en: Bitter, sour, salty, excessively hot, pungent, dry, and burning foods are preferred by those of rajasic nature; they bring pain, sorrow, and disease.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यातयामं गतरसं पूति पर्युषितं च यत् ।
उच्छिष्टमपि चामेध्यं भोजनं तामसप्रियम् ॥
translation_en: Food that is stale, tasteless, foul-smelling, spoiled, and also leftovers, and impure—this is dear to tamas.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्यज्ञो विधिदृष्टो य इज्यते ।
यष्टव्यमेवेति मनः समाधाय स सात्त्विकः ॥
translation_en: That yajna which is offered in accordance with scriptural injunction by those who seek no reward, with the mind firmly resolved that it ought simply to be performed, is sattvic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अभिसन्धाय तु फलं दम्भार्थमपि चैव यत् ।
इज्यते भरतश्रेष्ठ तं यज्ञं विद्धि राजसम् ॥
translation_en: But whatever is offered with an aim for the fruit, and indeed for ostentation as well, know that sacrifice to be rajasic, O best of the Bharatas.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
विधिहीनमसृष्टान्नं मन्त्रहीनमदक्षिणम् ।
श्रद्धाविरहितं यज्ञं तामसं परिचक्षते ॥
translation_en: Sacrifice performed without regard to proper procedure, without distribution of food, without mantras, without gifts, and devoid of faith is said to be tamasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
देवद्विजगुरुप्राज्ञपूजनं शौचमार्जवम् ।
ब्रह्मचर्यमहिंसा च शारीरं तप उच्यते ॥
translation_en: Worship of the devas, brahmins, teachers, and the wise, purity, straightforwardness, celibacy, and nonviolence—these are said to be bodily austerity.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनुद्वेगकरं वाक्यं सत्यं प्रियहितं च यत् ।
स्वाध्यायाभ्यसनं चैव वाङ्मयं तप उच्यते ॥
translation_en: Speech that causes no agitation, is truthful, pleasant, and beneficial, and also the regular recitation and study of the Vedas—this is called austerity of speech.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मनः प्रसादः सौम्यत्वं मौनमात्मविनिग्रहः ।
भावसंशुद्धिरित्येतत्तपो मानसमुच्यते ॥
translation_en: Serenity of mind, gentleness, silence, and self-restraint: this is called mental austerity.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
श्रद्धया परया तप्तं तपस्तत्त्रिविधं नरैः ।
अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्युक्तैः सात्त्विकं परिचक्षते ॥
translation_en: The threefold austerity performed by men with supreme faith, by those who are steadfast and seek no reward, is called sattvic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सत्कारमानपूजार्थं तपो दम्भेन चैव यत् ।
क्रियते तदिह प्रोक्तं राजसं चलमध्रुवम् ॥
translation_en: Whatever austerity is performed here for the sake of honor, respect, and worship, and indeed with hypocrisy—that is said to be rajasic, unstable, and impermanent.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मूढग्राहेणात्मनो यत्पीडया क्रियते तपः ।
परस्योत्सादनार्थं वा तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥
translation_en: Austerity that is performed with foolish obstinacy, by self-torment, or for the purpose of harming another, is said to be tamasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
दातव्यमिति यद्दानं दीयतेऽनुपकारिणे ।
देशे काले च पात्रे च तद्दानं सात्त्विकं स्मृतम् ॥
translation_en: A gift given as a duty, without expectation of return, at the proper place and time, and to a worthy recipient, is regarded as sattvic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यत्तु प्रत्युपकारार्थं फलमुद्दिश्य वा पुनः ।
दीयते च परिक्लिष्टं तद्दानं राजसं स्मृतम् ॥
translation_en: But whatever is given with expectation of return, or with a desire for reward, and given grudgingly, that gift is regarded as rajasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अदेशकाले यद्दानमपात्रेभ्यश्च दीयते ।
असत्कृतमवज्ञातं तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥
translation_en: A gift given at the wrong place and time, and to unworthy recipients, given without respect and with contempt, is said to be tamasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ॐतत्सदिति निर्देशो ब्रह्मणस्त्रिविधः स्मृतः ।
ब्राह्मणास्तेन वेदाश्च यज्ञाश्च विहिताः पुरा ॥
translation_en: Om, Tat, Sat—this has been declared as the threefold designation of Brahman. By that the brāhmaṇas, the Vedas, and sacrifices were ordained in ancient times.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तस्मादोमित्युदाहृत्य यज्ञदानतपःक्रियाः ।
प्रवर्तन्ते विधानोक्ताः सततं ब्रह्मवादिनाम् ॥
translation_en: Therefore, the acts of sacrifice, gift-giving, and austerity, as prescribed by the injunctions, are always undertaken by the expounders of Brahman, uttering Om.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तदित्यनभिसन्धाय फलं यज्ञतपःक्रियाः ।
दानक्रियाश्च विविधाः क्रियन्ते मोक्षकाङ्क्षिभिः ॥
translation_en: Saying “tat,” without aiming at the fruit, acts of sacrifice, austerity, and various acts of giving are performed by those who desire liberation.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सद्भावे साधुभावे च सदित्येतत्प्रयुज्यते ।
प्रशस्ते कर्मणि तथा सच्छब्दः पार्थ युज्यते ॥
translation_en: The word sat is used in the sense of being and in the sense of goodness; and, O Partha, the word sat is likewise applied to a praiseworthy action.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यज्ञे तपसि दाने च स्थितिः सदिति चोच्यते ।
कर्म चैव तदर्थीयं सदित्येवाभिधीयते ॥
translation_en: Steadfastness in sacrifice, austerity, and charity is also called satt; and action performed for that purpose is likewise called satt.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अश्रद्धया हुतं दत्तं तपस्तप्तं कृतं च यत् ।
असदित्युच्यते पार्थ न च तत्प्रेत्य नो इह ॥
translation_en: Whatever is offered, given, or practiced in austerity without faith, O Pārtha, is called asat; it is neither here nor after death.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
संन्यासस्य महाबाहो तत्त्वमिच्छामि वेदितुम् ।
त्यागस्य च हृषीकेश पृथक्केशिनिषूदन ॥
translation_en: Mighty-armed one, I wish to know the true nature of renunciation and of tyāga separately, O Hṛṣīkeśa, slayer of Keśin.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
काम्यानां कर्मणां न्यासं संन्यासं कवयो विदुः ।
सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं प्राहुस्त्यागं विचक्षणाः ॥
translation_en: The wise understand renunciation to be the giving up of desire-motivated actions. The discerning declare that tyaga is the relinquishment of the fruits of all actions.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
त्याज्यं दोषवदित्येके कर्म प्राहुर्मनीषिणः ।
यज्ञदानतपःकर्म न त्याज्यमिति चापरे ॥
translation_en: Some sages say that action should be abandoned as defective; others say that the acts of sacrifice, charity, and austerity should not be abandoned.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
निश्चयं श‍ृणु मे तत्र त्यागे भरतसत्तम ।
त्यागो हि पुरुषव्याघ्र त्रिविधः सम्प्रकीर्तितः ॥
translation_en:
Listen from me now to the certainty regarding that renunciation, O best of the Bharatas.  
For renunciation, O tiger among men, is said to be of three kinds.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यज्ञदानतपःकर्म न त्याज्यं कार्यमेव तत् ।
यज्ञो दानं तपश्चैव पावनानि मनीषिणाम् ॥
translation_en: Sacrifice, charity, and austerity should not be abandoned; they must indeed be performed. For the wise, sacrifice, charity, and austerity are purifying acts.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एतान्यपि तु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा फलानि च ।
कर्तव्यानीति मे पार्थ निश्चितं मतमुत्तमम् ॥
translation_en: But even these actions should be performed, giving up attachment and the fruits of action. This is, O Partha, my firm and highest opinion.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नियतस्य तु संन्यासः कर्मणो नोपपद्यते ।
मोहात्तस्य परित्यागस्तामसः परिकीर्तितः ॥
translation_en: But renunciation of prescribed action is not proper; abandoning it out of delusion is said to be tamasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
दुःखमित्येव यत्कर्म कायक्लेशभयात्त्यजेत् ।
स कृत्वा राजसं त्यागं नैव त्यागफलं लभेत् ॥
translation_en: If one abandons action because it is painful, from fear of bodily strain, he performs rajasic renunciation and does not obtain the fruit of renunciation.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कार्यमित्येव यत्कर्म नियतं क्रियतेऽर्जुन ।
सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा फलं चैव स त्यागः सात्त्विको मतः ॥
translation_en: O Arjuna, that prescribed action which is performed simply because it ought to be done, abandoning attachment and also the fruit, is regarded as sattvic renunciation.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न द्वेष्ट्यकुशलं कर्म कुशले नानुषज्जते ।
त्यागी सत्त्वसमाविष्टो मेधावी छिन्नसंशयः ॥
translation_en: He does not hate unwholesome action, nor is he attached to wholesome action. The renunciant, filled with sattva, is wise, and his doubts are cut away.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न हि देहभृता शक्यं त्यक्तुं कर्माण्यशेषतः ।
यस्तु कर्मफलत्यागी स त्यागीत्यभिधीयते ॥
translation_en: For the embodied one, it is indeed not possible to renounce actions entirely. But one who renounces the fruits of action is called a renouncer.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनिष्टमिष्टं मिश्रं च त्रिविधं कर्मणः फलम् ।
भवत्यत्यागिनां प्रेत्य न तु संन्यासिनां क्वचित् ॥
translation_en: For those who have not renounced, after death there are three kinds of the fruit of action: undesirable, desirable, and mixed; but for those who have renounced, there is none at all.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पञ्चैतानि महाबाहो कारणानि निबोध मे ।
साङ्ख्ये कृतान्ते प्रोक्तानि सिद्धये सर्वकर्मणाम् ॥
translation_en: O mighty-armed one, learn from Me these five causes, as taught in Sāṅkhya for the accomplishment of all actions.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अधिष्ठानं तथा कर्ता करणं च पृथग्विधम् ।
विविधाश्च पृथक्चेष्टा दैवं चैवात्र पञ्चमम् ॥
translation_en: The basis, the agent, and the various instruments, and the many and different kinds of action, and also fate as the fifth.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
शरीरवाङ्मनोभिर्यत्कर्म प्रारभते नरः ।
न्याय्यं वा विपरीतं वा पञ्चैते तस्य हेतवः ॥
translation_en: Whatever action a person undertakes with the body, speech, and mind, whether right or wrong, these five are its causes.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तत्रैवं सति कर्तारमात्मानं केवलं तु यः ।
पश्यत्यकृतबुद्धित्वान्न स पश्यति दुर्मतिः ॥
translation_en: Therefore, in this matter, one who, because of undeveloped understanding, sees the Self alone as the sole doer—he, of confused mind, does not see.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यस्य नाहङ्कृतो भावो बुद्धिर्यस्य न लिप्यते ।
हत्वाऽपि स इमाँल्लोकान्न हन्ति न निबध्यते ॥
translation_en: One whose sense of ego is absent, whose intelligence is not tainted, even if he kills all these worlds, he does not kill, and is not bound.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं परिज्ञाता त्रिविधा कर्मचोदना ।
करणं कर्म कर्तेति त्रिविधः कर्मसङ्ग्रहः ॥
translation_en:
Knowledge, the knowable, and the knower—these are the threefold impulse to action.  
The doer, the act, and the instrument are the threefold basis of karma.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ज्ञानं कर्म च कर्ता च त्रिधैव गुणभेदतः ।
प्रोच्यते गुणसङ्ख्याने यथावच्छृणु तान्यपि ॥
translation_en: Knowledge, action, and the doer are likewise said, in the classification of the gunas, to be threefold according to the distinctions among the gunas. Listen also to them as they are.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वभूतेषु येनैकं भावमव्ययमीक्षते ।
अविभक्तं विभक्तेषु तज्ज्ञानं विद्धि सात्त्विकम् ॥
translation_en: Know that knowledge to be sattvic by which one sees the one imperishable reality in all beings, the undivided in the divided.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पृथक्त्वेन तु यज्ज्ञानं नानाभावान्पृथग्विधान् ।
वेत्ति सर्वेषु भूतेषु तज्ज्ञानं विद्धि राजसम् ॥
translation_en: But that knowledge by which one sees separate, diverse existences in all beings, know that knowledge to be rajasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यत्तु कृत्स्नवदेकस्मिन्कार्ये सक्तमहैतुकम् ।
अतत्त्वार्थवदल्पं च तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥
translation_en: But that which, clinging to a single task as if it were the whole, is groundless, and which is trivial and without regard for true purpose—that is said to be tamasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नियतं सङ्गरहितमरागद्वेषतः कृतम् ।
अफलप्रेप्सुना कर्म यत्तत्सात्त्विकमुच्यते ॥
translation_en: Action that is obligatory, done without attachment and without desire or aversion to results, is said to be sattvic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यत्तु कामेप्सुना कर्म साहङ्कारेण वा पुनः ।
क्रियते बहुलायासं तद्राजसमुदाहृतम् ॥
translation_en: But whatever action is performed by one who desires pleasure, or again by egotism, and is done with great effort, that is called rajas.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनुबन्धं क्षयं हिंसामनपेक्ष्य च पौरुषम् ।
मोहादारभ्यते कर्म यत्तत्तामसमुच्यते ॥
translation_en: Action that is undertaken out of delusion, without regard for consequence, loss, injury, or one’s own capacity, is said to be tamasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मुक्तसङ्गोऽनहंवादी धृत्युत्साहसमन्वितः ।
सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योर्निर्विकारः कर्ता सात्त्विक उच्यते ॥
translation_en: Free from attachment, free from egoism, endowed with firmness and enthusiasm, and unchanged in success and failure, such a doer is said to be sattvic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
रागी कर्मफलप्रेप्सुर्लुब्धो हिंसात्मकोऽशुचिः ।
हर्षशोकान्वितः कर्ता राजसः परिकीर्तितः ॥
translation_en: Passionate, seeking the fruits of action, greedy, violent, and impure, elated and sorrowful, such a doer is said to be rajasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अयुक्तः प्राकृतः स्तब्धः शठो नैष्कृतिकोऽलसः ।
विषादी दीर्घसूत्री च कर्ता तामस उच्यते ॥
translation_en: Undisciplined, crude, obstinate, deceitful, dishonest, lazy, despondent, and procrastinating: such a doer is said to be tamasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
बुद्धेर्भेदं धृतेश्चैव गुणतस्त्रिविधं श‍ृणु ।
प्रोच्यमानमशेषेण पृथक्त्वेन धनञ्जय ॥
translation_en: Hear now, O Dhananjaya, in full and distinctly, the threefold distinction of buddhi and dhriti according to the gunas.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रवृत्तिं च निवृत्तिं च कार्याकार्ये भयाभये ।
बन्धं मोक्षं च या वेत्ति बुद्धिः सा पार्थ सात्त्विकी ॥
translation_en: And, O Partha, that intellect is sattvic which understands engagement and renunciation, what should be done and what should not be done, fear and fearlessness, bondage and liberation.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यया धर्ममधर्मं च कार्यं चाकार्यमेव च ।
अयथावत्प्रजानाति बुद्धिः सा पार्थ राजसी ॥
translation_en: That intellect by which one does not truly know dharma and adharma, and what should be done and what should not be done—that intellect, O Partha, is rajasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अधर्मं धर्ममिति या मन्यते तमसावृता ।
सर्वार्थान्विपरीतांश्च बुद्धिः सा पार्थ तामसी ॥
translation_en: That intellect, O Partha, which is veiled by tamas, mistakes adharma for dharma and regards all things in contrary ways, is tamasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
धृत्या यया धारयते मनःप्राणेन्द्रियक्रियाः ।
योगेनाव्यभिचारिण्या धृतिः सा पार्थ सात्त्विकी ॥
translation_en: The steadfastness by which one sustains the activities of the mind, the life-force, and the senses through unwavering yoga—that steadfastness, O Partha, is sattvic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यया तु धर्मकामार्थान्धृत्या धारयतेऽर्जुन ।
प्रसङ्गेन फलाकाङ्क्षी धृतिः सा पार्थ राजसी ॥
translation_en: But that firmness, O Arjuna, by which one upholds dharma, kama, and artha with attachment to results, O Partha, is rajasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यया स्वप्नं भयं शोकं विषादं मदमेव च ।
न विमुञ्चति दुर्मेधा धृतिः सा पार्थ तामसी ॥
translation_en: The steadfastness by which the dull-minded does not abandon sleep, fear, sorrow, dejection, and also delusion, O Partha, that steadfastness is tamasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सुखं त्विदानीं त्रिविधं श‍ृणु मे भरतर्षभ ।
अभ्यासाद्रमते यत्र दुःखान्तं च निगच्छति ॥
translation_en: Now hear from me, O best of the Bharatas, the threefold happiness: that in which one takes delight through practice and comes to the end of suffering.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यत्तदग्रे विषमिव परिणामेऽमृतोपमम् ।
तत्सुखं सात्त्विकं प्रोक्तमात्मबुद्धिप्रसादजम् ॥
translation_en: That pleasure which at first is like poison but in the end is like nectar is said to be sattvic, born of the clarity of self-understanding.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
विषयेन्द्रियसंयोगाद्यत्तदग्रेऽमृतोपमम् ।
परिणामे विषमिव तत्सुखं राजसं स्मृतम् ॥
translation_en: That pleasure which arises from the contact of the senses with their objects, appearing like nectar at first, but like poison in the end, is remembered as rajasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदग्रे चानुबन्धे च सुखं मोहनमात्मनः ।
निद्रालस्यप्रमादोत्थं तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥
translation_en: That happiness which, at first and in the end, deludes the self, and which arises from sleep, laziness, and negligence—that is said to be tamasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न तदस्ति पृथिव्यां वा दिवि देवेषु वा पुनः ।
सत्त्वं प्रकृतिजैर्मुक्तं यदेभिः स्यात्त्रिभिर्गुणैः ॥
translation_en: There is nothing on earth, or in heaven among the devas, that is free from these three gunas born of prakriti.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ब्राह्मणक्षत्रियविशां शूद्राणां च परन्तप ।
कर्माणि प्रविभक्तानि स्वभावप्रभवैर्गुणैः ॥
translation_en: O scorcher of enemies, the duties of the brāhmaṇas, kshatriyas, vaishyas, and śūdras are divided according to the guṇas born of their own nature.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
शमो दमस्तपः शौचं क्षान्तिरार्जवमेव च ।
ज्ञानं विज्ञानमास्तिक्यं ब्रह्मकर्म स्वभावजम् ॥
translation_en:
Tranquility, self-restraint, austerity, purity, forbearance, and straightforwardness,  
knowledge, realization, faith, and the duties proper to a Brahmin, born of one’s own nature.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
शौर्यं तेजो धृतिर्दाक्ष्यं युद्धे चाप्यपलायनम् ।
दानमीश्वरभावश्च क्षात्रं कर्म स्वभावजम् ॥
translation_en: Valor, splendor, steadfastness, dexterity, and not fleeing in battle, generosity, and lordly bearing—these are the natural duties of a kshatriya.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कृषिगौरक्ष्यवाणिज्यं वैश्यकर्म स्वभावजम् ।
परिचर्यात्मकं कर्म शूद्रस्यापि स्वभावजम् ॥
translation_en: Agriculture, cattle-rearing, and trade are the natural duties of the vaisya; and service is the natural duty even of the shudra.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
स्वे स्वे कर्मण्यभिरतः संसिद्धिं लभते नरः ।
स्वकर्मनिरतः सिद्धिं यथा विन्दति तच्छृणु ॥
translation_en: A person, devoted to his own duty, attains perfection. Hear now how one devoted to his own duty attains success.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यतः प्रवृत्तिर्भूतानां येन सर्वमिदं ततम् ।
स्वकर्मणा तमभ्यर्च्य सिद्धिं विन्दति मानवः ॥
translation_en: From whom the movement of all beings has arisen, and by whom all this is pervaded, by worshiping Him through one’s own action, a human being attains perfection.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
श्रेयान्स्वधर्मो विगुणः परधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात् ।
स्वभावनियतं कर्म कुर्वन्नाप्नोति किल्बिषम् ॥
translation_en: Better is one’s own dharma, though imperfect, than another’s dharma well performed. By doing the karma ordained by one’s own nature, one incurs no sin.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सहजं कर्म कौन्तेय सदोषमपि न त्यजेत् ।
सर्वारम्भा हि दोषेण धूमेनाग्निरिवावृताः ॥
translation_en:
O son of Kunti, one should not abandon the action born of one’s nature, even if it has faults.  
For all undertakings are covered by defect, as fire is by smoke.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
असक्तबुद्धिः सर्वत्र जितात्मा विगतस्पृहः ।
नैष्कर्म्यसिद्धिं परमां संन्यासेनाधिगच्छति ॥
translation_en: One whose understanding is unattached everywhere, who has subdued the self, and who is free from longing, attains the highest perfection of non-action through renunciation.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सिद्धिं प्राप्तो यथा ब्रह्म तथाप्नोति निबोध मे ।
समासेनैव कौन्तेय निष्ठा ज्ञानस्य या परा ॥
translation_en: Listen from me, O Kaunteya, how one who has attained siddhi likewise attains Brahman, in brief—the supreme consummation of knowledge.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
बुद्ध्या विशुद्धया युक्तो धृत्यात्मानं नियम्य च ।
शब्दादीन्विषयांस्त्यक्त्वा रागद्वेषौ व्युदस्य च ॥
translation_en: Endowed with purified intellect and restraining the self with steadfastness, having cast aside sense objects such as sound, and having given up attachment and aversion,

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
विविक्तसेवी लघ्वाशी यतवाक्कायमानसः ।
ध्यानयोगपरो नित्यं वैराग्यं समुपाश्रितः ॥
translation_en: One who dwells in solitude, eats lightly, and has controlled speech, body, and mind; ever devoted to meditation and yoga, and established in detachment.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अहङ्कारं बलं दर्पं कामं क्रोधं परिग्रहम् ।
विमुच्य निर्ममः शान्तो ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते ॥
translation_en: Free from ego, strength, arrogance, lust, anger, and possessiveness, and without a sense of “mine,” one becomes fit for the state of Brahman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ब्रह्मभूतः प्रसन्नात्मा न शोचति न काङ्क्षति ।
समः सर्वेषु भूतेषु मद्भक्तिं लभते पराम् ॥
translation_en: One who has become Brahman and is of serene self neither grieves nor desires; being equal to all beings, he attains supreme devotion to Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
भक्त्या मामभिजानाति यावान्यश्चास्मि तत्त्वतः ।
ततो मां तत्त्वतो ज्ञात्वा विशते तदनन्तरम् ॥
translation_en: By devotion he comes to know Me, as I am in truth; and then, knowing Me in truth, he enters into Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वकर्माण्यपि सदा कुर्वाणो मद्व्यपाश्रयः ।
मत्प्रसादादवाप्नोति शाश्वतं पदमव्ययम् ॥
translation_en: Always performing all actions, taking refuge in Me, he attains by My grace the eternal, imperishable state.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
चेतसा सर्वकर्माणि मयि संन्यस्य मत्परः ।
बुद्धियोगमुपाश्रित्य मच्चित्तः सततं भव ॥
translation_en: With your mind surrendering all actions to Me, intent on Me, take refuge in buddhi-yoga and always keep your mind fixed on Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मच्चित्तः सर्वदुर्गाणि मत्प्रसादात्तरिष्यसि ।
अथ चेत्त्वमहङ्कारान्न श्रोष्यसि विनङ्क्ष्यसि ॥
translation_en: With your mind set on Me, by My grace you will cross over all obstacles. But if, because of egoism, you do not listen, you will perish.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदहङ्कारमाश्रित्य न योत्स्य इति मन्यसे ।
मिथ्यैष व्यवसायस्ते प्रकृतिस्त्वां नियोक्ष्यति ॥
translation_en: If, relying on ego, you think, “I will not fight,” this resolve of yours is vain; prakriti will compel you.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
स्वभावजेन कौन्तेय निबद्धः स्वेन कर्मणा ।
कर्तुं नेच्छसि यन्मोहात्करिष्यस्यवशोऽपि तत् ॥
translation_en: O son of Kunti, bound by your own karma, born of your own nature, you do not wish to do that which, through delusion, you will nevertheless do, even against your will.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ईश्वरः सर्वभूतानां हृद्देशेऽर्जुन तिष्ठति ।
भ्रामयन्सर्वभूतानि यन्त्रारूढानि मायया ॥
translation_en: The Lord dwells in the heart-region of all beings, Arjuna, causing all beings to revolve, as if mounted on a machine, by means of maya.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तमेव शरणं गच्छ सर्वभावेन भारत ।
तत्प्रसादात्परां शान्तिं स्थानं प्राप्स्यसि शाश्वतम् ॥
translation_en: Take refuge in Him alone with all your being, O Bharata; by His grace you will attain supreme peace and the eternal abode.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इति ते ज्ञानमाख्यातं गुह्याद्गुह्यतरं मया ।
विमृश्यैतदशेषेण यथेच्छसि तथा कुरु ॥
translation_en:
Thus I have explained to you this knowledge, more secret than all secrets.  
Reflect on this fully, and then do as you wish.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वगुह्यतमं भूयः श‍ृणु मे परमं वचः ।
इष्टोऽसि मे दृढमिति ततो वक्ष्यामि ते हितम् ॥
translation_en: Listen again to My supreme, most secret words. You are dear to Me, and so I shall speak to you what is for your good.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु ।
मामेवैष्यसि सत्यं ते प्रतिजाने प्रियोऽसि मे ॥
translation_en:
Fix your mind on Me, be devoted to Me, sacrifice to Me, and bow down to Me.  
You will surely come to Me; I promise you truly, for you are dear to Me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज ।
अहं त्वा सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः ॥
translation_en: Abandon all dharmas and take refuge in Me alone. I will मुक्त you from all sins; do not grieve.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इदं ते नातपस्काय नाभक्ताय कदाचन ।
न चाशुश्रूषवे वाच्यं न च मां योऽभ्यसूयति ॥
translation_en: This is never to be spoken by you to one who is not disciplined, to one who is not devoted, nor to one who does not listen in service, nor to one who finds fault with me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
य इदं परमं गुह्यं मद्भक्तेष्वभिधास्यति ।
भक्तिं मयि परां कृत्वा मामेवैष्यत्यसंशयः ॥
translation_en: Whoever shall declare this supreme secret among My devotees, having shown supreme devotion to Me, will surely come to Me without doubt.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न च तस्मान्मनुष्येषु कश्चिन्मे प्रियकृत्तमः ।
भविता न च मे तस्मादन्यः प्रियतरो भुवि ॥
translation_en: And among all humans, there is none who does more for Me; nor will there ever be on earth anyone dearer to Me than he.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अध्येष्यते च य इमं धर्म्यं संवादमावयोः ।
ज्ञानयज्ञेन तेनाहमिष्टः स्यामिति मे मतिः ॥
translation_en: And whoever studies this righteous dialogue of ours, by the yajna of knowledge, to him I shall be worshiped in this way—such is my view.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
श्रद्धावाननसूयश्च श‍ृणुयादपि यो नरः ।
सोऽपि मुक्तः शुभाँल्लोकान्प्राप्नुयात्पुण्यकर्मणाम् ॥
translation_en:
Any man who hears this, with faith and without jealousy,  
he too, being freed, will attain the auspicious worlds of those who perform meritorious deeds.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कच्चिदेतच्छ्रुतं पार्थ त्वयैकाग्रेण चेतसा ।
कच्चिदज्ञानसम्मोहः प्रनष्टस्ते धनञ्जय ॥
translation_en:
O son of Pṛthā, have you heard this with an attentive mind?  
O Dhananjaya, has your delusion born of ignorance been dispelled?

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नष्टो मोहः स्मृतिर्लब्धा त्वत्प्रसादान्मयाच्युत ।
स्थितोऽस्मि गतसन्देहः करिष्ये वचनं तव ॥
translation_en: My delusion is destroyed, and my memory has returned by your grace, O Acyuta. I stand firm, free from doubt; I will do your word.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
इत्यहं वासुदेवस्य पार्थस्य च महात्मनः ।
संवादमिममश्रौषमद्भुतं रोमहर्षणम् ॥
translation_en: Thus I heard this wondrous, thrilling dialogue between Vāsudeva and the great-souled Pārtha.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
व्यासप्रसादाच्छ्रुतवानेतद्गुह्यमहं परम् ।
योगं योगेश्वरात्कृष्णात्साक्षात्कथयतः स्वयम् ॥
translation_en: By the grace of Vyāsa I have heard this supreme, most secret yoga directly from Kṛṣṇa, the lord of yoga, who spoke it himself.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
राजन्संस्मृत्य संस्मृत्य संवादमिममद्भुतम् ।
केशवार्जुनयोः पुण्यं हृष्यामि च मुहुर्मुहुः ॥
translation_en: O King, remembering again and again this wondrous conversation of Keshava and Arjuna, I rejoice again and again.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
तच्च संस्मृत्य संस्मृत्य रूपमत्यद्भुतं हरेः ।
विस्मयो मे महान् राजन्हृष्यामि च पुनः पुनः ॥
translation_en:
Remembering that wondrous form of Hari again and again,  
great is my amazement, O King, and I rejoice again and again.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
यत्र योगेश्वरः कृष्णो यत्र पार्थो धनुर्धरः ।
तत्र श्रीर्विजयो भूतिर्ध्रुवा नीतिर्मतिर्मम ॥
translation_en: Wherever Krishna, the lord of yoga, is, and wherever Partha, the archer, is, there are prosperity, victory, splendor, and steadfast policy; that is my conviction.
